75. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/util/config/env.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192package config\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192import (\n     4\u2192\t\"encoding/csv\"\n     5\u2192\t\"errors\"\n     6\u2192\t\"os\"\n     7\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n     8\u2192\t\"strings\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/kballard/go-shellquote\"\n    11\u2192\tlog \"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n    12\u2192)\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192var flags map[string]string\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192func init() {\n    17\u2192\terr := loadFlags()\n    18\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    19\u2192\t\tlog.Fatal(err)\n    20\u2192\t}\n    21\u2192}\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192func loadFlags() error {\n    24\u2192\tflags = make(map[string]string)\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192\topts, err := shellquote.Split(os.Getenv(\"ARGOCD_OPTS\"))\n    27\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    28\u2192\t\treturn err\n    29\u2192\t}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192\tvar key string\n    32\u2192\tfor _, opt := range opts {\n    33\u2192\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(opt, \"--\") {\n    34\u2192\t\t\tif key != \"\" {\n    35\u2192\t\t\t\tflags[key] = \"true\"\n    36\u2192\t\t\t}\n    37\u2192\t\t\tkey = strings.TrimPrefix(opt, \"--\")\n    38\u2192\t\t} else if key != \"\" {\n    39\u2192\t\t\tflags[key] = opt\n    40\u2192\t\t\tkey = \"\"\n    41\u2192\t\t} else {\n    42\u2192\t\t\treturn errors.New(\"ARGOCD_OPTS invalid at '\" + opt + \"'\")\n    43\u2192\t\t}\n    44\u2192\t}\n    45\u2192\tif key != \"\" {\n    46\u2192\t\tflags[key] = \"true\"\n    47\u2192\t}\n    48\u2192\t// pkg shellquota doesn't recognize `=` so that the opts in format `foo=bar` could not work.\n    49\u2192\t// issue ref: https://github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/issues/6822\n    50\u2192\tfor k, v := range flags {\n    51\u2192\t\tif strings.Contains(k, \"=\") && v == \"true\" {\n    52\u2192\t\t\tkv := strings.SplitN(k, \"=\", 2)\n    53\u2192\t\t\tactualKey, actualValue := kv[0], kv[1]\n    54\u2192\t\t\tif _, ok := flags[actualKey]; !ok {\n    55\u2192\t\t\t\tflags[actualKey] = actualValue\n    56\u2192\t\t\t}\n    57\u2192\t\t}\n    58\u2192\t}\n    59\u2192\treturn nil\n    60\u2192}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192func GetFlag(key, fallback string) string {\n    63\u2192\tval, ok := flags[key]\n    64\u2192\tif ok {\n    65\u2192\t\treturn val\n    66\u2192\t}\n    67\u2192\treturn fallback\n    68\u2192}\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192func GetBoolFlag(key string) bool {\n    71\u2192\treturn GetFlag(key, \"false\") == \"true\"\n    72\u2192}\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192func GetIntFlag(key string, fallback int) int {\n    75\u2192\tval, ok := flags[key]\n    76\u2192\tif !ok {\n    77\u2192\t\treturn fallback\n    78\u2192\t}\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192\tv, err := strconv.Atoi(val)\n    81\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    82\u2192\t\tlog.Fatal(err)\n    83\u2192\t}\n    84\u2192\treturn v\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192func GetStringSliceFlag(key string, fallback []string) []string {\n    88\u2192\tval, ok := flags[key]\n    89\u2192\tif !ok {\n    90\u2192\t\treturn fallback\n    91\u2192\t}\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192\tif val == \"\" {\n    94\u2192\t\treturn []string{}\n    95\u2192\t}\n    96\u2192\tstringReader := strings.NewReader(val)\n    97\u2192\tcsvReader := csv.NewReader(stringReader)\n    98\u2192\tv, err := csvReader.Read()\n    99\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   100\u2192\t\tlog.Fatal(err)\n   101\u2192\t}\n   102\u2192\treturn v\n   103\u2192}\n   104\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
76. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/util/env/env.go"
}
Output
"     1\u2192package env\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192import (\n     4\u2192\t\"math\"\n     5\u2192\t\"os\"\n     6\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n     7\u2192\t\"strings\"\n     8\u2192\t\"time\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\ttimeutil \"github.com/argoproj/pkg/time\"\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192\tlog \"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n    13\u2192)\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192// Helper function to parse a number from an environment variable. Returns a\n    16\u2192// default if env is not set, is not parseable to a number, exceeds max (if\n    17\u2192// max is greater than 0) or is less than min.\n    18\u2192//\n    19\u2192// nolint:unparam\n    20\u2192func ParseNumFromEnv(env string, defaultValue, min, max int) int {\n    21\u2192\tstr := os.Getenv(env)\n    22\u2192\tif str == \"\" {\n    23\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    24\u2192\t}\n    25\u2192\tnum, err := strconv.ParseInt(str, 10, 0)\n    26\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    27\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Could not parse '%s' as a number from environment %s\", str, env)\n    28\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    29\u2192\t}\n    30\u2192\tif num > math.MaxInt || num < math.MinInt {\n    31\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %d is outside of the min and max %d allowed values. Using default %d\", env, num, min, defaultValue)\n    32\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    33\u2192\t}\n    34\u2192\tif int(num) < min {\n    35\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %d, which is less than minimum %d allowed\", env, num, min)\n    36\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    37\u2192\t}\n    38\u2192\tif int(num) > max {\n    39\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %d, which is greater than maximum %d allowed\", env, num, max)\n    40\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    41\u2192\t}\n    42\u2192\treturn int(num)\n    43\u2192}\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192// Helper function to parse a int64 from an environment variable. Returns a\n    46\u2192// default if env is not set, is not parseable to a number, exceeds max (if\n    47\u2192// max is greater than 0) or is less than min.\n    48\u2192//\n    49\u2192// nolint:unparam\n    50\u2192func ParseInt64FromEnv(env string, defaultValue, min, max int64) int64 {\n    51\u2192\tstr := os.Getenv(env)\n    52\u2192\tif str == \"\" {\n    53\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    54\u2192\t}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192\tnum, err := strconv.ParseInt(str, 10, 64)\n    57\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    58\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Could not parse '%s' as a int64 from environment %s\", str, env)\n    59\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\tif num < min {\n    62\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %d, which is less than minimum %d allowed\", env, num, min)\n    63\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    64\u2192\t}\n    65\u2192\tif num > max {\n    66\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %d, which is greater than maximum %d allowed\", env, num, max)\n    67\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    68\u2192\t}\n    69\u2192\treturn num\n    70\u2192}\n    71\u2192\n    72\u2192// Helper function to parse a float32 from an environment variable. Returns a\n    73\u2192// default if env is not set, is not parseable to a number, exceeds max (if\n    74\u2192// max is greater than 0) or is less than min (and min is greater than 0).\n    75\u2192//\n    76\u2192// nolint:unparam\n    77\u2192func ParseFloatFromEnv(env string, defaultValue, min, max float32) float32 {\n    78\u2192\tstr := os.Getenv(env)\n    79\u2192\tif str == \"\" {\n    80\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    81\u2192\t}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192\tnum, err := strconv.ParseFloat(str, 32)\n    84\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    85\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Could not parse '%s' as a float32 from environment %s\", str, env)\n    86\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    87\u2192\t}\n    88\u2192\tif float32(num) < min {\n    89\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %f, which is less than minimum %f allowed\", env, num, min)\n    90\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    91\u2192\t}\n    92\u2192\tif float32(num) > max {\n    93\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %f, which is greater than maximum %f allowed\", env, num, max)\n    94\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n    95\u2192\t}\n    96\u2192\treturn float32(num)\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192// Helper function to parse a float64 from an environment variable. Returns a\n   100\u2192// default if env is not set, is not parseable to a number, exceeds max (if\n   101\u2192// max is greater than 0) or is less than min (and min is greater than 0).\n   102\u2192//\n   103\u2192// nolint:unparam\n   104\u2192func ParseFloat64FromEnv(env string, defaultValue, min, max float64) float64 {\n   105\u2192\tstr := os.Getenv(env)\n   106\u2192\tif str == \"\" {\n   107\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n   108\u2192\t}\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\tnum, err := strconv.ParseFloat(str, 64)\n   111\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   112\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Could not parse '%s' as a float32 from environment %s\", str, env)\n   113\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n   114\u2192\t}\n   115\u2192\tif num < min {\n   116\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %f, which is less than minimum %f allowed\", env, num, min)\n   117\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n   118\u2192\t}\n   119\u2192\tif num > max {\n   120\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %f, which is greater than maximum %f allowed\", env, num, max)\n   121\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n   122\u2192\t}\n   123\u2192\treturn num\n   124\u2192}\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192// Helper function to parse a time duration from an environment variable. Returns a\n   127\u2192// default if env is not set, is not parseable to a duration, exceeds max (if\n   128\u2192// max is greater than 0) or is less than min.\n   129\u2192//\n   130\u2192// nolinit:unparam\n   131\u2192func ParseDurationFromEnv(env string, defaultValue, min, max time.Duration) time.Duration {\n   132\u2192\tstr := os.Getenv(env)\n   133\u2192\tif str == \"\" {\n   134\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n   135\u2192\t}\n   136\u2192\tdurPtr, err := timeutil.ParseDuration(str)\n   137\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   138\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Could not parse '%s' as a duration string from environment %s\", str, env)\n   139\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n   140\u2192\t}\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192\tdur := *durPtr\n   143\u2192\tif dur < min {\n   144\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %s, which is less than minimum %s allowed\", env, dur, min)\n   145\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n   146\u2192\t}\n   147\u2192\tif dur > max {\n   148\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Value in %s is %s, which is greater than maximum %s allowed\", env, dur, max)\n   149\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n   150\u2192\t}\n   151\u2192\treturn dur\n   152\u2192}\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192type StringFromEnvOpts struct {\n   155\u2192\t// AllowEmpty allows the value to be empty as long as the environment variable is set.\n   156\u2192\tAllowEmpty bool\n   157\u2192}\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192func StringFromEnv(env string, defaultValue string, opts ...StringFromEnvOpts) string {\n   160\u2192\topt := StringFromEnvOpts{}\n   161\u2192\tfor _, o := range opts {\n   162\u2192\t\topt.AllowEmpty = opt.AllowEmpty || o.AllowEmpty\n   163\u2192\t}\n   164\u2192\tif str, ok := os.LookupEnv(env); opt.AllowEmpty && ok || str != \"\" {\n   165\u2192\t\treturn str\n   166\u2192\t}\n   167\u2192\treturn defaultValue\n   168\u2192}\n   169\u2192\n   170\u2192// StringsFromEnv parses given value from the environment as a list of strings,\n   171\u2192// using separator as the delimeter, and returns them as a slice. The strings\n   172\u2192// in the returned slice will have leading and trailing white space removed.\n   173\u2192func StringsFromEnv(env string, defaultValue []string, separator string) []string {\n   174\u2192\tif str := os.Getenv(env); str != \"\" {\n   175\u2192\t\tss := strings.Split(str, separator)\n   176\u2192\t\tfor i, s := range ss {\n   177\u2192\t\t\tss[i] = strings.TrimSpace(s)\n   178\u2192\t\t}\n   179\u2192\t\treturn ss\n   180\u2192\t}\n   181\u2192\treturn defaultValue\n   182\u2192}\n   183\u2192\n   184\u2192// ParseBoolFromEnv retrieves a boolean value from given environment envVar.\n   185\u2192// Returns default value if envVar is not set.\n   186\u2192//\n   187\u2192// nolinit:unparam\n   188\u2192func ParseBoolFromEnv(envVar string, defaultValue bool) bool {\n   189\u2192\tif val := os.Getenv(envVar); val != \"\" {\n   190\u2192\t\tif strings.ToLower(val) == \"true\" {\n   191\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n   192\u2192\t\t} else if strings.ToLower(val) == \"false\" {\n   193\u2192\t\t\treturn false\n   194\u2192\t\t}\n   195\u2192\t}\n   196\u2192\treturn defaultValue\n   197\u2192}\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192// ParseStringToStringVar parses given value from the environment as a map of string.\n   200\u2192// Returns default value if envVar is not set.\n   201\u2192func ParseStringToStringFromEnv(envVar string, defaultValue map[string]string, separator string) map[string]string {\n   202\u2192\tstr := os.Getenv(envVar)\n   203\u2192\tstr = strings.TrimSpace(str)\n   204\u2192\tif str == \"\" {\n   205\u2192\t\treturn defaultValue\n   206\u2192\t}\n   207\u2192\n   208\u2192\tparsed := make(map[string]string)\n   209\u2192\tfor _, pair := range strings.Split(str, separator) {\n   210\u2192\t\tkeyvalue := strings.Split(pair, \"=\")\n   211\u2192\t\tif len(keyvalue) != 2 {\n   212\u2192\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Invalid key-value pair when parsing environment '%s' as a string map\", str)\n   213\u2192\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n   214\u2192\t\t}\n   215\u2192\t\tkey := strings.TrimSpace(keyvalue[0])\n   216\u2192\t\tvalue := strings.TrimSpace(keyvalue[1])\n   217\u2192\t\tif _, ok := parsed[key]; ok {\n   218\u2192\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Duplicate key '%s' when parsing environment '%s' as a string map\", key, str)\n   219\u2192\t\t\treturn defaultValue\n   220\u2192\t\t}\n   221\u2192\t\tparsed[key] = value\n   222\u2192\t}\n   223\u2192\treturn parsed\n   224\u2192}\n   225\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
77. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/util/settings/settings.go",
  "limit": 200,
  "offset": 100
}
Output
"   100\u2192\tUiBannerPermanent bool `json:\"uiBannerPermanent,omitempty\"`\n   101\u2192\t// Position of UI Banner\n   102\u2192\tUiBannerPosition string `json:\"uiBannerPosition,omitempty\"`\n   103\u2192\t// PasswordPattern for password regular expression\n   104\u2192\tPasswordPattern string `json:\"passwordPattern,omitempty\"`\n   105\u2192\t// BinaryUrls contains the URLs for downloading argocd binaries\n   106\u2192\tBinaryUrls map[string]string `json:\"binaryUrls,omitempty\"`\n   107\u2192\t// InClusterEnabled indicates whether to allow in-cluster server address\n   108\u2192\tInClusterEnabled bool `json:\"inClusterEnabled\"`\n   109\u2192\t// ServerRBACLogEnforceEnable temporary var indicates whether rbac will be enforced on logs\n   110\u2192\tServerRBACLogEnforceEnable bool `json:\"serverRBACLogEnforceEnable\"`\n   111\u2192\t// MaxPodLogsToRender the maximum number of pod logs to render\n   112\u2192\tMaxPodLogsToRender int64 `json:\"maxPodLogsToRender\"`\n   113\u2192\t// ExecEnabled indicates whether the UI exec feature is enabled\n   114\u2192\tExecEnabled bool `json:\"execEnabled\"`\n   115\u2192\t// ExecShells restricts which shells are allowed for `exec` and in which order they are tried\n   116\u2192\tExecShells []string `json:\"execShells\"`\n   117\u2192\t// TrackingMethod defines the resource tracking method to be used\n   118\u2192\tTrackingMethod string `json:\"application.resourceTrackingMethod,omitempty\"`\n   119\u2192\t// OIDCTLSInsecureSkipVerify determines whether certificate verification is skipped when verifying tokens with the\n   120\u2192\t// configured OIDC provider (either external or the bundled Dex instance). Setting this to `true` will cause JWT\n   121\u2192\t// token verification to pass despite the OIDC provider having an invalid certificate. Only set to `true` if you\n   122\u2192\t// understand the risks.\n   123\u2192\tOIDCTLSInsecureSkipVerify bool `json:\"oidcTLSInsecureSkipVerify\"`\n   124\u2192\t// AppsInAnyNamespaceEnabled indicates whether applications are allowed to be created in any namespace\n   125\u2192\tAppsInAnyNamespaceEnabled bool `json:\"appsInAnyNamespaceEnabled\"`\n   126\u2192\t// ExtensionConfig configurations related to ArgoCD proxy extensions. The keys are the extension name.\n   127\u2192\t// The value is a yaml string defined in extension.ExtensionConfigs struct.\n   128\u2192\tExtensionConfig map[string]string `json:\"extensionConfig,omitempty\"`\n   129\u2192\t// ImpersonationEnabled indicates whether Application sync privileges can be decoupled from control plane\n   130\u2192\t// privileges using impersonation\n   131\u2192\tImpersonationEnabled bool `json:\"impersonationEnabled\"`\n   132\u2192}\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192type GoogleAnalytics struct {\n   135\u2192\tTrackingID     string `json:\"trackingID,omitempty\"`\n   136\u2192\tAnonymizeUsers bool   `json:\"anonymizeUsers,omitempty\"`\n   137\u2192}\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192type GlobalProjectSettings struct {\n   140\u2192\tProjectName   string               `json:\"projectName,omitempty\"`\n   141\u2192\tLabelSelector metav1.LabelSelector `json:\"labelSelector,omitempty\"`\n   142\u2192}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192// Help settings\n   145\u2192type Help struct {\n   146\u2192\t// the URL for getting chat help, this will typically be your Slack channel for support\n   147\u2192\tChatURL string `json:\"chatUrl,omitempty\"`\n   148\u2192\t// the text for getting chat help, defaults to \"Chat now!\"\n   149\u2192\tChatText string `json:\"chatText,omitempty\"`\n   150\u2192\t// the URLs for downloading argocd binaries\n   151\u2192\tBinaryURLs map[string]string `json:\"binaryUrl,omitempty\"`\n   152\u2192}\n   153\u2192\n   154\u2192// oidcConfig is the same as the public OIDCConfig, except the public one excludes the AllowedAudiences and the\n   155\u2192// SkipAudienceCheckWhenTokenHasNoAudience fields.\n   156\u2192// AllowedAudiences should be accessed via ArgoCDSettings.OAuth2AllowedAudiences.\n   157\u2192// SkipAudienceCheckWhenTokenHasNoAudience should be accessed via ArgoCDSettings.SkipAudienceCheckWhenTokenHasNoAudience.\n   158\u2192type oidcConfig struct {\n   159\u2192\tOIDCConfig\n   160\u2192\tAllowedAudiences                        []string `json:\"allowedAudiences,omitempty\"`\n   161\u2192\tSkipAudienceCheckWhenTokenHasNoAudience *bool    `json:\"skipAudienceCheckWhenTokenHasNoAudience,omitempty\"`\n   162\u2192}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192func (o *oidcConfig) toExported() *OIDCConfig {\n   165\u2192\tif o == nil {\n   166\u2192\t\treturn nil\n   167\u2192\t}\n   168\u2192\treturn &OIDCConfig{\n   169\u2192\t\tName:                     o.Name,\n   170\u2192\t\tIssuer:                   o.Issuer,\n   171\u2192\t\tClientID:                 o.ClientID,\n   172\u2192\t\tClientSecret:             o.ClientSecret,\n   173\u2192\t\tCLIClientID:              o.CLIClientID,\n   174\u2192\t\tUserInfoPath:             o.UserInfoPath,\n   175\u2192\t\tEnableUserInfoGroups:     o.EnableUserInfoGroups,\n   176\u2192\t\tUserInfoCacheExpiration:  o.UserInfoCacheExpiration,\n   177\u2192\t\tRequestedScopes:          o.RequestedScopes,\n   178\u2192\t\tRequestedIDTokenClaims:   o.RequestedIDTokenClaims,\n   179\u2192\t\tLogoutURL:                o.LogoutURL,\n   180\u2192\t\tRootCA:                   o.RootCA,\n   181\u2192\t\tEnablePKCEAuthentication: o.EnablePKCEAuthentication,\n   182\u2192\t\tDomainHint:               o.DomainHint,\n   183\u2192\t}\n   184\u2192}\n   185\u2192\n   186\u2192type OIDCConfig struct {\n   187\u2192\tName                     string                 `json:\"name,omitempty\"`\n   188\u2192\tIssuer                   string                 `json:\"issuer,omitempty\"`\n   189\u2192\tClientID                 string                 `json:\"clientID,omitempty\"`\n   190\u2192\tClientSecret             string                 `json:\"clientSecret,omitempty\"`\n   191\u2192\tCLIClientID              string                 `json:\"cliClientID,omitempty\"`\n   192\u2192\tEnableUserInfoGroups     bool                   `json:\"enableUserInfoGroups,omitempty\"`\n   193\u2192\tUserInfoPath             string                 `json:\"userInfoPath,omitempty\"`\n   194\u2192\tUserInfoCacheExpiration  string                 `json:\"userInfoCacheExpiration,omitempty\"`\n   195\u2192\tRequestedScopes          []string               `json:\"requestedScopes,omitempty\"`\n   196\u2192\tRequestedIDTokenClaims   map[string]*oidc.Claim `json:\"requestedIDTokenClaims,omitempty\"`\n   197\u2192\tLogoutURL                string                 `json:\"logoutURL,omitempty\"`\n   198\u2192\tRootCA                   string                 `json:\"rootCA,omitempty\"`\n   199\u2192\tEnablePKCEAuthentication bool                   `json:\"enablePKCEAuthentication,omitempty\"`\n   200\u2192\tDomainHint               string                 `json:\"domainHint,omitempty\"`\n   201\u2192}\n   202\u2192\n   203\u2192// DEPRECATED. Helm repository credentials are now managed using RepoCredentials\n   204\u2192type HelmRepoCredentials struct {\n   205\u2192\tURL            string                   `json:\"url,omitempty\"`\n   206\u2192\tName           string                   `json:\"name,omitempty\"`\n   207\u2192\tUsernameSecret *apiv1.SecretKeySelector `json:\"usernameSecret,omitempty\"`\n   208\u2192\tPasswordSecret *apiv1.SecretKeySelector `json:\"passwordSecret,omitempty\"`\n   209\u2192\tCertSecret     *apiv1.SecretKeySelector `json:\"certSecret,omitempty\"`\n   210\u2192\tKeySecret      *apiv1.SecretKeySelector `json:\"keySecret,omitempty\"`\n   211\u2192}\n   212\u2192\n   213\u2192// KustomizeVersion holds information about additional Kustomize version\n   214\u2192type KustomizeVersion struct {\n   215\u2192\t// Name holds Kustomize version name\n   216\u2192\tName string\n   217\u2192\t// Path holds corresponding binary path\n   218\u2192\tPath string\n   219\u2192\t// BuildOptions that are specific to Kustomize version\n   220\u2192\tBuildOptions string\n   221\u2192}\n   222\u2192\n   223\u2192// KustomizeSettings holds kustomize settings\n   224\u2192type KustomizeSettings struct {\n   225\u2192\tBuildOptions string\n   226\u2192\tVersions     []KustomizeVersion\n   227\u2192}\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192var (\n   230\u2192\tByClusterURLIndexer     = \"byClusterURL\"\n   231\u2192\tbyClusterURLIndexerFunc = func(obj interface{}) ([]string, error) {\n   232\u2192\t\ts, ok := obj.(*apiv1.Secret)\n   233\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   234\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   235\u2192\t\t}\n   236\u2192\t\tif s.Labels == nil || s.Labels[common.LabelKeySecretType] != common.LabelValueSecretTypeCluster {\n   237\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   238\u2192\t\t}\n   239\u2192\t\tif s.Data == nil {\n   240\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   241\u2192\t\t}\n   242\u2192\t\tif url, ok := s.Data[\"server\"]; ok {\n   243\u2192\t\t\treturn []string{strings.TrimRight(string(url), \"/\")}, nil\n   244\u2192\t\t}\n   245\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil\n   246\u2192\t}\n   247\u2192\tByClusterNameIndexer     = \"byClusterName\"\n   248\u2192\tbyClusterNameIndexerFunc = func(obj interface{}) ([]string, error) {\n   249\u2192\t\ts, ok := obj.(*apiv1.Secret)\n   250\u2192\t\tif !ok {\n   251\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   252\u2192\t\t}\n   253\u2192\t\tif s.Labels == nil || s.Labels[common.LabelKeySecretType] != common.LabelValueSecretTypeCluster {\n   254\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   255\u2192\t\t}\n   256\u2192\t\tif s.Data == nil {\n   257\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   258\u2192\t\t}\n   259\u2192\t\tif name, ok := s.Data[\"name\"]; ok {\n   260\u2192\t\t\treturn []string{string(name)}, nil\n   261\u2192\t\t}\n   262\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil\n   263\u2192\t}\n   264\u2192\tByProjectClusterIndexer = \"byProjectCluster\"\n   265\u2192\tByProjectRepoIndexer    = \"byProjectRepo\"\n   266\u2192\tbyProjectIndexerFunc    = func(secretType string) func(obj interface{}) ([]string, error) {\n   267\u2192\t\treturn func(obj interface{}) ([]string, error) {\n   268\u2192\t\t\ts, ok := obj.(*apiv1.Secret)\n   269\u2192\t\t\tif !ok {\n   270\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   271\u2192\t\t\t}\n   272\u2192\t\t\tif s.Labels == nil || s.Labels[common.LabelKeySecretType] != secretType {\n   273\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   274\u2192\t\t\t}\n   275\u2192\t\t\tif s.Data == nil {\n   276\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   277\u2192\t\t\t}\n   278\u2192\t\t\tif project, ok := s.Data[\"project\"]; ok {\n   279\u2192\t\t\t\treturn []string{string(project)}, nil\n   280\u2192\t\t\t}\n   281\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, nil\n   282\u2192\t\t}\n   283\u2192\t}\n   284\u2192)\n   285\u2192\n   286\u2192func (ks *KustomizeSettings) GetOptions(source v1alpha1.ApplicationSource) (*v1alpha1.KustomizeOptions, error) {\n   287\u2192\tbinaryPath := \"\"\n   288\u2192\tbuildOptions := \"\"\n   289\u2192\tif source.Kustomize != nil && source.Kustomize.Version != \"\" {\n   290\u2192\t\tfor _, ver := range ks.Versions {\n   291\u2192\t\t\tif ver.Name == source.Kustomize.Version {\n   292\u2192\t\t\t\t// add version specific path and build options\n   293\u2192\t\t\t\tbinaryPath = ver.Path\n   294\u2192\t\t\t\tbuildOptions = ver.BuildOptions\n   295\u2192\t\t\t\tbreak\n   296\u2192\t\t\t}\n   297\u2192\t\t}\n   298\u2192\t\tif binaryPath == \"\" {\n   299\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"kustomize version %s is not registered\", source.Kustomize.Version)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. If both\n/workspace/util/settings/settings.go:// GetGlobalProjectsSettings loads the global project settings from argocd-cm ConfigMap\n/workspace/util/settings/settings.go:\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error retrieving argocd-cm: %w\", err)\n/workspace/util/settings/settings.go:// IsImpersonationEnabled returns true if application sync with impersonation feature is enabled in argocd-cm configmap\n/workspace/util/settings/settings_test.go:\tdata, err := os.ReadFile(\"../../docs/operator-manual/argocd-cm.yaml\")\n/workspace/util/settings/settings_test.go:\tt.Run(\"Valid external TLS secret overrides argocd-secret\", func(t *testing.T) {\n/workspace/util/settings/settings_test.go:\t// When there is no argocd-cm itself,\n/workspace/util/settings/settings_test.go:\t\t\"with no argocd-cm config map, IsImpersonationEnabled() must return return false (default value)\")\n/workspace/util/settings/settings_test.go:\trequire.ErrorContains(t, err, \"configmap \\\"argocd-cm\\\" not found\",\n/workspace/util/settings/settings_test.go:\t\t\"with no argocd-cm config map, IsImpersonationEnabled() must return an error\")"

for accessing repositories\n   356\u2192type RepositoryCredentials struct {\n   357\u2192\t// The URL pattern the repository URL has to match\n   358\u2192\tURL string `json:\"url,omitempty\"`\n   359\u2192\t// Name of the secret storing the username used to access the repo\n   360\u2192\tUsernameSecret *apiv1.SecretKeySelector `json:\"usernameSecret,omitempty\"`\n   361\u2192\t// Name of the *apiv1.SecretKeySelector `json:\"githubAppPrivateKeySecret,omitempty\"`\n   371\u2192\t// Github App ID of the app used to access the repo\n   372\u2192\tGithubAppId int64 `json:\"githubAppID,omitempty\"`\n   373\u2192\t// Github App Installation ID of the installed GitHub App\n   374\u2192\tGithubAppInstallationId int64 `json:\"githubAppInstallationID,omitempty\"`\n   375\u2192\t// Github App Enterprise base url if empty will default to https://api.github.com\n   376\u2192\tGithubAppEnterpriseBaseURL string `json:\"githubAppEnterpriseBaseUrl,omitempty\"`\n   377\u2192\t// EnableOCI specifies whether helm-oci support should be enabled for this repo\n   378\u2192\tEnableOCI bool `json:\"enableOCI,omitempty\"`\n   379\u2192\t// the type of the repositoryCredentials, \"git\" or \"helm\", assumed to be \"git\" if empty or absent\n   380\u2192\tType string `json:\"type,omitempty\"`\n   381\u2192\t// GCPServiceAccountKey specifies the service account key in JSON format to be used for getting credentials to Google Cloud Source repos\n   382\u2192\tGCPServiceAccountKey *apiv1.SecretKeySelector `json:\"gcpServiceAccountKey,omitempty\"`\n   383\u2192\t// ForceHttpBasicAuth determines whether Argo CD should force use of basic auth for HTTP connected repositories\n   384\u2192\tForceHttpBasicAuth bool `json:\"forceHttpBasicAuth,omitempty\"`\n   385\u2192}\n   386\u2192\n   387\u2192// DeepLink structure\n   388\u2192type DeepLink struct {\n   389\u2192\t// URL that the deep link will redirect to\n   390\u2192\tURL string `json:\"url\"`\n   391\u2192\t// Title that will be displayed in the UI corresponding to that link\n   392\u2192\tTitle string `json:\"title\"`\n   393\u2192\t// Description (optional) a description for what the deep link is about\n   394\u2192\tDescription *string `json:\"description,omitempty\"`\n   395\u2192\t// IconClass (optional) a font-awesome icon class to be used when displaying the links in dropdown menus.\n   396\u2192\tIconClass *string `json:\"icon.class,omitempty\"`\n   397\u2192\t// Condition (optional) a conditional statement depending on which the deep link shall be rendered\n   398\u2192\tCondition *string `json:\"if,omitempty\"`\n   399\u2192}\n   400\u2192\n   401\u2192const (\n   402\u2192\t// settingServerSignatureKey designates the key for a server secret key inside a Kubernetes secret.\n   403\u2192\tsettingServerSignatureKey = \"server.secretkey\"\n   404\u2192\t// gaTrackingID holds Google Analytics tracking id\n   405\u2192\tgaTrackingID = \"ga.trackingid\"\n   406\u2192\t// the URL for getting chat help, this will typically be your Slack channel for support\n   407\u2192\thelpChatURL = \"help.chatUrl\"\n   408\u2192\t// the text for getting chat help, defaults to \"Chat now!\"\n   409\u2192\thelpChatText = \"help.chatText\"\n   410\u2192\t// gaAnonymizeUsers specifies if user ids should be anonymized (hashed) before sending to Google Analytics. True unless value is set to 'false'\n   411\u2192\tgaAnonymizeUsers = \"ga.anonymizeusers\"\n   412\u2192\t// settingServerCertificate designates the key for the public cert used in TLS\n   413\u2192\tsettingServerCertificate = \"tls.crt\"\n   414\u2192\t// settingServerPrivateKey designates the key for the private key used in TLS\n   415\u2192\tsettingServerPrivateKey = \"tls.key\"\n   416\u2192\t// settingURLKey designates the key where Argo CD's external URL is set\n   417\u2192\tsettingURLKey = \"url\"\n   418\u2192\t// settingAdditionalUrlsKey designates the key where Argo CD's additional external URLs are set\n   419\u2192\tsettingAdditionalUrlsKey = \"additionalUrls\"\n   420\u2192\t// repositoriesKey designates the key where ArgoCDs repositories list is set\n   421\u2192\trepositoriesKey = \"repositories\"\n   422\u2192\t// repositoryCredentialsKey designates the key where ArgoCDs repositories credentials list is set\n   423\u2192\trepositoryCredentialsKey = \"repository.credentials\"\n   424\u2192\t// helmRepositoriesKey designates the key where list of helm repositories is set\n   425\u2192\thelmRepositoriesKey = \"helm.repositories\"\n   426\u2192\t// settingDexConfigKey designates the key for the dex config\n   427\u2192\tsettingDexConfigKey = \"dex.config\"\n   428\u2192\t// settingsOIDCConfigKey designates the key for OIDC config\n   429\u2192\tsettingsOIDCConfigKey = \"oidc.config\"\n   430\u2192\t// statusBadgeEnabledKey holds the key which enables of disables status badge feature\n   431\u2192\tstatusBadgeEnabledKey = \"statusbadge.enabled\"\n   432\u2192\t// statusBadgeRootUrlKey holds the key for the root badge URL override\n   433\u2192\tstatusBadgeRootUrlKey = \"statusbadge.url\"\n   434\u2192\t// settingsWebhookGitHubSecret is the key for the GitHub shared webhook secret\n   435\u2192\tsettingsWebhookGitHubSecretKey = \"webhook.github.secret\"\n   436\u2192\t// settingsWebhookGitLabSecret is the key for the GitLab shared webhook secret\n   437\u2192\tsettingsWebhookGitLabSecretKey = \"webhook.gitlab.secret\"\n   438\u2192\t// settingsWebhookBitbucketUUID is the key for Bitbucket webhook UUID\n   439\u2192\tsettingsWebhookBitbucketUUIDKey = \"webhook.bitbucket.uuid\"\n   440\u2192\t// settingsWebhookBitbucketServerSecret is the key for BitbucketServer webhook secret\n   441\u2192\tsettingsWebhookBitbucketServerSecretKey = \"webhook.bitbucketserver.secret\"\n   442\u2192\t// settingsWebhookGogsSecret is the key for Gogs webhook secret\n   443\u2192\tsettingsWebhookGogsSecretKey = \"webhook.gogs.secret\"\n   444\u2192\t// settingsWebhookAzureDevOpsUsernameKey is the key for Azure DevOps webhook username\n   445\u2192\tsettingsWebhookAzureDevOpsUsernameKey = \"webhook.azuredevops.username\"\n   446\u2192\t// settingsWebhookAzureDevOpsPasswordKey is the key for Azure DevOps webhook password\n   447\u2192\tsettingsWebhookAzureDevOpsPasswordKey = \"webhook.azuredevops.password\"\n   448\u2192\t// settingsWebhookMaxPayloadSize is the key for the maximum payload size for webhooks in MB\n   449\u2192\tsettingsWebhookMaxPayloadSizeMB = \"webhook.maxPayloadSizeMB\"\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "   600\u2192\t\tgo mgr.reposOrClusterChanged()\n   601\u2192\t}\n   602\u2192}\n   603\u2192\n   604\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) RespectRBAC() (int, error) {\n   605\u2192\tcm, err := mgr.getConfigMap()\n   606\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   607\u2192\t\treturn enginecache.RespectRbacDisabled, err\n   608\u2192\t}\n   609\u2192\tif cm.Data[RespectRBAC] != \"\" {\n   610\u2192\t\tswitch cm.Data[RespectRBAC] {\n   611\u2192\t\tcase RespectRBACValueNormal:\n   612\u2192\t\t\treturn enginecache.RespectRbacNormal, nil\n   613\u2192\t\tcase RespectRBACValueStrict:\n   614\u2192\t\t\treturn enginecache.RespectRbacStrict, nil\n   615\u2192\t\tdefault:\n   616\u2192\t\t\treturn enginecache.RespectRbacDisabled, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid value for %s: %s\", RespectRBAC, 627\u2192\treturn mgr.secrets, nil\n   628\u2192}\n   629\u2192\n   630\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) GetSecretsInformer() (cache.SharedIndexInformer, error) {\n   631\u2192\terr := mgr.ensureSynced(false)\n   632\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   633\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error ensuring that the secrets manager is synced: %w\", err)\n   634\u2192\t}\n   635\u2192\treturn mgr.secretsInformer, nil\n   636\u2192}\n   637\u2192\n   638\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) updateSecret(callback func(*apiv1.Secret) error) error {\n   639\u2192\terr := mgr.ensureSynced(false)\n 834\u2192\targoCDCM, err := mgr.getConfigMap()\n   835\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   836\u2192\t\treturn 10, err\n   837\u2192\t}\n   838\u2192\n   839\u2192\tif argoCDCM.Data[settingsMaxPodLogsToRender] 846\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) GetDeepLinks(deeplinkType string) ([]DeepLink, error) {\n   847\u2192\targoCDCM, err := mgr.getConfigMap()\n   848\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   849\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error retrieving argocd-cm: %w\", err)\n   850\u2192\t}\n   851\u2192\tdeepLinks := make([]DeepLink, 0)\n   852\u2192\tif value, ok := argoCDCM.Data[deeplinkType]; ok {\n   853\u2192\t\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(value), &deepLinks)\n   854\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   855\u2192\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error unmarshalling deep links %w\", err)\n   856\u2192\t\t}\n   857\u2192\t}\n   858\u2192\treturn deepLinks, nil\n   859\u2192}\n   860\u2192\n   861\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) GetEnabledSourceTypes() (map[string]bool, error) {\n   862\u2192\targoCDCM, err := mgr.getConfigMap()\n   863\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   864\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get argo-cd config map: %w\", err)\n   865\u2192\t}\n   866\u2192\tres := map[string]bool{}\n   867\u2192\tfor sourceType := range sourceTypeToEnableGenerationKey {\n   868\u2192\t\tres[string(sourceType)] = true\n   869\u2192\t}\n   870\u2192\tfor sourceType, key := range sourceTypeToEnableGenerationKey {\n   871\u2192\t\tif val, ok := argoCDCM.Data[key]; ok && val != \"\" {\n   872\u2192\t\t\tres[string(sourceType)] = val == \"true\"\n   873\u2192\t\t}\n   874\u2192\t}\n   875\u2192\t// plugin based manifest generation cannot be disabled\n   876\u2192\tres[string(v1alpha1.ApplicationSourceTypePlugin)] = true\n   877\u2192\treturn res, nil\n   878\u2192}\n   879\u2192\n   880\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) GetIgnoreResourceUpdatesOverrides() (map[string]v1alpha1.ResourceOverride, error) {\n   881\u2192\tcompareOptions, err := mgr.GetResourceCompareOptions()\n   882\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   883\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get compare options: %w\", err)\n   884\u2192\t}\n   885\u2192\n   886\u2192\tresourceOverrides, err := mgr.GetResourceOverrides()\n   887\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   888\u2192\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get resource overrides: %w\", err)\n   889\u2192\t}\n   890\u2192\n   891\u2192\tfor k, v := range resourceOverrides {\n   892\u2192\t\tresourceUpdates := v.IgnoreResourceUpdates\n   893\u2192\t\tif compareOptions.IgnoreDifferencesOnResourceUpdates {\n   894\u2192\t\t\tresourceUpdates.JQPathExpressions = append(resourceUpdates.JQPathExpressions, v.IgnoreDifferences.JQPathExpressions...)\n   895\u2192\t\t\tresourceUpdates.JSONPointers = append(resourceUpdates.JSONPointers, v.IgnoreDifferences.JSONPointers...)\n   896\u2192\t\t\tresourceUpdates.ManagedFieldsManagers = append(resourceUpdates.ManagedFieldsManagers, v.IgnoreDifferences.ManagedFieldsManagers...)\n   897\u2192\t\t}\n   898\u2192\t\t// Set the IgnoreDifferences because these are the overrides used by Normalizers\n   899\u2192\t\tv.IgnoreDifferences = resourceUpdates\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "   450\u2192\t// settingsApplicationInstanceLabelKey is the key to configure injected app instance label key\n   451\u2192\tsettingsApplicationInstanceLabelKey = \"application.instanceLabelKey\"\n   452\u2192\t// settingsResourceTrackingMethodKey is the key to configure tracking method for application resources\n   453\u2192\tsettingsResourceTrackingMethodKey = \"application.resourceTrackingMethod\"\n   454\u2192\t// settingsInstallationID holds the key for the instance installation ID\n   455\u2192\tsettingsInstallationID = \"installationID\"\n   456\u2192\t// resourcesCustomizationsKey is the key to the map of resource overrides\n   457\u2192\tresourceCustomizationsKey = \"resource.customizations\"\n   458\u2192\t// resourceExclusions is the key to the list of excluded resources\n   459\u2192\tresourceExclusionsKey = \"resource.exclusions\"\n   460\u2192\t// resourceInclusions is the key to the list of explicitly watched resources\n   461\u2192\tresourceInclusionsKey = \"resource.inclusions\"\n   462\u2192\t// resourceIgnoreResourceUpdatesEnabledKey is the key to a boolean determining whether the resourceIgnoreUpdates feature is enabled\n   463\u2192\tresourceIgnoreResourceUpdatesEnabledKey = \"resource.ignoreResourceUpdatesEnabled\"\n   464\u2192\t// resourceCustomLabelKey is the key to a custom label to show in node info, if present\n   465\u2192\tresourceCustomLabelsKey = \"resource.customLabels\"\n   466\u2192\t// resourceIncludeEventLabelKeys is the key to labels to be added onto Application k8s events if present on an Application or it's AppProject. Supports wildcard.\n   467\u2192\tresourceIncludeEventLabelKeys = \"resource.includeEventLabelKeys\"\n   468\u2192\t// resourceExcludeEventLabelKeys is the key to labels to be excluded from adding onto Application's k8s events. Supports wildcard.\n   469\u2192\tresourceExcludeEventLabelKeys = \"resource.excludeEventLabelKeys\"\n   470\u2192\t// kustomizeBuildOptionsKey is a string of kustomize build parameters\n   471\u2192\tkustomizeBuildOptionsKey = \"kustomize.buildOptions\"\n   472\u2192\t// kustomizeVersionKeyPrefix is a kustomize version key prefix\n   473\u2192\tkustomizeVersionKeyPrefix = \"kustomize.version\"\n   474\u2192\t// kustomizePathPrefixKey is a kustomize path for a specific version\n   475\u2192\tkustomizePathPrefixKey = \"kustomize.path\"\n   476\u2192\t// anonymousUserEnabledKey is the key which enables or disables anonymous user\n   477\u2192\tanonymousUserEnabledKey = \"users.anonymous.enabled\"\n   478\u2192\t// userSessionDurationKey is the key which specifies token expiration duration\n   479\u2192\tuserSessionDurationKey = \"users.session.duration\"\n   480\u2192\t// diffOptions is the key where diff options are configured\n   481\u2192\tresourceCompareOptionsKey = \"resource.compareoptions\"\n   482\u2192\t// settingUiCssURLKey designates the key for user-defined CSS URL for UI customization\n   483\u2192\tsettingUiCssURLKey = \"ui.cssurl\"\n   484\u2192\t// settingUiBannerContentKey designates the key for content of user-defined info banner for UI\n   485\u2192\tsettingUiBannerContentKey = \"ui.bannercontent\"\n   486\u2192\t// settingUiBannerURLKey designates the key for the link for user-defined info banner for UI\n   487\u2192\tsettingUiBannerURLKey = \"ui.bannerurl\"\n   488\u2192\t// settingUiBannerPermanentKey designates the key for whether the banner is permanent and not closeable\n   489\u2192\tsettingUiBannerPermanentKey = \"ui.bannerpermanent\"\n   490\u2192\t// settingUiBannerPositionKey designates the key for the position of the banner\n   491\u2192\tsettingUiBannerPositionKey = \"ui.bannerposition\"\n   492\u2192\t// settingsBinaryUrlsKey designates the key for the argocd binary URLs\n   493\u2192\tsettingsBinaryUrlsKey = \"help.download\"\n   494\u2192\t// globalProjectsKey designates the key for global project settings\n   495\u2192\tglobalProjectsKey = \"globalProjects\"\n   496\u2192\t// initialPasswordSecretName is the name of the secret that will hold the initial admin password\n   497\u2192\tinitialPasswordSecretName = \"argocd-initial-admin-secret\"\n   498\u2192\t// initialPasswordSecretField is the name of the field in initialPasswordSecretName to store the password\n   499\u2192\tinitialPasswordSecretField = \"password\"\n   500\u2192\t// initialPasswordLength defines the length of the generated initial password\n   501\u2192\tinitialPasswordLength = 16\n   502\u2192\t// externalServerTLSSecretName defines the name of the external secret holding the server's TLS certificate\n   503\u2192\texternalServerTLSSecretName = \"argocd-server-tls\"\n   504\u2192\t// partOfArgoCDSelector holds label selector that should be applied to config maps and secrets used to manage Argo CD\n   505\u2192\tpartOfArgoCDSelector = \"app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argocd\"\n   506\u2192\t// settingsPasswordPatternKey is the key to configure user password regular expression\n   507\u2192\tsettingsPasswordPatternKey = \"passwordPattern\"\n   508\u2192\t// inClusterEnabledKey is the key to configure whether to allow in-cluster server address\n   509\u2192\tinClusterEnabledKey = \"cluster.inClusterEnabled\"\n   510\u2192\t// settingsServerRBACLogEnforceEnable is the key to configure whether logs RBAC enforcement is enabled\n   511\u2192\tsettingsServerRBACLogEnforceEnableKey = \"server.rbac.log.enforce.enable\"\n   512\u2192\t// MaxPodLogsToRender the maximum number of pod logs to render\n   513\u2192\tsettingsMaxPodLogsToRender = \"server.maxPodLogsToRender\"\n   514\u2192\t// helmValuesFileSchemesKey is the key to configure the list of supported helm values file schemas\n   515\u2192\thelmValuesFileSchemesKey = \"helm.valuesFileSchemes\"\n   516\u2192\t// execEnabledKey is the key to configure whether the UI exec feature is enabled\n   517\u2192\texecEnabledKey = \"exec.enabled\"\n   518\u2192\t// execShellsKey is the key to configure which shells are allowed for `exec` and in what order they are tried\n   519\u2192\texecShellsKey = \"exec.shells\"\n   520\u2192\t// oidcTLSInsecureSkipVerifyKey is the key to configure whether TLS cert verification is skipped for OIDC connections\n   521\u2192\toidcTLSInsecureSkipVerifyKey = \"oidc.tls.insecure.skip.verify\"\n   522\u2192\t// ApplicationDeepLinks is the application deep link key\n   523\u2192\tApplicationDeepLinks = \"application.links\"\n   524\u2192\t// ProjectDeepLinks is the project deep link key\n   525\u2192\tProjectDeepLinks = \"project.links\"\n   526\u2192\t// ResourceDeepLinks is the resource deep link key\n   527\u2192\tResourceDeepLinks = \"resource.links\"\n   528\u2192\textensionConfig   = \"extension.config\"\n   529\u2192\t// RespectRBAC is the key to configure argocd to respect rbac while watching for resources\n   530\u2192\tRespectRBAC            = \"resource.respectRBAC\"\n   531\u2192\tRespectRBACValueStrict = \"strict\"\n   532\u2192\tRespectRBACValueNormal = \"normal\"\n   533\u2192\t// impersonationEnabledKey is the key to configure whether the application sync decoupling through impersonation feature is enabled\n   534\u2192\timpersonationEnabledKey = \"application.sync.impersonation.enabled\"\n   535\u2192)\n   536\u2192\n   537\u2192const (\n   538\u2192\t// default max webhook payload size is 1GB\n   539\u2192\tdefaultMaxWebhookPayloadSize = int64(1) * 1024 * 1024 * 1024\n   540\u2192\n   541\u2192\t// application sync with impersonation feature is disabled by default.\n   542\u2192\tdefaultImpersonationEnabledFlag = false\n   543\u2192)\n   544\u2192\n   545\u2192var sourceTypeToEnableGenerationKey = map[v1alpha1.ApplicationSourceType]string{\n   546\u2192\tv1alpha1.ApplicationSourceTypeKustomize: \"kustomize.enable\",\n   547\u2192\tv1alpha1.ApplicationSourceTypeHelm:      \"helm.enable\",\n   548\u2192\tv1alpha1.ApplicationSourceTypeDirectory: \"jsonnet.enable\",\n   549\u2192}\n   550\u2192\n   551\u2192// SettingsManager holds config info for a new manager with which to access Kubernetes ConfigMaps.\n   552\u2192type SettingsManager struct {\n   553\u2192\tctx             context.Context\n   554\u2192\tclientset       kubernetes.Interface\n   555\u2192\tsecrets         v1listers.SecretLister\n   556\u2192\tsecretsInformer cache.SharedIndexInformer\n   557\u2192\tconfigmaps      v1listers.ConfigMapLister\n   558\u2192\tnamespace       string\n   559\u2192\t// subscribers is a list of subscribers to settings updates\n   560\u2192\tsubscribers []chan<- *ArgoCDSettings\n   561\u2192\t// mutex protects concurrency sensitive parts of settings manager: access to subscribers list and initialization flag\n   562\u2192\tmutex                 *sync.Mutex\n   563\u2192\tinitContextCancel     func()\n   564\u2192\treposCache            []Repository\n   565\u2192\trepoCredsCache        []RepositoryCredentials\n   566\u2192\treposOrClusterChanged func()\n   567\u2192}\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192type incompleteSettingsError struct {\n   570\u2192\tmessage string\n   571\u2192}\n   572\u2192\n   573\u2192type IgnoreStatus string\n   574\u2192\n   575\u2192const (\n   576\u2192\t// IgnoreResourceStatusInCRD ignores status changes for all CRDs\n   577\u2192\tIgnoreResourceStatusInCRD IgnoreStatus = \"crd\"\n   578\u2192\t// IgnoreResourceStatusInAll ignores status changes for all resources\n   579\u2192\tIgnoreResourceStatusInAll IgnoreStatus = \"all\"\n   580\u2192\t// IgnoreResourceStatusInNone ignores status changes for no resources\n   581\u2192\tIgnoreResourceStatusInNone IgnoreStatus = \"off\"\n   582\u2192)\n 489\u2192\tsettingUiBannerPermanentKey = \"ui.bannerpermanent\"\n   490\u2192\t// settingUiBannerPositionKey designates the key for the position of the banner\n   491\u2192\tsettingUiBannerPositionKey = \"ui.bannerposition\"\n   492\u2192\t// settingsBinaryUrlsKey designates the key for the argocd binary URLs\n   493\u2192\tsettingsBinaryUrlsKey = \"help.download\"\n   494\u2192\t// globalProjectsKey designates the key for global project settings\n   495\u2192\tglobalProjectsKey = \"globalProjects\"\n   496\u2192\t// initialPasswordSecretName is the name of the secret that will hold the initial admin password\n   497\u2192\tinitialPasswordSecretName = \"argocd-initial-admin-secret\"\n   498\u2192\t// initialPasswordSecretField is the name of the field in initialPasswordSecretName to store the password\n   499\u2192\tinitialPasswordSecretField = \"password\"\n   500\u2192\t// initialPasswordLength defines the length of the generated initial password\n   501\u2192\tinitialPasswordLength = 16\n   502\u2192\t// externalServerTLSSecretName defines the name of the external secret holding the server's TLS certificate\n   503\u2192\texternalServerTLSSecretName = \"argocd-server-tls\"\n   504\u2192\t// partOfArgoCDSelector holds label selector that should be applied to config maps and secrets used to manage Argo CD\n   505\u2192\tpartOfArgoCDSelector = \"app.kubernetes.io/part-of=argocd\"\n   506\u2192\t// settingsPasswordPatternKey is the key to configure user password regular expression\n   507\u2192\tsettingsPasswordPatternKey = \"passwordPattern\"\n   508\u2192\t// inClusterEnabledKey is the key to configure whether to allow in-cluster server address\n   509\u2192\tinClusterEnabledKey = \"cluster.inClusterEnabled\"\n   510\u2192\t// settingsServerRBACLogEnforceEnable is the key to configure whether logs RBAC enforcement is enabled\n   511\u2192\tsettingsServerRBACLogEnforceEnableKey = \"server.rbac.log.enforce.enable\"\n   512\u2192\t// MaxPodLogsToRender the maximum number of pod logs to render\n   513\u2192\tsettingsMaxPodLogsToRender = \"server.maxPodLogsToRender\"\n   514\u2192\t// helmValuesFileSchemesKey is the key to configure the list of supported helm values file schemas\n   515\u2192\thelmValuesFileSchemesKey = \"helm.valuesFileSchemes\"\n   516\u2192\t// execEnabledKey is the key to configure whether the UI exec feature is enabled\n   517\u2192\texecEnabledKey = \"exec.enabled\"\n   518\u2192\t// execShellsKey is the key to configure which shells are allowed for `exec` and in what order they are tried\n   519\u2192\texecShellsKey = \"exec.shells\"\n   520\u2192\t// oidcTLSInsecureSkipVerifyKey is the key to configure whether TLS cert verification is skipped for OIDC connections\n   521\u2192\toidcTLSInsecureSkipVerifyKey = \"oidc.tls.insecure.skip.verify\"\n   522\u2192\t// ApplicationDeepLinks is the application deep link key\n   523\u2192\tApplicationDeepLinks = \"application.links\"\n   524\u2192\t// ProjectDeepLinks is the project deep link key\n   525\u2192\tProjectDeepLinks = \"project.links\"\n   526\u2192\t// ResourceDeepLinks is the resource deep link key\n   527\u2192\tResourceDeepLinks = \"resource.links\"\n   528\u2192\textensionConfig   = \"extension.config\"\n   529\u2192\t// RespectRBAC is the key to configure argocd to respect rbac while watching for resources\n   530\u2192\tRespectRBAC            = \"resource.respectRBAC\"\n   531\u2192\tRespectRBACValueStrict = \"strict\"\n   532\u2192\tRespectRBACValueNormal = \"normal\"\n   533\u2192\t// impersonationEnabledKey is the key to configure whether the application sync decoupling through impersonation feature is enabled\n   534\u2192\timpersonationEnabledKey = \"application.sync.impersonation.enabled\"\n   535\u2192)\n   536\u2192\n   537\u2192const (\n   538\u2192\t// default max webhook payload size is 1GB\n   539\u2192\tdefaultMaxWebhookPayloadSize = int64(1) * 1024 * 1024 * 1024\n   540\u2192\n   541\u2192\t// application sync with impersonation feature is disabled by default.\n   542\u2192\tdefaultImpersonationEnabledFlag = false\n   543\u2192)\n   544\u2192\n   545\u2192var sourceTypeToEnableGenerationKey = map[v1alpha1.ApplicationSourceType]string{\n   546\u2192\tv1alpha1.ApplicationSourceTypeKustomize: \"kustomize.enable\",\n   547\u2192\tv1alpha1.ApplicationSourceTypeHelm:      \"helm.enable\",\n   548\u2192\tv1alpha1.ApplicationSourceTypeDirectory: \"jsonnet.enable\",\n   549\u2192}\n   550\u2192\n   551\u2192// SettingsManager holds config info for a new manager with which to access Kubernetes ConfigMaps.\n   552\u2192type SettingsManager struct {\n   553\u2192\tctx             context.Context\n   554\u2192\tclientset       kubernetes.Interface\n   555\u2192\tsecrets         v1listers.SecretLister\n   556\u2192\tsecretsInformer cache.SharedIndexInformer\n   557\u2192\tconfigmaps 561\u2192\t// mutex protects concurrency sensitive parts of settings manager: access to subscribers list and initialization flag\n   562\u2192\tmutex                 *sync.Mutex\n   563\u2192\tinitContextCancel     func()\n   564\u2192\treposCache            []Repository\n   565\u2192\trepoCredsCache        []RepositoryCredentials\n   566\u2192\treposOrClusterChanged func()\n   567\u2192}\n   568\u2192\n   569\u2192type incompleteSettingsError struct {\n   570\u2192\tmessage string\n   571\u2192}\n   572\u2192\n   573\u2192type IgnoreStatus string\n   574\u2192\n   575\u2192const (\n   576\u2192\t// IgnoreResourceStatusInCRD ignores status changes for all CRDs\n   577\u2192\tIgnoreResourceStatusInCRD IgnoreStatus = \"crd\"\n   578\u2192\t// IgnoreResourceStatusInAll ignores status changes for all resources\n   579\u2192\tIgnoreResourceStatusInAll IgnoreStatus = \"all\"\n   580\u2192\t// IgnoreResourceStatusInNone ignores status changes for no resources\n   581\u2192\tIgnoreResourceStatusInNone IgnoreStatus = \"off\"\n   582\u2192)\n   583\u2192\n   584\u2192type ArgoCDDiffOptions struct {\n   585\u2192\tIgnoreAggregatedRoles bool `json:\"ignoreAggregatedRoles,omitempty\"`\n   586\u2192\n   587\u2192\t// If set to true then differences caused by status are ignored.\n   588\u2192\tIgnoreResourceStatusField IgnoreStatus `json:\"ignoreResourceStatusField,omitempty\"`\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192\t// If set to true then ignoreDifferences are applied to ignore application refresh on resource updates.\n   591\u2192\tIgnoreDifferencesOnResourceUpdates bool `json:\"ignoreDifferencesOnResourceUpdates,omitempty\"`\n   592\u2192}\n   593\u2192\n   594\u2192func (e *incompleteSettingsError) Error() string {\n   595\u2192\treturn e.message\n   596\u2192}\n   597\u2192\n   598\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) onRepoOrClusterChanged() {\n   599\u2192\tif mgr.reposOrClusterChanged != nil {\n   600\u2192\t\tgo mgr.reposOrClusterChanged()\n   601\u2192\t}\n   602\u2192}\n   603\u2192\n   604\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) RespectRBAC() (int, error) {\n   605\u2192\tcm, err := mgr.getConfigMap()\n   606\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   607\u2192\t\treturn enginecache.RespectRbacDisabled, err\n   608\u2192\t}\n   609\u2192\tif cm.Data[RespectRBAC] != \"\" {\n   610\u2192\t\tswitch cm.Data[RespectRBAC] {\n   611\u2192\t\tcase RespectRBACValueNormal:\n   612\u2192\t\t\treturn enginecache.RespectRbacNormal, nil\n   613\u2192\t\tcase RespectRBACValueStrict:\n cm.Data[RespectRBAC])\n   617\u2192\t\t}\n   618\u2192\t}\n   619\u2192\treturn enginecache.RespectRbacDisabled, nil\n   620\u2192}\n   621\u2192\n   622\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) GetSecretsLister() (v1listers.SecretLister, error) {\n   623\u2192\terr := mgr.ensureSynced(false)\n   624\u2192\tif err != nil {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider != nil {\n  1055\u2192\t\t\t\treturn err\n  1056\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1057\u2192\t\t\toverrideVal.KnownTypeFields = knownTypeFields\n  1058\u2192\t\tdefault:\n  1059\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"resource customization type %s not supported\", customizationType)\n  1060\u2192\t\t}\n  1061\u2192\t\tresourceOverrides[overrideKey] = overrideVal\n  1062\u2192\t}\n  1063\u2192\treturn nil\n  1064\u2192}\n  1065\u2192\n  1066\u2192// Convert group-kind format to <group/kind>, allowed key format examples\n  1067\u2192// resource.customizations.health.cert-manager.io_Certificate\n  1068\u2192// resource.customizations.health.Certificate\n  1069\u2192func convertToOverrideKey(groupKind string) (string, error) {\n  1070\u2192\tparts := strings.Split(groupKind, \"_\")\n  1071\u2192\tif len(parts) == 2 {\n  1072\u2192\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s\", parts[0], parts[1]), nil\n  1073\u2192\t} else if len(parts) == 1 && groupKind != \"\" {\n  1074\u2192\t\treturn groupKind, nil\n  1075\u2192\t}\n  1076\u2192\treturn \"\", fmt.Errorf(\"group kind should be in format `resource.customizations.<type>.<group_kind>` or resource.customizations.<type>.<kind>`, got group kind: '%s'\", 1087\u2192\n  1088\u2192\targoCDCM, err := mgr.getConfigMap()\n  1089\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1090\u2192\t\treturn diffOptions, err\n  1091\u2192\t}\n  1092\u2192\n  1093\u2192\tif value, ok := "  1800\u2192// NewSettingsManager generates a new SettingsManager pointer and returns it\n  1801\u2192func NewSettingsManager(ctx context.Context, clientset kubernetes.Interface, namespace string, opts ...SettingsManagerOpts) *SettingsManager {\n  1802\u2192\tmgr := &SettingsManager{\n  1803\u2192\t\tctx:       ctx,\n  1804\u2192\t\tclientset: clientset,\n  1805\u2192\t\tnamespace: namespace,\n  1806\u2192\t\tmutex:     &sync.Mutex{},\n  1807\u2192\t}\n  1808\u2192\tfor i := range opts {\n  1809\u2192\t\topts[i](mgr)\n  1810\u2192\t}\n  1811\u2192\n  1812\u2192\treturn mgr\n  1813\u2192}\n  1814\u2192\n  1815\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) ResyncInformers() error {\n  1816\u2192\treturn mgr.ensureSynced(true)\n  1817\u2192}\n  1818\u2192\n  1819\u2192// IsSSOConfigured returns whether or not single-sign-on is configured\n  1820\u2192func (a *ArgoCDSettings) IsSSOConfigured() bool {\n  1821\u2192\tif a.IsDexConfigured() {\n  1822\u2192\t\treturn true\n  1823\u2192\t}\n  1824\u2192\tif a.OIDCConfig() != nil {\n  1825\u2192\t\treturn true\n  1826\u2192\t}\n  1827\u2192\treturn false\n  1828\u2192}\n  1829\u2192\n  1830\u2192func (a *ArgoCDSettings) IsDexConfigured() bool {\n  1831\u2192\tif a.URL == \"\" {\n  1832\u2192\t\treturn false\n  1833\u2192\t}\n  1834\u2192\tdexCfg, err := UnmarshalDexConfig(a.DexConfig)\n  1835\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1836\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"invalid dex yaml config: %s\", err.Error())\n  1837\u2192\t\treturn false\n  1838\u2192\t}\n  1839\u2192\treturn len(dexCfg) > 0\n  1840\u2192}\n  1841\u2192\n  1842\u2192// GetServerEncryptionKey generates a new server encryption key using the server signature as a passphrase\n  1843\u2192func (a *ArgoCDSettings) GetServerEncryptionKey() ([]byte, error) {\n  1844\u2192\treturn crypto.KeyFromPassphrase(string(a.ServerSignature))\n  1845\u2192}\n  1846\u2192\n  1847\u2192func UnmarshalDexConfig(config string) (map[string]interface{}, error) {\n  1848\u2192\tvar dexCfg map[string]interface{}\n  1849\u2192\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(config), &dexCfg)\n  1850\u2192\treturn dexCfg, err\n  1851\u2192}\n  1852\u2192\n  1853\u2192func (a *ArgoCDSettings) oidcConfig() *oidcConfig {\n  1854\u2192\tif a.OIDCConfigRAW == \"\" {\n  1855\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1856\u2192\t}\n  1857\u2192\tconfigMap := map[string]interface{}{}\n  1858\u2192\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(a.OIDCConfigRAW), &configMap)\n  1859\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1860\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"invalid oidc config: %v\", err)\n  1861\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1862\u2192\t}\n  1863\u2192\n  1864\u2192\tconfigMap = ReplaceMapSecrets(configMap, a.Secrets)\n  1865\u2192\tdata, err := yaml.Marshal(configMap)\n  1866\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1867\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"invalid oidc config: %v\", err)\n  1868\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1869\u2192\t}\n  1870\u2192\n  1871\u2192\tconfig, err := unmarshalOIDCConfig(string(data))\n  1872\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1873\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"invalid oidc config: %v\", err)\n  1874\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1875\u2192\t}\n  1876\u2192\n  1877\u2192\treturn &config\n  1878\u2192}\n  1879\u2192\n  1880\u2192func (a *ArgoCDSettings) OIDCConfig() *OIDCConfig {\n  1881\u2192\tconfig := a.oidcConfig()\n  1882\u2192\tif config == nil {\n  1883\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1884\u2192\t}\n  1885\u2192\treturn config.toExported()\n  1886\u2192}\n  1887\u2192\n  1888\u2192func unmarshalOIDCConfig(configStr string) (oidcConfig, error) {\n  1889\u2192\tvar config oidcConfig\n  1890\u2192\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(configStr), &config)\n  1891\u2192\treturn config, err\n  1892\u2192}\n  1893\u2192\n  1894\u2192func ValidateOIDCConfig(configStr string) error {\n  1895\u2192\t_, err := unmarshalOIDCConfig(configStr)\n  1896\u2192\treturn err\n  1897\u2192}\n  1898\u2192\n  1899\u2192// TLSConfig returns a tls.Config with the configured certificates\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. secretsInformer\n  1449\u2192\tmgr.configmaps = v1listers.NewConfigMapLister(cmInformer.GetIndexer())\n  1450\u2192\treturn nil\n  1451\u2192}\n  1452\u2192\n  1453\u2192func (mgr *SettingsManager) ensureSynced(forceResync bool) error {\n  1454\u2192\tmgr.mutex.Lock()\n  1455\u2192\tdefer mgr.mutex.Unlock()\n  1456\u2192\tif !forceResync && mgr.secrets != nil && mgr.configmaps != nil {\n  1457\u2192\t\treturn nil\n  1458\u2192\t}\n  1459\u2192\n  1460\u2192\tif mgr.initContextCancel != nil {\n  1461\u2192\t\tmgr.initContextCancel()\n  1462\u2192\t}\n  1463\u2192\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(mgr.ctx)\n  1464\u2192\tmgr.initContextCancel = cancel\n  1465\u2192\treturn mgr.initialize(ctx)\n  1466\u2192}\n  1467\u2192\n  1468\u2192func getDownloadBinaryUrlsFromConfigMap(argoCDCM *apiv1.ConfigMap) map[string]string {\n  1469\u2192\tbinaryUrls := map[string]string{}\n  1470\u2192\tfor _, archType := range []string{\"darwin-amd64\", \"darwin-arm64\", \"windows-amd64\", \"linux-amd64\", \"linux-arm64\", \"linux-ppc64le\", \"linux-s390x\"} {\n  1471\u2192\t\tif val, ok := argoCDCM.Data[settingsBinaryUrlsKey+\".\"+archType]; ok {\n  1472\u2192\t\t\tbinaryUrls[archType] = val\n  1473\u2192\t\t}\n  1474\u2192\t}\n  1475\u2192\treturn binaryUrls\n  1476\u2192}\n  1477\u2192\n  1478\u2192// updateSettingsFromConfigMap transfers settings from a Kubernetes configmap into an ArgoCDSettings struct.\n  1479\u2192func updateSettingsFromConfigMap(settings *ArgoCDSettings, argoCDCM *apiv1.ConfigMap) {\n  1480\u2192\tsettings.DexConfig = argoCDCM.Data[settingDexConfigKey]\n  1481\u2192\tsettings.OIDCConfigRAW = argoCDCM.Data[settingsOIDCConfigKey]\n  1482\u2192\tsettings.KustomizeBuildOptions = argoCDCM.Data[kustomizeBuildOptionsKey]\n  1483\u2192\tsettings.StatusBadgeEnabled = argoCDCM.Data[statusBadgeEnabledKey] == \"true\"\n  1484\u2192\tsettings.StatusBadgeRootUrl = argoCDCM.Data[statusBadgeRootUrlKey]\n  1485\u2192\tsettings.AnonymousUserEnabled = argoCDCM.Data[anonymousUserEnabledKey] == \"true\"\n  1486\u2192\tsettings.UiCssURL = argoCDCM.Data[settingUiCssURLKey]\n  1487\u2192\tsettings.UiBannerContent = argoCDCM.Data[settingUiBannerContentKey]\n  1488\u2192\tsettings.UiBannerPermanent = argoCDCM.Data[settingUiBannerPermanentKey] == \"true\"\n  1489\u2192\tsettings.UiBannerPosition = argoCDCM.Data[settingUiBannerPositionKey]\n  1490\u2192\tsettings.ServerRBACLogEnforceEnable = argoCDCM.Data[settingsServerRBACLogEnforceEnableKey] == \"true\"\n  1491\u2192\tsettings.BinaryUrls = getDownloadBinaryUrlsFromConfigMap(argoCDCM)\n  1492\u2192\tif err := validateExternalURL(argoCDCM.Data[settingURLKey]); err != nil {\n  1493\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Failed to validate URL in configmap: %v\", err)\n  1494\u2192\t}\n  1495\u2192\tsettings.URL = argoCDCM.Data[settingURLKey]\n  1496\u2192\tif err := validateExternalURL(argoCDCM.Data[settingUiBannerURLKey]); err != nil {\n  1497\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Failed to validate UI banner URL in configmap: %v\", err)\n  1498\u2192\t}\n  1499\u2192\tif argoCDCM.Data[settingAdditionalUrlsKey] != \"\" {\n  1500\u2192\t\tif err := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(argoCDCM.Data[settingAdditionalUrlsKey]), &settings.AdditionalURLs); err != nil {\n  1501\u2192\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Failed to decode all additional URLs in configmap: %v\", err)\n  1502\u2192\t\t}\n  1503\u2192\t}\n  1504\u2192\tfor _, url := range settings.AdditionalURLs {\n  1505\u2192\t\tif err := validateExternalURL(url); err != nil {\n  1506\u2192\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Failed to validate external URL in configmap: %v\", err)\n  1507\u2192\t\t}\n  1508\u2192\t}\n  1509\u2192\tsettings.UiBannerURL = argoCDCM.Data[settingUiBannerURLKey]\n  1510\u2192\tsettings.UserSessionDuration = time.Hour * 24\n  1511\u2192\tif userSessionDurationStr, ok := argoCDCM.Data[userSessionDurationKey]; ok {\n  1512\u2192\t\tif val, err := timeutil.ParseDuration(userSessionDurationStr); err != nil {\n  1513\u2192\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Failed to parse '%s' key: %v\", userSessionDurationKey, err)\n  1514\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1515\u2192\t\t\tsettings.UserSessionDuration = *val\n  1516\u2192\t\t}\n  1517\u2192\t}\n  1518\u2192\tsettings.PasswordPattern = argoCDCM.Data[settingsPasswordPatternKey]\n  1519\u2192\tif settings.PasswordPattern == \"\" {\n  1520\u2192\t\tsettings.PasswordPattern = common.PasswordPatten\n  1521\u2192\t}\n  1522\u2192\tif maxPodLogsToRenderStr, ok := argoCDCM.Data[settingsMaxPodLogsToRender]; ok {\n  1523\u2192\t\tif val, err := strconv.ParseInt(maxPodLogsToRenderStr, 10, 64); err != nil {\n  1524\u2192\t\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Failed to parse '%s' key: %v\", settingsMaxPodLogsToRender, err)\n  1525\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1526\u2192\t\t\tsettings.MaxPodLogsToRender = val\n  1527\u2192\t\t}\n  1528\u2192\t}\n  1529\u2192\tsettings.InClusterEnabled = argoCDCM.Data[inClusterEnabledKey] != \"false\"\n  1530\u2192\tsettings.ExecEnabled = argoCDCM.Data[execEnabledKey] == \"true\"\n  1531\u2192\texecShells := argoCDCM.Data[execShellsKey]\n  1532\u2192\tif execShells != \"\" {\n  1533\u2192\t\tsettings.ExecShells = strings.Split(execShells, \",\")\n  1534\u2192\t} else {\n  1535\u2192\t\t// Fall back to default. If you change this list, also change docs/operator-manual/argocd-cm.yaml.\n  1536\u2192\t\tsettings.ExecShells = []string{\"bash\", \"sh\", \"powershell\", \"cmd\"}\n  1537\u2192\t}\n  1538\u2192\tsettings.TrackingMethod = argoCDCM.Data[settingsResourceTrackingMethodKey]\n  1539\u2192\tsettings.OIDCTLSInsecureSkipVerify = argoCDCM.Data[oidcTLSInsecureSkipVerifyKey] == \"true\"\n  1540\u2192\tsettings.ExtensionConfig = getExtensionConfigs(argoCDCM.Data)\n  1541\u2192\tsettings.ImpersonationEnabled = argoCDCM.Data[impersonationEnabledKey] == \"true\"\n  1542\u2192}\n  1543\u2192\n  1544\u2192func getExtensionConfigs(cmData map[string]string) map[string]string {\n  1545\u2192\tresult := make(map[string]string)\n  1546\u2192\tfor k, v := range cmData {\n  1547\u2192\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, extensionConfig) {\n  1548\u2192\t\t\textName := strings.TrimPrefix(strings.TrimPrefix(k, extensionConfig), \".\")\n  1549\u2192\t\t\tresult[extName] = v\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

SaveSSHKnownHostsData(ctx context.Context, knownHostsList []string) error {\n  1728\u2192\terr := mgr.ensureSynced(false)\n  1729\u2192\tif err != nil {\n  1730\u2192\t\treturn err\n  1731\u2192\t}\n 7\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n     8\u2192\t\"flag\"\n     9\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n    10\u2192\t\"os\"\n    11\u2192\t\"os/exec\"\n    12\u2192\t\"path\"\n    13\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n    14\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/gitops-engine/pkg/utils/text\"\n    17\u2192\t\"github.com/google/shlex\"\n    18\u2192\tlog \"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n    19\u2192\t\"github.com/spf13/cobra\"\n    20\u2192\t\"github.com/spf13/pflag\"\n    21\u2192\tterminal \"golang.org/x/term\"\n    22\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\"\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/clientcmd\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/klog/v2\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/util/term\"\n    26\u2192\t\"sigs.k8s.io/yaml\"\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/common\"\n    29\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/errors\"\n    30\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/io\"\n    31\u2192\tutillog \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/log\"\n    32\u2192)\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192// NewVersionCmd returns a new `version` command to be used as a sub-command to root\n    35\u2192func NewVersionCmd(cliName string) *cobra.Command {\n    36\u2192\tvar short bool\n    37\u2192\tversionCmd := cobra.Command{\n    38\u2192\t\tUse:   \"version\",\n    39\u2192\t\tShort: \"Print version information\",\n    40\u2192\t\tRun: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) {\n    41\u2192\t\t\tversion := common.GetVersion()\n    42\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"%s: %s\\n\", cliName, version)\n    43\u2192\t\t\tif short {\n    44\u2192\t\t\t\treturn\n    45\u2192\t\t\t}\n    46\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  BuildDate: %s\\n\", version.BuildDate)\n    47\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  GitCommit: %s\\n\", version.GitCommit)\n    48\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  GitTreeState: %s\\n\", version.GitTreeState)\n    49\u2192\t\t\tif version.GitTag != \"\" {\n    50\u2192\t\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  GitTag: %s\\n\", version.GitTag)\n    51\u2192\t\t\t}\n    52\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  GoVersion: %s\\n\", version.GoVersion)\n    53\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  Compiler: %s\\n\", version.Compiler)\n    54\u2192\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  Platform: %s\\n\", version.Platform)\n    55\u2192\t\t\tif version.ExtraBuildInfo != \"\" {\n    56\u2192\t\t\t\tfmt.Printf(\"  ExtraBuildInfo: %s\\n\", version.ExtraBuildInfo)\n    57\u2192\t\t\t}\n    58\u2192\t\t},\n    59\u2192\t}\n    60\u2192\tversionCmd.Flags().BoolVar(&short, \"short\", false, \"print just the version number\")\n    61\u2192\treturn &versionCmd\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192// AddKubectlFlagsToCmd adds kubectl like flags to a persistent flags of a command and returns the ClientConfig interface\n    65\u2192// for retrieving the values.\n    66\u2192func AddKubectlFlagsToCmd(cmd *cobra.Command) clientcmd.ClientConfig {\n    67\u2192\treturn AddKubectlFlagsToSet(cmd.PersistentFlags())\n    68\u2192}\n    69\u2192\n    70\u2192// AddKubectlFlagsToSet adds kubectl like flags to a provided flag set and returns the ClientConfig interface\n    71\u2192// for retrieving the values.\n    72\u2192func AddKubectlFlagsToSet(flags *pflag.FlagSet) clientcmd.ClientConfig {\n    73\u2192\tloadingRules := clientcmd.NewDefaultClientConfigLoadingRules()\n    74\u2192\tloadingRules.DefaultClientConfig = &clientcmd.DefaultClientConfig\n    75\u2192\toverrides := clientcmd.ConfigOverrides{}\n    76\u2192\tkflags := clientcmd.RecommendedConfigOverrideFlags(\"\")\n    77\u2192\tflags.StringVar(&loadingRules.ExplicitPath, \"kubeconfig\", \"\", \"Path to a kube config. Only required if out-of-cluster\")\n    78\u2192\tclientcmd.BindOverrideFlags(&overrides, flags, kflags)\n    79\u2192\treturn clientcmd.NewInteractiveDeferredLoadingClientConfig(loadingRules, &overrides, os.Stdin)\n    80\u2192}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192// PromptCredentials is a helper to prompt the user for a username and password (unless already supplied)\n    83\u2192func PromptCredentials(username, password string) (string, string) {\n    84\u2192\treturn PromptUsername(username), PromptPassword(password)\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192// PromptUsername prompts the user for a username value\n    88\u2192func PromptUsername(username string) string {\n    89\u2192\treturn PromptMessage(\"Username\", username)\n    90\u2192}\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192// PromptMessage prompts the user for a value (unless already supplied)\n    93\u2192func PromptMessage(message, value string) string {\n    94\u2192\tfor value == \"\" {\n    95\u2192\t\treader := bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin)\n    96\u2192\t\tfmt.Print(message + \": \")\n    97\u2192\t\tvalueRaw, err := reader.ReadString('\\n')\n    98\u2192\t\terrors.CheckError(err)\n    99\u2192\t\tvalue = strings.TrimSpace(valueRaw)\n   100\u2192\t}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192package common\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192import (\n     4\u2192\t\"context\"\n     5\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     6\u2192\t\"os\"\n     7\u2192\t\"path/filepath\"\n     8\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n     9\u2192\t\"time\"\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/pkg/errors\"\n    12\u2192\t\"github.com/redis/go-redis/v9\"\n    13\u2192\t\"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n    14\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/codes\"\n    15\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/status\"\n    16\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    17\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes\"\n    18\u2192)\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192// Component names\n    21\u2192const (\n    22\u2192\tApplicationController = \"argocd-application-controller\"\n    23\u2192)\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192// Default service addresses and URLS of Argo CD internal services\n    26\u2192const (\n    27\u2192\t// DefaultRepoServerAddr is the gRPC address of the Argo CD repo server\n    28\u2192\tDefaultRepoServerAddr = \"argocd-repo-server:8081\"\n    29\u2192\t// DefaultDexServerAddr is the HTTP address of the Dex OIDC server, which we run a reverse proxy against\n    30\u2192\tDefaultDexServerAddr = \"argocd-dex-server:5556\"\n    31\u2192\t// DefaultRedisAddr is the default redis address\n    32\u2192\tDefaultRedisAddr = \"argocd-redis:6379\"\n    33\u2192)\n    34\u2192\n    35\u2192// Kubernetes ConfigMap and Secret resource names which hold Argo CD settings\n    36\u2192const (\n    37\u2192\tArgoCDConfigMapName              = \"argocd-cm\"\n    38\u2192\tArgoCDSecretName                 = \"argocd-secret\"\n    39\u2192\tArgoCDNotificationsConfigMapName = \"argocd-notifications-cm\"\n    40\u2192\tArgoCDNotificationsSecretName    = \"argocd-notifications-secret\"\n    41\u2192\tArgoCDRBACConfigMapName          = \"argocd-rbac-cm\"\n    42\u2192\t// ArgoCDKnownHostsConfigMapName contains SSH known hosts data for connecting repositories. Will get mounted as volume to pods\n    43\u2192\tArgoCDKnownHostsConfigMapName = \"argocd-ssh-known-hosts-cm\"\n    44\u2192\t// ArgoCDTLSCertsConfigMapName contains TLS certificate data for connecting repositories. Will get mounted as volume to pods\n    45\u2192\tArgoCDTLSCertsConfigMapName = \"argocd-tls-certs-cm\"\n    46\u2192\tArgoCDGPGKeysConfigMapName  = \"argocd-gpg-keys-cm\"\n    47\u2192\t// ArgoCDAppControllerShardConfigMapName contains the application controller to shard mapping\n    48\u2192\tArgoCDAppControllerShardConfigMapName = \"argocd-app-controller-shard-cm\"\n    49\u2192\tArgoCDCmdParamsConfigMapName          = \"argocd-cmd-params-cm\"\n    50\u2192)\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192// Some default configurables\n    53\u2192const (\n    54\u2192\tDefaultSystemNamespace = \"kube-system\"\n    55\u2192\tDefaultRepoType        = \"git\"\n    56\u2192)\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192// Default listener ports for ArgoCD components\n    59\u2192const (\n    60\u2192\tDefaultPortAPIServer              = 8080\n    61\u2192\tDefaultPortRepoServer             = 8081\n    62\u2192\tDefaultPortArgoCDMetrics          = 8082\n    63\u2192\tDefaultPortArgoCDAPIServerMetrics = 8083\n    64\u2192\tDefaultPortRepoServerMetrics      = 8084\n    65\u2192)\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192// DefaultAddressAPIServer for ArgoCD components\n    68\u2192const (\n    69\u2192\tDefaultAddressAdminDashboard    = \"localhost\"\n    70\u2192\tDefaultAddressAPIServer         = \"0.0.0.0\"\n    71\u2192\tDefaultAddressAPIServerMetrics  = \"0.0.0.0\"\n    72\u2192\tDefaultAddressRepoServer        = \"0.0.0.0\"\n    73\u2192\tDefaultAddressRepoServerMetrics = \"0.0.0.0\"\n    74\u2192)\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192// Default paths on the pod's file system\n    77\u2192const (\n    78\u2192\t// DefaultPathTLSConfig is the default path where TLS certificates for repositories are located\n    79\u2192\tDefaultPathTLSConfig = \"/app/config/tls\"\n    80\u2192\t// DefaultPathSSHConfig is the default path where SSH known hosts are stored\n    81\u2192\tDefaultPathSSHConfig = \"/app/config/ssh\"\n    82\u2192\t// DefaultSSHKnownHostsName is the Default name for the SSH known hosts file\n    83\u2192\tDefaultSSHKnownHostsName = \"ssh_known_hosts\"\n    84\u2192\t// DefaultGnuPgHomePath is the Default path to GnuPG home directory\n    85\u2192\tDefaultGnuPgHomePath = \"/app/config/gpg/keys\"\n    86\u2192\t// DefaultAppConfigPath is the Default path to repo server TLS endpoint config\n    87\u2192\tDefaultAppConfigPath = \"/app/config\"\n    88\u2192\t// DefaultPluginSockFilePath is the Default path to cmp server plugin socket file\n    89\u2192\tDefaultPluginSockFilePath = \"/home/argocd/cmp-server/plugins\"\n    90\u2192\t// DefaultPluginConfigFilePath is the Default path to cmp server plugin configuration file\n    91\u2192\tDefaultPluginConfigFilePath = \"/home/argocd/cmp-server/config\"\n    92\u2192\t// PluginConfigFileName is the Plugin Config File is a ConfigManagementPlugin manifest located inside the plugin container\n    93\u2192\tPluginConfigFileName = \"plugin.yaml\"\n    94\u2192)\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192// Argo CD application related constants\n    97\u2192const (\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192\t// ArgoCDAdminUsername is the username of the 'admin' user\n   100\u2192\tArgoCDAdminUsername = \"admin\"\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be {
