"213:\tEnvVarSSODebug = \"ARGOCD_SSO_DEBUG\"\n215:\tEnvVarRBACDebug = \"ARGOCD_RBAC_DEBUG\"\n217:\tEnvVarSSHDataPath = \"ARGOCD_SSH_DATA_PATH\"\n219:\tEnvVarTLSDataPath = \"ARGOCD_TLS_DATA_PATH\"\n221:\tEnvGitAttemptsCount = \"ARGOCD_GIT_ATTEMPTS_COUNT\"\n223:\tEnvGitRetryMaxDuration = \"ARGOCD_GIT_RETRY_MAX_DURATION\"\n225:\tEnvGitRetryDuration = \"ARGOCD_GIT_RETRY_DURATION\"\n227:\tEnvGitRetryFactor = \"ARGOCD_GIT_RETRY_FACTOR\"\n229:\tEnvGitSubmoduleEnabled = \"ARGOCD_GIT_MODULES_ENABLED\"\n231:\tEnvGnuPGHome = \"ARGOCD_GNUPGHOME\"\n233:\tEnvWatchAPIBufferSize = \"ARGOCD_WATCH_API_BUFFER_SIZE\"\n235:\tEnvPauseGenerationAfterFailedAttempts = \"ARGOCD_PAUSE_GEN_AFTER_FAILED_ATTEMPTS\"\n237:\tEnvPauseGenerationMinutes = \"ARGOCD_PAUSE_GEN_MINUTES\"\n239:\tEnvPauseGenerationRequests = \"ARGOCD_PAUSE_GEN_REQUESTS\"\n241:\tEnvControllerReplicas = \"ARGOCD_CONTROLLER_REPLICAS\"\n243:\tEnvControllerHeartbeatTime = \"ARGOCD_CONTROLLER_HEARTBEAT_TIME\"\n245:\tEnvControllerShard = \"ARGOCD_CONTROLLER_SHARD\"\n247:\tEnvControllerShardingAlgorithm = \"ARGOCD_CONTROLLER_SHARDING_ALGORITHM\"\n249:\tEnvEnableDynamicClusterDistribution = \"ARGOCD_ENABLE_DYNAMIC_CLUSTER_DISTRIBUTION\"\n251:\tEnvEnableGRPCTimeHistogramEnv = \"ARGOCD_ENABLE_GRPC_TIME_HISTOGRAM\"\n253:\tEnvGithubAppCredsExpirationDuration = \"ARGOCD_GITHUB_APP_CREDS_EXPIRATION_DURATION\"\n255:\tEnvHelmIndexCacheDuration = \"ARGOCD_HELM_INDEX_CACHE_DURATION\"\n256:\t// EnvAppConfigPath allows to override the configuration path for repo server\n257:\tEnvAppConfigPath = \"ARGOCD_APP_CONF_PATH\"\n259:\tEnvLogFormat = \"ARGOCD_LOG_FORMAT\"\n261:\tEnvLogLevel = \"ARGOCD_LOG_LEVEL\"\n263:\tEnvLogFormatEnableFullTimestamp = \"ARGOCD_LOG_FORMAT_ENABLE_FULL_TIMESTAMP\"\n265:\tEnvMaxCookieNumber = \"ARGOCD_MAX_COOKIE_NUMBER\"\n267:\tEnvPluginSockFilePath = \"ARGOCD_PLUGINSOCKFILEPATH\"\n269:\tEnvCMPChunkSize = \"ARGOCD_CMP_CHUNK_SIZE\""
102. Task @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Analyze test structure and frameworks",
  "prompt": "Analyze the test structure in Argo CD:\n1. What testing frameworks are used? (Look for testify, ginkgo, standard testing)\n2. Where are unit tests located? Show examples\n3. Where are integration tests located?\n4. Where are E2E tests located and what test framework is used?\n5. What is the structure of E2E tests?\n6. Are there fixture/testdata directories? Where?\n7. How are mocks created? (mockgen, manual, etc.)\n8. Show examples of test files and their structure\n\nFocus on:\n- /workspace/test/ directory\n- *_test.go files in various packages\n- Test fixtures and testdata directories",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "adec3e372d53e4c6d",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Excellent! Now I have enough information to provide a comprehensive analysis. Let me create the final report.\n\n## Comprehensive Test Structure Analysis for Argo CD\n\nBased on my thorough exploration of the Argo CD repository, here's the complete analysis of the test structure:\n\n---\n\n### 1. TESTING FRAMEWORKS USED\n\n**Primary Frameworks:**\n- **testify** (v1.9.0) - Assertion and mocking library\n  - `github.com/stretchr/testify/assert` - Assertion functions\n  - `github.com/stretchr/testify/require` - Assertion functions with fatal error handling\n  - `github.com/stretchr/testify/mock` - Mock objects for unit testing\n\n- **Standard Go testing** - Built-in `testing` package with `*testing.T`\n\n- **No Ginkgo** - The repository does not use Ginkgo for test frameworks\n\n**Supporting Tools:**\n- **Mockery** (v2.43.2) - Code generation tool for creating mock interfaces\n- **miniredis** - In-memory Redis for testing\n- **gitops-engine testing utilities** - Provided testing helpers\n\n---\n\n### 2. UNIT TESTS LOCATION\n\nUnit tests are located **alongside source files** in the same directory as the code they test, following Go convention.\n\n**Examples:**\n\n| Location | File | Type |\n|----------|------|------|\n| `/workspace/common/` | `common_test.go` | Common utilities |\n| `/workspace/server/notification/` | `notification_test.go` | Server module |\n| `/workspace/server/extension/` | `extension_test.go` | Server module |\n| `/workspace/server/cluster/` | `cluster_test.go` | Server module |\n| `/workspace/server/repository/` | `repository_test.go` | Server module |\n| `/workspace/server/project/` | `project_test.go` | Server module |\n| `/workspace/server/badge/` | `badge_test.go` | Server module |\n| `/workspace/server/deeplinks/` | `deeplinks_test.go` | Server module |\n\n**Total Unit Tests:** 266 `*_test.go` files across the codebase\n\n**Unit Test Pattern Example** (`/workspace/common/common_test.go`):\n```go\nimport (\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n)\n\nfunc Test_GRPCKeepAliveMinNotSet(t *testing.T) {\n    grpcKeepAliveMin := GetGRPCKeepAliveEnforcementMinimum()\n    grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin := defaultGRPCKeepAliveEnforcementMinimum\n    assert.Equal(t, grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin, grpcKeepAliveMin)\n}\n\nfunc TestSetOptionalRedisPasswordFromKubeConfig(t *testing.T) {\n    t.Parallel()\n    testCases := []struct {\n        name, namespace, expectedPassword, expectedErr string\n        secret *corev1.Secret\n    }{\n        // Test cases...\n    }\n    for _, tc := range testCases {\n        tc := tc\n        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            t.Parallel()\n            // Test implementation\n        })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n**Key Patterns:**\n- Use of `t.Run()` for subtests\n- Use of `t.Parallel()` for parallel test execution\n- Table-driven tests with test case slices\n- Both `assert` (non-fatal) and `require` (fatal) from testify\n\n---\n\n### 3. INTEGRATION TESTS LOCATION\n\nIntegration tests are located in **two main areas:**\n\n1. **Server module integration tests:**\n   - `/workspace/server/application/application_test.go`\n   - `/workspace/server/application/logs_test.go`\n   - `/workspace/server/application/broadcaster_test.go`\n   - `/workspace/server/application/websocket_test.go`\n   - `/workspace/server/cache/cache_test.go`\n\n2. **Test infrastructure helpers:**\n   - `/workspace/test/testutil.go` - Test utilities for integration tests\n   - `/workspace/test/testdata.go` - Test data generators (manifests, fake objects)\n\n**Integration Test Utilities** (`/workspace/test/testutil.go`):\n```go\n// StartInformer - Start K8s informer with cache sync\nfunc StartInformer(informer cache.SharedIndexInformer) context.CancelFunc\n\n// GetFreePort - Find available free port\nfunc GetFreePort() (int, error)\n\n// WaitForPortListen - Wait for port to become available\nfunc WaitForPortListen(addr string, timeout time.Duration) error\n\n// YamlToUnstructured - Convert YAML to K8s unstructured object\nfunc YamlToUnstructured(yamlStr string) *unstructured.Unstructured\n\n// YamlToApplication - Convert YAML to Argo CD Application\nfunc YamlToApplication(yamlStr string) *v1alpha1.Application\n```\n\n**Test Data Helpers** (`/workspace/test/testdata.go`):\n```go\n// NewFakeConfigMap - Create fake Kubernetes ConfigMap\nfunc NewFakeConfigMap() *apiv1.ConfigMap\n\n// NewFakeSecret - Create fake Kubernetes Secret\nfunc NewFakeSecret(policy ...string) *apiv1.Secret\n\n// NewFakeProjLister - Create fake AppProject lister\nfunc NewFakeProjLister(objects ...runtime.Object) applister.AppProjectNamespaceLister\n\n// NewInMemoryRedis - Create in-memory Redis for testing\nfunc NewInMemoryRedis() (*redis.Client, func())\n```\n\n---\n\n### 4. END-TO-END TESTS LOCATION & FRAMEWORK\n\n**Location:** `/workspace/test/e2e/`\n\n**Test Framework:** Custom **Given-When-Then (GWT)** Behavior-Driven Development (BDD) pattern using Go's standard `testing` package with testify assertions\n\n**Total E2E Tests:** 40+ test files, ~17,000 lines of test code\n\n**E2E Test Files** (Examples):\n```\n/workspace/test/e2e/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 app_management_test.go        (95,000+ bytes)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 app_management_ns_test.go     (84,000+ bytes)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 app_autosync_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 app_deletion_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 applicationset_test.go        (109,000+ bytes)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cluster_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cluster_generator_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 clusterdecisiongenerator_e2e_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 custom_tool_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 deployment_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 helm_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 hook_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 kustomize_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 matrix_e2e_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 merge_e2e_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 project_management_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 repo_creds_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 sync_options_test.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 sync_waves_test.go\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 ... (40+ total test files)\n```\n\n**E2E Framework Pattern:**\n```go\nimport (\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n    . \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/test/e2e/fixture\"\n    . \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/test/e2e/fixture/app\"\n    accountFixture \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/test/e2e/fixture/account\"\n)\n\nfunc TestGetLogsAllowSwitchOn(t *testing.T) {\n    SkipOnEnv(t, \"OPENSHIFT\")\n    \n    accountFixture.Given(t).\n        Name(\"test\").\n        When().\n        Create().\n        Login().\n        SetPermissions([]fixture.ACL{...}, \"app-creator\").\n    \n    GivenWithSameState(t).\n        Path(\"guestbook-logs\").\n        When().\n        CreateApp().\n        Sync().\n        SetParamInSettingConfigMap(\"server.rbac.log.enforce.enable\", \"true\").\n        Then().\n        Expect(HealthIs(health.HealthStatusHealthy)).\n        And(func(app *Application) {\n            out, err := RunCliWithRetry(appLogsRetryCount, \"app\", \"logs\", app.Name)\n            require.NoError(t, err)\n            assert.Contains(t, out, \"Hi\")\n        })\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. E2E TEST STRUCTURE (GWT Pattern)\n\nThe E2E tests use a sophisticated **Given-When-Then** pattern with fluent API:\n\n**Architecture:**\n```\nfixture.go          - Main fixture initialization and setup\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 fixture/fixture.go (36KB) - E2E test infrastructure\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 fixture/cmd.go       - Command execution\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 fixture/util.go      - Utilities\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 fixture/tmpl.go      - Template handling\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 fixture/http.go      - HTTP utilities\n\nfixture/app/         - Application-specific fixtures\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 context.go        - \"Given\" phase (test setup)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 actions.go        - \"When\" phase (test actions)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 expectation.go    - \"Then\" phase (assertions)\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 consequences.go   - Result handling\n\nfixture/account/     - Account/user fixtures\nfixture/admin/       - Admin fixtures\nfixture/cluster/     - Cluster fixtures\nfixture/project/     - Project fixtures\nfixture/repos/       - Repository fixtures\nfixture/applicationsets/ - ApplicationSet fixtures\nfixture/notification/  - Notification fixtures\n```\n\n**Pattern Components:**\n\n1. **Context (Given Phase):** Setup test state\n```go\n// /workspace/test/e2e/fixture/app/context.go\ntype Context struct {\n    t *testing.T\n    path string\n    chart string\n    repoURLType fixture.RepoURLType\n    timeout int\n    name string\n    // ... more fields\n}\n\nfunc Given(t *testing.T, opts ...fixture.TestOption) *Context\nfunc GivenWithSameState(t *testing.T) *Context\n```\n\n2. **Actions (When Phase):** Perform operations\n```go\n// /workspace/test/e2e/fixture/app/actions.go\ntype Actions struct {\n    context *Context\n    lastOutput string\n    lastError error\n    ignoreErrors bool\n}\n\nfunc (a *Actions) CreateApp() *Actions\nfunc (a *Actions) Sync() *Actions\nfunc (a *Actions) WriteFile(fileName, fileContents string) *Actions\nfunc (a *Actions) PatchFile(file string, jsonPath string) *Actions\n```\n\n3. **Expectation (Then Phase):** Assert results\n```go\n// /workspace/test/e2e/fixture/app/expectation.go\ntype Expectation func(c *Consequences) (state state, message string)\n\nfunc OperationPhaseIs(expected OperationPhase) Expectation\nfunc SyncStatusIs(expected SyncStatusCode) Expectation\nfunc HealthIs(expected HealthStatus) Expectation\nfunc Condition(conditionType ApplicationConditionType, ...) Expectation\n```\n\n4. **Consequences:** Verify state after actions\n```go\n// /workspace/test/e2e/fixture/app/consequences.go\ntype Consequences struct {\n    // ... fields\n}\n\nfunc (c *Consequences) And(a func(app *Application)) *Consequences\nfunc (c *Consequences) Expect(expectations ...Expectation) *Consequences\nfunc (c *Consequences) Then() *Consequences\n```\n\n---\n\n### 6. FIXTURE & TESTDATA DIRECTORIES\n\n**Fixture Directories:**\n\n1. **E2E Fixtures** (`/workspace/test/e2e/fixture/`):\n   ```\n   /workspace/test/e2e/fixture/\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 fixture.go                 - Main fixture (36KB)\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 app/                       - App fixture (context, actions, expectations)\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 account/                   - Account/user fixtures\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 admin/                     - Admin fixtures\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 admin/utils/               - Admin utilities\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 applicationsets/           - ApplicationSet fixtures\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 applicationsets/utils/     - ApplicationSet utilities\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 cluster/                   - Cluster fixtures\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 project/                   - Project fixtures\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 repos/                     - Repository fixtures\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 notification/              - Notification fixtures\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 certs/                     - Test certificates\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 gpgkeys/                   - GPG keys for signing tests\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 cmd.go                     - Command execution\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 http.go                    - HTTP utilities\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 tmpl.go                    - Template handling\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 util.go                    - Utilities\n   \u2514\u2500\u2500 versions.go                - Version handling\n   ```\n\n2. **Test Fixtures** (`/workspace/test/fixture/`):\n   ```\n   /workspace/test/fixture/\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 certs/                     - SSL/TLS certificates\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 gpg/                       - GPG keys\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 log/                       - Log fixtures\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 path/                      - Path utilities\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 revision_metadata/         - Git revision metadata\n   \u251c\u2500\u2500 test/                      - General test fixtures\n   \u2514\u2500\u2500 testrepos/                 - Test Git repositories\n   ```\n\n**Test Data Directories:**\n\n1. **E2E Test Data** (`/workspace/test/e2e/testdata/` & `testdata2/`):\n   - **50+ application test scenarios:**\n   ```\n   guestbook/                     - Basic guestbook example\n   guestbook-logs/                - Guestbook with logging\n   guestbook-with-namespace/      - Namespace variants\n   deployment/                    - Deployment examples\n   helm/                          - Helm chart examples\n   helm-with-dependencies/        - Helm with dependencies\n   kustomize/                     - Kustomize examples\n   jsonnet-ext-var/               - Jsonnet with variables\n   ksonnet/                       - Ksonnet examples\n   hook/                          - Sync hook examples\n   crd-creation/                  - CRD creation tests\n   cmp-find-command/              - Config management plugin tests\n   cluster-role/                  - RBAC examples\n   declarative-apps/              - Declarative app definitions\n   git-submodule/                 - Git submodule handling\n   sync-options-validate-false/   - Sync options\n   ... (60+ more directories)\n   ```\n\n2. **Unit Test Data** (`/workspace/controller/testdata/`):\n   ```\n   target-deployment.yaml\n   live-deployment.yaml\n   configmap.yaml\n   job-failed.yaml\n   pod-running-restart-always.yaml\n   diff-cache.yaml\n   ```\n\n3. **Repository Test Data** (`/workspace/reposerver/repository/testdata/`):\n   - Git file/directory structures\n   - Helm chart examples\n   - Configuration files\n\n**TestData Embedding** (`/workspace/test/e2e/testdata/data.go`):\n```go\nimport _ \"embed\"\n\nvar (\n    //go:embed ssa-revision-history/deployment.yaml\n    SSARevisionHistoryDeployment string\n\n    //go:embed guestbook/guestbook-ui-deployment.yaml\n    GuestbookDeployment string\n)\n```\n\n---\n\n### 7. MOCK CREATION METHOD\n\n**Tool Used:** **Mockery v2.43.2** - Automatic mock code generation\n\n**Configuration:** `/workspace/.mockery.yaml`\n\n**Mechanism:**\n```yaml\n# Global config\nfilename: \"{{.InterfaceName}}.go\"\ndir: \"{{.InterfaceDir}}/mocks\"\noutpkg: \"mocks\"\nmockname: \"{{.InterfaceName}}\"\nwith-expecter: false\n\n# Individual interfaces to mock\npackages:\n  github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/applicationset/generators:\n    interfaces:\n      Generator:\n  github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/controller/cache:\n    interfaces:\n      LiveStateCache:\n  github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/db:\n    interfaces:\n      ArgoDB:\n  github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/git:\n    interfaces:\n      Client:\n  # ... 60+ more interface configurations\n```\n\n**Build Command:** `/workspace/hack/generate-mock.sh`\n```bash\n#!/bin/bash\nset -x\nmockery --config ${PROJECT_ROOT}/.mockery.yaml\n```\n\n**Invoked by:** `make mockgen` in Makefile\n\n**Generated Mocks Location:**\n```\n/workspace/server/extension/mocks/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 ExtensionMetricsRegistry.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 RbacEnforcer.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 ProjectGetter.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 UserGetter.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 SettingsGetter.go\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 ApplicationGetter.go\n\n/workspace/controller/cache/mocks/\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 LiveStateCache.go\n\n/workspace/reposerver/apiclient/mocks/\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 RepoServerServiceClient.go\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 RepoServerService_GenerateManifestWithFilesClient.go\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 Clientset.go\n\n/workspace/util/db/mocks/\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 ArgoDB.go\n\n/workspace/util/git/mocks/\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 Client.go\n\n/workspace/util/helm/mocks/\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 Client.go\n\n... (20+ more mock packages)\n```\n\n**Example Generated Mock** (`/workspace/server/extension/mocks/RbacEnforcer.go`):\n```go\n// Code generated by mockery v2.43.2. DO NOT EDIT.\n\npackage mocks\n\nimport mock \"github.com/stretchr/testify/mock\"\n\n// RbacEnforcer is an autogenerated mock type for the RbacEnforcer type\ntype RbacEnforcer struct {\n    mock.Mock\n}\n\n// EnforceErr provides a mock function with given fields: rvals\nfunc (_m *RbacEnforcer) EnforceErr(rvals ...interface{}) error {\n    var _ca []interface{}\n    _ca = append(_ca, rvals...)\n    ret := _m.Called(_ca...)\n    // ...\n    return r0\n}\n\n// NewRbacEnforcer creates a new instance of RbacEnforcer\nfunc NewRbacEnforcer(t interface {\n    mock.TestingT\n    Cleanup(func())\n}) *RbacEnforcer {\n    mock := &RbacEnforcer{}\n    mock.Mock.Test(t)\n    t.Cleanup(func() { mock.AssertExpectations(t) })\n    return mock\n}\n```\n\n**Usage in Tests** (`/workspace/server/extension/extension_test.go`):\n```go\nfunc TestRegisterExtensions(t *testing.T) {\n    type fixture struct {\n        settingsGetterMock *mocks.SettingsGetter\n        manager *extension.Manager\n    }\n\n    setup := func() *fixture {\n        settMock := &mocks.SettingsGetter{}\n        // Configure mock expectations\n        settMock.On(\"GetSettings\", mock.Anything).Return(...)\n        // ...\n    }\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### 8. TEST FILE EXAMPLES & STRUCTURE\n\n#### **Unit Test Example** - `/workspace/common/common_test.go`\n```go\npackage common\n\nimport (\n    \"testing\"\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n)\n\nfunc Test_GRPCKeepAliveMinNotSet(t *testing.T) {\n    grpcKeepAliveMin := GetGRPCKeepAliveEnforcementMinimum()\n    grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin := defaultGRPCKeepAliveEnforcementMinimum\n    assert.Equal(t, grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin, grpcKeepAliveMin)\n}\n\nfunc TestSetOptionalRedisPasswordFromKubeConfig(t *testing.T) {\n    t.Parallel()\n    testCases := []struct {\n        name, namespace, expectedPassword, expectedErr string\n        secret *corev1.Secret\n    }{\n        {\n            name: \"Secret exists with correct key\",\n            namespace: \"default\",\n            expectedPassword: \"password123\",\n            expectedErr: \"\",\n            secret: &corev1.Secret{\n                ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: \"...\"},\n                Data: map[string][]byte{...},\n            },\n        },\n        // More test cases...\n    }\n    for _, tc := range testCases {\n        tc := tc\n        t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n            t.Parallel()\n            var (\n                ctx = context.TODO()\n                kubeClient = kubefake.NewSimpleClientset()\n                redisOptions = &redis.Options{}\n            )\n            // Test implementation\n        })\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### **Unit Test with Mocks** - `/workspace/server/extension/extension_test.go`\n```go\npackage extension_test\n\nimport (\n    \"testing\"\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/mock\"\n    \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/server/extension/mocks\"\n)\n\nfunc TestValidateHeaders(t *testing.T) {\n    t.Run(\"will build RequestResources successfully\", func(t *testing.T) {\n        // given\n        r, err := http.NewRequest(\"Get\", \"http://null\", nil)\n        require.NoError(t, err)\n        r.Header.Add(extension.HeaderArgoCDApplicationName, \"namespace:app-name\")\n        r.Header.Add(extension.HeaderArgoCDProjectName, \"project-name\")\n\n        // when\n        rr, err := extension.ValidateHeaders(r)\n\n        // then\n        require.NoError(t, err)\n        assert.NotNil(t, rr)\n        assert.Equal(t, \"namespace\", rr.ApplicationNamespace)\n    })\n}\n\nfunc TestRegisterExtensions(t *testing.T) {\n    type fixture struct {\n        settingsGetterMock *mocks.SettingsGetter\n        manager *extension.Manager\n    }\n\n    setup := func() *fixture {\n        settMock := &mocks.SettingsGetter{}\n        // Mock setup\n        return &fixture{settingsGetterMock: settMock}\n    }\n}\n```\n\n#### **E2E Test Example** - `/workspace/test/e2e/app_management_test.go`\n```go\npackage e2e\n\nimport (\n    \"testing\"\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert\"\n    \"github.com/stretchr/testify/require\"\n    . \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/test/e2e/fixture\"\n    . \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/test/e2e/fixture/app\"\n    accountFixture \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/test/e2e/fixture/account\"\n)\n\nfunc TestGetLogsAllowSwitchOn(t *testing.T) {\n    SkipOnEnv(t, \"OPENSHIFT\")\n    \n    // Setup account with specific permissions\n    accountFixture.Given(t).\n        Name(\"test\").\n        When().\n        Create().\n        Login().\n        SetPermissions([]fixture.ACL{\n            {\n                Resource: \"applications\",\n                Action: \"create\",\n                Scope: \"*\",\n            },\n            // More permissions...\n        }, \"app-creator\").\n    \n    // Test application behavior\n    GivenWithSameState(t).\n        Path(\"guestbook-logs\").\n        When().\n        CreateApp().\n        Sync().\n        SetParamInSettingConfigMap(\"server.rbac.log.enforce.enable\", \"true\").\n        Then().\n        Expect(HealthIs(health.HealthStatusHealthy)).\n        And(func(app *Application) {\n            out, err := RunCliWithRetry(appLogsRetryCount, \n                \"app\", \"logs\", app.Name, \n                \"--kind\", \"Deployment\", \n                \"--group\", \"\", \n                \"--name\", \"guestbook-ui\")\n            require.NoError(t, err)\n            assert.Contains(t, out, \"Hi\")\n        })\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### SUMMARY TABLE\n\n| Aspect | Details |\n|--------|---------|\n| **Testing Frameworks** | testify v1.9.0, standard Go testing package |\n| **Unit Tests** | 266+ files, located alongside source code |\n| **Integration Tests** | Server modules + test utilities/data helpers |\n| **E2E Tests** | 40+ files in `/workspace/test/e2e/`, ~17,000 lines |\n| **E2E Pattern** | Given-When-Then (GWT) with fluent API |\n| **Mock Framework** | Mockery v2.43.2 for auto-generation |\n| **Mock Generation** | Via `.mockery.yaml` config, 60+ interface mocks |\n| **Test Data** | 50+ test scenarios in `/workspace/test/e2e/testdata/` |\n| **Fixtures** | BDD-style fixtures in `/workspace/test/e2e/fixture/` |\n| **Fake K8s Objects** | Via `kubefake.NewSimpleClientset()` |\n| **Fake Redis** | Via `miniredis` in-memory Redis |\n| **Test Config** | Environment variables for E2E test control |\n\nThis comprehensive test structure enables Argo CD to maintain high test coverage with clear separation between unit, integration, and end-to-end testing while using a modern, maintainable BDD-style approach for E2E tests.",
      "type": "text"
    }

\"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    14\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    15\u2192\tkubefake \"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/fake\"\n    16\u2192)\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192// Test env var not set for EnvGRPCKeepAliveMin\n    19\u2192func Test_GRPCKeepAliveMinNotSet(t *testing.T) {\n    20\u2192\tgrpcKeepAliveMin := GetGRPCKeepAliveEnforcementMinimum()\n    21\u2192\tgrpcKeepAliveExpectedMin := defaultGRPCKeepAliveEnforcementMinimum\n    22\u2192\tassert.Equal(t, grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin, grpcKeepAliveMin)\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192\tgrpcKeepAliveTime := GetGRPCKeepAliveTime()\n    25\u2192\tassert.Equal(t, 2*grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin, grpcKeepAliveTime)\n    26\u2192}\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192// Test valid env var set for EnvGRPCKeepAliveMin\n    29\u2192func Test_GRPCKeepAliveMinIsSet(t *testing.T) {\n    30\u2192\tnumSeconds := 15\n    31\u2192\tos.Setenv(EnvGRPCKeepAliveMin, fmt.Sprintf(\"%ds\", numSeconds))\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192\tgrpcKeepAliveMin := GetGRPCKeepAliveEnforcementMinimum()\n    34\u2192\tgrpcKeepAliveExpectedMin := time.Duration(numSeconds) * time.Second\n    35\u2192\tassert.Equal(t, grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin, grpcKeepAliveMin)\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192\tgrpcKeepAliveTime := GetGRPCKeepAliveTime()\n    38\u2192\tassert.Equal(t, 2*grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin, grpcKeepAliveTime)\n    39\u2192}\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192// Test invalid env var set for EnvGRPCKeepAliveMin\n    42\u2192func Test_GRPCKeepAliveMinIncorrectlySet(t *testing.T) {\n    43\u2192\tnumSeconds := 15\n    44\u2192\tos.Setenv(EnvGRPCKeepAliveMin, fmt.Sprintf(\"%d\", numSeconds))\n    45\u2192\n    46\u2192\tgrpcKeepAliveMin := GetGRPCKeepAliveEnforcementMinimum()\n    47\u2192\tgrpcKeepAliveExpectedMin := defaultGRPCKeepAliveEnforcementMinimum\n    48\u2192\tassert.Equal(t, grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin, grpcKeepAliveMin)\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192\tgrpcKeepAliveTime := GetGRPCKeepAliveTime()\n    51\u2192\tassert.Equal(t, 2*grpcKeepAliveExpectedMin, grpcKeepAliveTime)\n    52\u2192}\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192func TestSetOptionalRedisPasswordFromKubeConfig(t *testing.T) {\n    55\u2192\tt.Parallel()\n    56\u2192\ttestCases := []struct {\n    57\u2192\t\tname, namespace, expectedPassword, expectedErr string\n    58\u2192\t\tsecret                                         *corev1.Secret\n    59\u2192\t}{\n    60\u2192\t\t{\n    61\u2192\t\t\tname:             \"Secret exists with correct key\",\n    62\u2192\t\t\tnamespace:        \"default\",\n    63\u2192\t\t\texpectedPassword: \"password123\",\n    64\u2192\t\t\texpectedErr:      \"\",\n    65\u2192\t\t\tsecret: &corev1.Secret{\n    66\u2192\t\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: DefaultRedisInitialPasswordSecretName},\n    67\u2192\t\t\t\tData:       map[string][]byte{DefaultRedisInitialPasswordKey: []byte(\"password123\")},\n    68\u2192\t\t\t},\n    69\u2192\t\t},\n    70\u2192\t\t{\n    71\u2192\t\t\tname:             \"Secret does not exist\",\n    72\u2192\t\t\tnamespace:        \"default\",\n    73\u2192\t\t\texpectedPassword: \"\",\n    74\u2192\t\t\texpectedErr:      fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to get secret default/%s\", DefaultRedisInitialPasswordSecretName),\n    75\u2192\t\t\tsecret:           nil,\n    76\u2192\t\t},\n    77\u2192\t\t{\n    78\u2192\t\t\tname:             \"Secret exists without correct key\",\n    79\u2192\t\t\tnamespace:        \"default\",\n    80\u2192\t\t\texpectedPassword: \"\",\n    81\u2192\t\t\texpectedErr:      fmt.Sprintf(\"secret default/%s does not contain key %s\", DefaultRedisInitialPasswordSecretName, DefaultRedisInitialPasswordKey),\n    82\u2192\t\t\tsecret: &corev1.Secret{\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: DefaultRedisInitialPasswordSecretName},\n    84\u2192\t\t\t\tData:       map[string][]byte{},\n    85\u2192\t\t\t},\n    86\u2192\t\t},\n    87\u2192\t}\n    88\u2192\tfor _, tc := range testCases {\n    89\u2192\t\ttc := tc\n    90\u2192\t\tt.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {\n    91\u2192\t\t\tt.Parallel()\n    92\u2192\t\t\tvar (\n    93\u2192\t\t\t\tctx          = context.TODO()\n    94\u2192\t\t\t\tkubeClient   = kubefake.NewSimpleClientset()\n    95\u2192\t\t\t\tredisOptions = &redis.Options{}\n    96\u2192\t\t\t)\n    97\u2192\t\t\tif tc.secret != nil {\n    98\u2192\t\t\t\tif _, err := kubeClient.CoreV1().Secrets(tc.namespace).Create(ctx, tc.secret, metav1.CreateOptions{}); err != nil {\n    99\u2192\t\t\t\t\tt.Fatalf(\"Failed to create secret: %v\", err)\n   100\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   101\u2192\t\t\t}\n   102\u2192\t\t\terr := SetOptionalRedisPasswordFromKubeConfig(ctx, kubeClient, tc.namespace, redisOptions)\n   103\u2192\t\t\tif tc.expectedErr != \"\" {\n   104\u2192\t\t\t\trequire.Error(t, err)\n   105\u2192\t\t\t\trequire.Contains(t, err.Error(), tc.expectedErr)\n   106\u2192\t\t\t} else {\n   107\u2192\t\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n   108\u2192\t\t\t}\n   109\u2192\t\t\trequire.Equal(t, tc.expectedPassword, redisOptions.Password)\n   110\u2192\t\t})\n   111\u2192\t}\n   112\u2192}\n   113\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

57\u2192// This empty test is here only for clarity, to conform to logs rbac tests structure in account. This exact usecase is covered in the TestAppLogs test\n    58\u2192func TestGetLogsAllowNoSwitch(t *testing.T) {\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192func TestGetLogsDenySwitchOn(t *testing.T) {\n    62\u2192\tSkipOnEnv(t, \"OPENSHIFT\")\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192\taccountFixture.Given(t).\n    65\u2192\t\tName(\"test\").\n    66\u2192\t\tWhen().\n    67\u2192\t\tCreate().\n    68\u2192\t\tLogin().\n    69\u2192\t\tSetPermissions([]fixture.ACL{\n    70\u2192\t\t\t{\n    71\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: \"applications\",\n    72\u2192\t\t\t\tAction:   \"create\",\n    73\u2192\t\t\t\tScope:    \"*\",\n    74\u2192\t\t\t},\n    75\u2192\t\t\t{\n    76\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: \"applications\",\n    77\u2192\t\t\t\tAction:   \"get\",\n    78\u2192\t\t\t\tScope:    \"*\",\n    79\u2192\t\t\t},\n    80\u2192\t\t\t{\n    81\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: \"applications\",\n    82\u2192\t\t\t\tAction:   \"sync\",\n    83\u2192\t\t\t\tScope:    \"*\",\n    84\u2192\t\t\t},\n    85\u2192\t\t\t{\n    86\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: \"projects\",\n    87\u2192\t\t\t\tAction:   \"get\",\n    88\u2192\t\t\t\tScope:    \"*\",\n    89\u2192\t\t\t},\n    90\u2192\t\t}, \"app-creator\")\n    91\u2192\n    92\u2192\tGivenWithSameState(t).\n    93\u2192\t\tPath(\"guestbook-logs\").\n    94\u2192\t\tWhen().\n    95\u2192\t\tCreateApp().\n    96\u2192\t\tSync().\n    97\u2192\t\tSetParamInSettingConfigMap(\"server.rbac.log.enforce.enable\", \"true\").\n    98\u2192\t\tThen().\n    99\u2192\t\tExpect(HealthIs(health.HealthStatusHealthy)).\n   100\u2192\t\tAnd(func(app *Application) {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 114. Bash @ -

136\u2192\t\t\t{\n   137\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: \"logs\",\n   138\u2192\t\t\t\tAction:   \"get\",\n   139\u2192\t\t\t\tScope:    \"*\",\n   140\u2192\t\t\t},\n   141\u2192\t\t}, \"app-creator\")\n   142\u2192\n   143\u2192\tGivenWithSameState(t).\n   144\u2192\t\tPath(\"guestbook-logs\").\n   145\u2192\t\tWhen().\n   146\u2192\t\tCreateApp().\n   147\u2192\t\tSync().\n   148\u2192\t\tSetParamInSettingConfigMap(\"server.rbac.log.enforce.enable\", \"true\").\n   149\u2192\t\tThen().\n   150\u2192\t\tExpect(HealthIs(health.HealthStatusHealthy)).\n   151\u2192\t\tAnd(func(app *Application) {\n   152\u2192\t\t\tout, err := RunCliWithRetry(appLogsRetryCount, \"app\", \"logs\", app.Name, \"--kind\", \"Deployment\", \"--group\", \"\", \"--name\", {\n   169\u2192\tSkipOnEnv(t, \"OPENSHIFT\")\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192\taccountFixture.Given(t).\n   172\u2192\t\tName(\"test\").\n   173\u2192\t\tWhen().\n   174\u2192\t\tCreate().\n   175\u2192\t\tLogin().\n   176\u2192\t\tSetPermissions([]fixture.ACL{\n   177\u2192\t\t\t{\n   178\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: \"applications\",\n   179\u2192\t\t\t\tAction:   \"create\",\n   180\u2192\t\t\t\tScope:    \"*\",\n   181\u2192\t\t\t},\n   182\u2192\t\t\t{\n   183\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: \"applications\",\n   184\u2192\t\t\t\tAction:   \"get\",\n   185\u2192\t\t\t\tScope: 191\u2192\t\t\t},\n   192\u2192\t\t\t{\n   193\u2192\t\t\t\tResource: \"projects\",\n   194\u2192\t\t\t\tAction:   \"get\",\n   195\u2192\t\t\t\tScope:    \"*\",\n   196\u2192\t\t\t},\n   197\u2192\t\t}, \"app-creator\")\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192\tGiven(t).\n   200\u2192\t\tPath(\"guestbook-logs\").\n   201\u2192\t\tWhen().\n   202\u2192\t\tCreateApp().\n   203\u2192\t\tSync().\n   204\u2192\t\tSetParamInSettingConfigMap(\"server.rbac.log.enforce.enable\", \"false\").\n   205\u2192\t\tThen().\n   206\u2192\t\tExpect(HealthIs(health.HealthStatusHealthy)).\n   207\u2192\t\tAnd(func(app *Application) {\n   208\u2192\t\t\tout, err := RunCliWithRetry(appLogsRetryCount, \"app\", \"logs\", app.Name, \"--kind\", \"Deployment\", \"--group\", \"\", \"--name\", \"guestbook-ui\")\n   209\u2192\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n   210\u2192\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, out, \"Hi\")\n   211\u2192\t\t}).\n   212\u2192\t\tAnd(func(app *Application) {\n   213\u2192\t\t\tout, err := RunCliWithRetry(appLogsRetryCount, \"app\", \"logs\", app.Name, \"--kind\", \"Pod\")\n   214\u2192\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n   215\u2192\t\t\tassert.Contains(t, out, \"Hi\")\n   216\u2192\t\t}).\n   217\u2192\t\tAnd(func(app *Application) {\n   218\u2192\t\t\tout, err := RunCliWithRetry(appLogsRetryCount, \"app\", \"logs\", app.Name, \"--kind\", \"Service\")\n   219\u2192\t\t\trequire.NoError(t, err)\n   220\u2192\t\t\tassert.NotContains(t, out, \"Hi\")\n   221\u2192\t\t})\n   222\u2192}\n   223\u2192\n   224\u2192func TestSyncToUnsignedCommit(t *testing.T) {\n   225\u2192\tSkipOnEnv(t, \"GPG\")\n   226\u2192\tGiven(t).\n   227\u2192\t\tProject(\"gpg\").\n   228\u2192\t\tPath(guestbookPath).\n   229\u2192\t\tWhen().\n   230\u2192\t\tIgnoreErrors().\n   231\u2192\t\tCreateApp().\n   232\u2192\t\tSync().\n   233\u2192\t\tThen().\n   234\u2192\t\tExpect(OperationPhaseIs(OperationError)).\n   235\u2192\t\tExpect(SyncStatusIs(SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync)).\n   236\u2192\t\tExpect(HealthIs(health.HealthStatusMissing))\n   237\u2192}\n   238\u2192\n   239\u2192func TestSyncToSignedCommitWithoutKnownKey(t *testing.T) {\n   240\u2192\tSkipOnEnv(t, \"GPG\")\n   241\u2192\tGiven(t).\n   242\u2192\t\tProject(\"gpg\").\n   243\u2192\t\tPath(guestbookPath).\n   244\u2192\t\tWhen().\n   245\u2192\t\tAddSignedFile(\"test.yaml\", \"null\").\n   246\u2192\t\tIgnoreErrors().\n   247\u2192\t\tCreateApp().\n   248\u2192\t\tSync().\n   249\u2192\t\tThen().\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192package app\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192import (\n     4\u2192\t\"context\"\n     5\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     6\u2192\t\"reflect\"\n     7\u2192\t\"regexp\"\n     8\u2192\t\"strings\"\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/gitops-engine/pkg/health\"\n    11\u2192\t. \"github.com/argoproj/gitops-engine/pkg/sync/common\"\n    12\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/api/core/v1\"\n    13\u2192\tapierr \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/api/errors\"\n    14\u2192\tmetav1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    15\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/fields\"\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192\t. \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/pkg/apis/application/v1alpha1\"\n    18\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/test/e2e/fixture\"\n    19\u2192)\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192type state = string\n    22\u2192\n    23\u2192const (\n    24\u2192\tfailed    = \"failed\"\n    25\u2192\tpending   = \"pending\"\n    26\u2192\tsucceeded = \"succeeded\"\n    27\u2192)\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192type Expectation func(c *Consequences) (state state, message string)\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192func OperationPhaseIs(expected OperationPhase) Expectation {\n    32\u2192\treturn func(c *Consequences) (state, string) {\n    33\u2192\t\toperationState := c.app().Status.OperationState\n    34\u2192\t\tactual := OperationRunning\n    35\u2192\t\tif operationState != nil {\n    36\u2192\t\t\tactual = operationState.Phase\n    37\u2192\t\t}\n    38\u2192\t\treturn simple(actual == expected, fmt.Sprintf(\"operation phase should be %s, is %s\", expected, actual))\n    39\u2192\t}\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192func OperationMessageContains(text string) Expectation {\n    43\u2192\treturn func(c *Consequences) (state, string) {\n    44\u2192\t\toperationState := c.app().Status.OperationState\n    45\u2192\t\tactual := \"\"\n    46\u2192\t\tif operationState != nil {\n    47\u2192\t\t\tactual = operationState.Message\n    48\u2192\t\t}\n    49\u2192\t\treturn simple(strings.Contains(actual, text), fmt.Sprintf(\"operation message should contains '%s', got: '%s'\", text, actual))\n    50\u2192\t}\n    51\u2192}\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192func simple(success bool, message string) (state, string) {\n    54\u2192\tif success {\n    55\u2192\t\treturn succeeded, message\n    56\u2192\t} else {\n    57\u2192\t\treturn pending, message\n    58\u2192\t}\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192func SyncStatusIs(expected SyncStatusCode) Expectation {\n    62\u2192\treturn func(c *Consequences) (state, string) {\n    63\u2192\t\tactual := c.app().Status.Sync.Status\n    64\u2192\t\treturn simple(actual == expected, fmt.Sprintf(\"sync status to be %s, is %s\", expected, actual))\n    65\u2192\t}\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192func Condition(conditionType ApplicationConditionType, conditionMessage string) Expectation {\n    69\u2192\treturn func(c *Consequences) (state, string) {\n    70\u2192\t\tgot := c.app().Status.Conditions\n    71\u2192\t\tmessage := fmt.Sprintf(\"condition {%s %s} in %v\", conditionType, conditionMessage, got)\n    72\u2192\t\tfor _, condition := range got {\n    73\u2192\t\t\tif conditionType == condition.Type && strings.Contains(condition.Message, conditionMessage) {\n    74\u2192\t\t\t\treturn succeeded, message\n    75\u2192\t\t\t}\n    76\u2192\t\t}\n    77\u2192\t\treturn pending, message\n    78\u2192\t}\n    79\u2192}\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192func NoConditions() Expectation {\n    82\u2192\treturn func(c *Consequences) (state, string) {\n    83\u2192\t\tmessage := \"no conditions\"\n    84\u2192\t\tif len(c.app().Status.Conditions) == 0 {\n    85\u2192\t\t\treturn succeeded, message\n    86\u2192\t\t}\n    87\u2192\t\treturn pending, message\n    88\u2192\t}\n    89\u2192}\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192func NoStatus() Expectation {\n    92\u2192\treturn func(c *Consequences) (state, string) {\n    93\u2192\t\tmessage := \"no status\"\n    94\u2192\t\tif reflect.ValueOf(c.app().Status).IsZero() {\n    95\u2192\t\t\treturn succeeded, message\n    96\u2192\t\t}\n    97\u2192\t\treturn pending, message\n    98\u2192\t}\n    99\u2192}\n   100\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

{
  "file_path": "/workspace/test/testutil.go"

Output
"     1\u2192package test\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192import (\n     4\u2192\t\"bytes\"\n     5\u2192\t\"context\"\n     6\u2192\t\"encoding/json\"\n     7\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     8\u2192\t\"net\"\n     9\u2192\t\"os\"\n    10\u2192\t\"testing\"\n    11\u2192\t\"time\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192\tlog \"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n    14\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\"\n    15\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/tools/cache\"\n    16\u2192\t\"sigs.k8s.io/yaml\"\n    17\u2192\n    18\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/pkg/apis/application/v1alpha1\"\n    19\u2192)\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192// StartInformer is a helper to start an informer, wait for its cache to sync and return a cancel func\n    22\u2192func StartInformer(informer cache.SharedIndexInformer) context.CancelFunc {\n    23\u2192\tctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n    24\u2192\tgo informer.Run(ctx.Done())\n    25\u2192\tif !cache.WaitForCacheSync(ctx.Done(), informer.HasSynced) {\n    26\u2192\t\tlog.Fatal(\"Timed out waiting for informer cache to sync\")\n    27\u2192\t}\n    28\u2192\treturn cancel\n    29\u2192}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192// GetFreePort finds an available free port on the OS\n    32\u2192func GetFreePort() (int, error) {\n    33\u2192\tln, err := net.Listen(\"tcp\", \"[::]:0\")\n    34\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    35\u2192\t\treturn 0, err\n    36\u2192\t}\n    37\u2192\treturn ln.Addr().(*net.TCPAddr).Port, ln.Close()\n    38\u2192}\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192// WaitForPortListen waits until the given address is listening on the port\n    41\u2192func WaitForPortListen(addr string, timeout time.Duration) error {\n    42\u2192\tticker := time.NewTicker(100 * time.Millisecond)\n    43\u2192\tdefer ticker.Stop()\n    44\u2192\ttimer := time.NewTimer(timeout)\n    45\u2192\tif timeout == 0 {\n    46\u2192\t\ttimer.Stop()\n    47\u2192\t} else {\n    48\u2192\t\tdefer timer.Stop()\n    49\u2192\t}\n    50\u2192\tfor {\n    51\u2192\t\tselect {\n    52\u2192\t\tcase <-ticker.C:\n    53\u2192\t\t\tif portIsOpen(addr) {\n    54\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil\n    55\u2192\t\t\t}\n    56\u2192\t\tcase <-timer.C:\n    57\u2192\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"timeout after %s\", timeout.String())\n    58\u2192\t\t}\n    59\u2192\t}\n    60\u2192}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192func portIsOpen(addr string) bool {\n    63\u2192\tconn, err := net.Dial(\"tcp\", addr)\n    64\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    65\u2192\t\treturn false\n    66\u2192\t}\n    67\u2192\t_ = conn.Close()\n    68\u2192\treturn true\n    69\u2192}\n    70\u2192\n    71\u2192// Read the contents of a file and returns it as string. Panics on error.\n    72\u2192func MustLoadFileToString(path string) string {\n    73\u2192\to, err := os.ReadFile(path)\n    74\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    75\u2192\t\tpanic(err.Error())\n    76\u2192\t}\n    77\u2192\treturn string(o)\n    78\u2192}\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192func YamlToUnstructured(yamlStr string) *unstructured.Unstructured {\n    81\u2192\tobj := make(map[string]interface{})\n    82\u2192\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yamlStr), &obj)\n    83\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    84\u2192\t\tpanic(err)\n    85\u2192\t}\n    86\u2192\treturn &unstructured.Unstructured{Object: obj}\n    87\u2192}\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192func YamlToApplication(yamlStr string) *v1alpha1.Application {\n    90\u2192\tapp := v1alpha1.Application{}\n    91\u2192\terr := yaml.Unmarshal([]byte(yamlStr), &app)\n    92\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    93\u2192\t\tpanic(err)\n    94\u2192\t}\n    95\u2192\treturn &app\n    96\u2192}\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192// ToMap converts any object to a map[string]interface{}\n    99\u2192func ToMap(obj interface{}) map[string]interface{} {\n   100\u2192\tdata, err := json.Marshal(obj)\n   101\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   102\u2192\t\tpanic(err)\n   103\u2192\t}\n   104\u2192\tvar res map[string]interface{}\n   105\u2192\terr = json.Unmarshal(data, &res)\n   106\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   107\u2192\t\tpanic(err)\n   108\u2192\t}\n   109\u2192\treturn res\n   110\u2192}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192// GetTestDir will return the full directory path of the\n   113\u2192// calling test file.\n   114\u2192func GetTestDir(t *testing.T) string {\n   115\u2192\tt.Helper()\n   116\u2192\tcwd, err := os.Getwd()\n   117\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   118\u2192\t\tt.Fatal(err)\n   119\u2192\t}\n   120\u2192\treturn cwd\n   121\u2192}\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192// CaptureLogEntries captures log entries generated by the logger and return it as string\n   124\u2192func CaptureLogEntries(run func()) string {\n   125\u2192\tf := log.StandardLogger().Formatter\n   126\u2192\tlog.SetFormatter(&log.TextFormatter{DisableColors: true})\n   127\u2192\tdefer log.SetFormatter(f)\n   128\u2192\toutput := bytes.NewBuffer(nil)\n   129\u2192\tlog.SetOutput(output)\n   130\u2192\tlog.SetLevel(log.DebugLevel)\n   131\u2192\tdefer log.SetOutput(os.Stdout)\n   132\u2192\n   133\u2192\trun()\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192\treturn output.String()\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
128. Bash @ -

{
  "command": "find /workspace -path \"*/testdata/*.yaml\" -o -path \"*/testdata/*.yml\" -o -path \"*/testdata/*.json\" | head -20"

NewControllerRevision() *unstructured.Unstructured {\n    30\u2192\treturn testing.Unstructured(`\n    31\u2192kind: ControllerRevision\n    32\u2192apiVersion: metacontroller.k8s.io/v1alpha1\n    33\u2192metadata:\n    34\u2192  labels:\n    35\u2192    app: nginx\n    36\u2192    controller.kubernetes.io/hash: c7cd8d57f\n    37\u2192  name: web-c7cd8d57f\n    38\u2192  namespace: statefulset\n    39\u2192revision: 2\n    40\u2192`)\n    41\u2192}\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192var DeploymentManifest = `\n    44\u2192{\n    45\u2192  \"apiVersion\": \"apps/v1\",\n    46\u2192  \"kind\": \"Deployment\",\n    47\u2192  \"metadata\": {\n    48\u2192    \"name\": \"nginx-deployment\",\n    49\u2192    \"labels\": {\n    50\u2192      \"app\": \"nginx\"\n    51\u2192    }\n    52\u2192  },\n    53\u2192  \"spec\": {\n    54\u2192    \"replicas\": 3,\n    55\u2192    \"selector\": {\n    56\u2192      \"matchLabels\": {\n    57\u2192        \"app\": \"nginx\"\n    58\u2192      }\n    59\u2192    },\n    60\u2192    \"template\": {\n    61\u2192      \"metadata\": {\n    62\u2192        \"labels\": {\n    63\u2192          \"app\": \"nginx\"\n    64\u2192        }\n    65\u2192      },\n    66\u2192      \"spec\": {\n    67\u2192        \"containers\": [\n    68\u2192          {\n    69\u2192            \"name\": \"nginx\",\n    70\u2192            \"image\": \"nginx:1.15.4\",\n    71\u2192            \"ports\": [\n    72\u2192              {\n    73\u2192                \"containerPort\": 80\n    74\u2192              }\n    75\u2192            ]\n    76\u2192          }\n    77\u2192        ]\n    78\u2192      }\n    79\u2192    }\n    80\u2192  }\n    81\u2192}\n    82\u2192`\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192func NewDeployment() *unstructured.Unstructured {\n    85\u2192\treturn testing.Unstructured(DeploymentManifest)\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192var ConfigMapManifest = `\n    89\u2192{\t\n    90\u2192  \"apiVersion\": \"v1\",\n    91\u2192  \"kind\": \"ConfigMap\",\n    92\u2192  \"metadata\": {\n    93\u2192    \"name\": \"my-configmap\",\n    94\u2192  },\n    95\u2192  \"data\": {\n    96\u2192    \"config.yaml\": \"auth: token\\nconfig:field\"\n    97\u2192  }\n    98\u2192}`\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192func NewConfigMap() *unstructured.Unstructured {\n   101\u2192\treturn testing.Unstructured(ConfigMapManifest)\n   102\u2192}\n   103\u2192\n   104\u2192func NewFakeConfigMap() *apiv1.ConfigMap {\n   105\u2192\tcm := apiv1.ConfigMap{\n   106\u2192\t\tTypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{\n   107\u2192\t\t\tKind:       \"ConfigMap\",\n   108\u2192\t\t\tAPIVersion: \"v1\",\n   109\u2192\t\t},\n   110\u2192\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n   111\u2192\t\t\tName:      common.ArgoCDConfigMapName,\n   112\u2192\t\t\tNamespace: FakeArgoCDNamespace,\n   113\u2192\t\t\tLabels: map[string]string{\n   114\u2192\t\t\t\t\"app.kubernetes.io/part-of\": \"argocd\",\n   115\u2192\t\t\t},\n   116\u2192\t\t},\n   117\u2192\t\tData: make(map[string]string),\n   118\u2192\t}\n   119\u2192\treturn &cm\n   120\u2192}\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192func NewFakeSecret(policy ...string) *apiv1.Secret {\n   123\u2192\tsecret := apiv1.Secret{\n   124\u2192\t\tTypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{\n   125\u2192\t\t\tKind:       \"Secret\",\n   126\u2192\t\t\tAPIVersion: \"v1\",\n   127\u2192\t\t},\n   128\u2192\t\tObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{\n   129\u2192\t\t\tName:      common.ArgoCDSecretName,\n   130\u2192\t\t\tNamespace: FakeArgoCDNamespace,\n   131\u2192\t\t},\n   132\u2192\t\tData: map[string][]byte{\n   133\u2192\t\t\t\"admin.password\":   []byte(\"test\"),\n   134\u2192\t\t\t\"server.secretkey\": []byte(\"test\"),\n   135\u2192\t\t},\n   136\u2192\t}\n   137\u2192\treturn &secret\n   138\u2192}\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192type interfaceLister struct {\n   141\u2192\tappProjects appclient.AppProjectInterface\n   142\u2192}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192func (l interfaceLister) List(selector labels.Selector) ([]*v1alpha1.AppProject, error) {\n   145\u2192\tres, err := l.appProjects.List(context.Background(), metav1.ListOptions{LabelSelector: selector.String()})\n   146\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   147\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n   148\u2192\t}\n   149\u2192\titems := make([]*v1alpha1.AppProject, len(res.Items))\n   150\u2192\tfor i := range res.Items {\n   151\u2192\t\titems[i] = &res.Items[i]\n   152\u2192\t}\n   153\u2192\treturn items, nil\n   154\u2192}\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192func (l interfaceLister) Get(name string) (*v1alpha1.AppProject, error) {\n   157\u2192\treturn l.appProjects.Get(context.Background(), name, metav1.GetOptions{})\n   158\u2192}\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192func NewFakeProjListerFromInterface(appProjects appclient.AppProjectInterface) applister.AppProjectNamespaceLister {\n   161\u2192\treturn &interfaceLister{appProjects: appProjects}\n   162\u2192}\n   163\u2192\n   164\u2192func NewFakeProjLister(objects ...runtime.Object) applister.AppProjectNamespaceLister {\n   165\u2192\tfakeAppClientset := apps.NewSimpleClientset(objects...)\n   166\u2192\tfactory := appinformer.NewSharedInformerFactoryWithOptions(fakeAppClientset, 0, appinformer.WithNamespace(\"\"), appinformer.WithTweakListOptions(func(options *metav1.ListOptions) {}))\n   167\u2192\tprojInformer := factory.Argoproj().V1alpha1().AppProjects().Informer()\n   168\u2192\tcancel := StartInformer(projInformer)\n   169\u2192\tdefer cancel()\n   170\u2192\treturn factory.Argoproj().V1alpha1().AppProjects().Lister().AppProjects(FakeArgoCDNamespace)\n   171\u2192}\n   172\u2192\n   173\u2192func NewInMemoryRedis() (*redis.Client, func()) {\n   174\u2192\tmr, err := miniredis.Run()\n   175\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   176\u2192\t\tpanic(err)\n   177\u2192\t}\n   178\u2192\treturn redis.NewClient(&redis.Options{Addr: mr.Addr()}), mr.Close\n   179\u2192}\n   180\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
