appKey, err)\n  1562\u2192\t\treturn\n  1563\u2192\t}\n  1564\u2192\tif !exists {\n  1565\u2192\t\t// This happens after app was deleted, but the work queue still had an entry for it.\n  1566\u2192\t\treturn\n  1567\u2192\t}\n  1568\u2192\torigApp, ok := obj.(*appv1.Application)\n  1569\u2192\tif !ok {\n  1570\u2192\t\tlog.Warnf(\"Key '%s' in index is not an application\", appKey)\n  1571\u2192\t\treturn\n  1572\u2192\t}\n  1573\u2192\torigApp = origApp.DeepCopy()\n  1574\u2192\tneedRefresh, refreshType, comparisonLevel := ctrl.needRefreshAppStatus(origApp, ctrl.statusRefreshTimeout, ctrl.statusHardRefreshTimeout)\n  1575\u2192\n  1576\u2192\tif !needRefresh {\n  1577\u2192\t\treturn\n  1578\u2192\t}\n  1579\u2192\tapp := origApp.DeepCopy()\n  1580\u2192\tlogCtx := getAppLog(app).WithFields(log.Fields{\n  1581\u2192\t\t\"comparison-level\": comparisonLevel,\n  1582\u2192\t\t\"dest-server\":      origApp.Spec.Destination.Server,\n  1583\u2192\t\t\"dest-name\":        origApp.Spec.Destination.Name,\n  1584\u2192\t\t\"dest-namespace\":   origApp.Spec.Destination.Namespace,\n  1585\u2192\t})\n  1586\u2192\n  1587\u2192\tstartTime := time.Now()\n  1588\u2192\tts := stats.NewTimingStats()\n  1589\u2192\tdefer func() {\n  1590\u2192\t\treconcileDuration := time.Since(startTime)\n  1591\u2192\t\tctrl.metricsServer.IncReconcile(origApp, reconcileDuration)\n  1592\u2192\t\tfor k, v := range ts.Timings() {\n  1593\u2192\t\t\tlogCtx = logCtx.WithField(k, v.Milliseconds())\n  1594\u2192\t\t}\n  1595\u2192\t\tlogCtx.WithFields(log.Fields{\n  1596\u2192\t\t\t\"time_ms\":  reconcileDuration.Milliseconds(),\n  1597\u2192\t\t\t\"patch_ms\": patchMs.Milliseconds(),\n  1598\u2192\t\t\t\"setop_ms\": setOpMs.Milliseconds(),\n  1599\u2192\t\t}).Info(\"Reconciliation completed\")\n  1600\u2192\t}()\n  1601\u2192\n  1602\u2192\tif comparisonLevel == ComparisonWithNothing {\n  1603\u2192\t\tmanagedResources := make([]*appv1.ResourceDiff, 0)\n  1604\u2192\t\tif err := ctrl.cache.GetAppManagedResources(app.InstanceName(ctrl.namespace), &managedResources); err != nil {\n  1605\u2192\t\t\tlogCtx.Warnf(\"Failed to get cached managed resources for tree reconciliation, fall back to full reconciliation\")\n  1606\u2192\t\t} else {\n  1607\u2192\t\t\tvar tree *appv1.ApplicationTree\n  1608\u2192\t\t\tif tree, err = ctrl.getResourceTree(app, managedResources); err == nil {\n  1609\u2192\t\t\t\tapp.Status.Summary = tree.GetSummary(app)\n  1610\u2192\t\t\t\tif err := ctrl.cache.SetAppResourcesTree(app.InstanceName(ctrl.namespace), tree); err != nil {\n  1611\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlogCtx.Errorf(\"Failed to cache resources tree: %v\", err)\n  1612\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn\n  1613\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n  1614\u2192\t\t\t}\n  1615\u2192\n  1616\u2192\t\t\tpatchMs = ctrl.persistAppStatus(origApp, &app.Status)\n  1617\u2192\t\t\treturn\n  1618\u2192\t\t}\n  1619\u2192\t}\n  1620\u2192\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"comparison_with_nothing_ms\")\n  1621\u2192\n  1622\u2192\tproject, hasErrors := ctrl.refreshAppConditions(app)\n  1623\u2192\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"refresh_app_conditions_ms\")\n  1624\u2192\tif hasErrors {\n  1625\u2192\t\tapp.Status.Sync.Status = appv1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown\n  1626\u2192\t\tapp.Status.Health.Status = health.HealthStatusUnknown\n  1627\u2192\t\tpatchMs = ctrl.persistAppStatus(origApp, &app.Status)\n  1628\u2192\n  1629\u2192\t\tif err := ctrl.cache.SetAppResourcesTree(app.InstanceName(ctrl.namespace), &appv1.ApplicationTree{}); err != nil {\n  1630\u2192\t\t\tlogCtx.Warnf(\"failed to set app resource tree: %v\", err)\n  1631\u2192\t\t}\n  1632\u2192\t\tif err := ctrl.cache.SetAppManagedResources(app.InstanceName(ctrl.namespace), nil); err != nil {\n  1633\u2192\t\t\tlogCtx.Warnf(\"failed to set app managed resources tree: %v\", err)\n  1634\u2192\t\t}\n  1635\u2192\t\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"process_refresh_app_conditions_errors_ms\")\n  1636\u2192\t\treturn\n  1637\u2192\t}\n  1638\u2192\n  1639\u2192\tvar localManifests []string\n  1640\u2192\tif opState := app.Status.OperationState; opState != nil && opState.Operation.Sync != nil {\n  1641\u2192\t\tlocalManifests = opState.Operation.Sync.Manifests\n  1642\u2192\t}\n  1643\u2192\n  1644\u2192\trevisions := make([]string, 0)\n  1645\u2192\tsources := make([]appv1.ApplicationSource, 0)\n  1646\u2192\n  1647\u2192\thasMultipleSources := app.Spec.HasMultipleSources()\n  1648\u2192\n  1649\u2192\t// If we have multiple sources, we use all the sources under `sources` field and ignore source under `source` field.\n  1650\u2192\t// else we use the source under the source field.\n  1651\u2192\tif hasMultipleSources {\n  1652\u2192\t\tfor _, source := range app.Spec.Sources {\n  1653\u2192\t\t\t// We do not perform any filtering of duplicate sources.\n  1654\u2192\t\t\t// Argo CD will apply and update the resources generated from the sources automatically\n  1655\u2192\t\t\t// based on the order in which manifests were generated\n  1656\u2192\t\t\tsources = append(sources, source)\n  1657\u2192\t\t\trevisions = append(revisions, source.TargetRevision)\n  1658\u2192\t\t}\n  1659\u2192\t\tif comparisonLevel == CompareWithRecent {\n  1660\u2192\t\t\trevisions = app.Status.Sync.Revisions\n  1661\u2192\t\t}\n  1662\u2192\t} else {\n  1663\u2192\t\trevision := app.Spec.GetSource().TargetRevision\n  1664\u2192\t\tif comparisonLevel == CompareWithRecent {\n  1665\u2192\t\t\trevision = app.Status.Sync.Revision\n  1666\u2192\t\t}\n  1667\u2192\t\trevisions = append(revisions, revision)\n  1668\u2192\t\tsources = append(sources, app.Spec.GetSource())\n  1669\u2192\t}\n  1670\u2192\tnow := metav1.Now()\n  1671\u2192\n  1672\u2192\tcompareResult, err := ctrl.appStateManager.CompareAppState(app, project, revisions, sources,\n  1673\u2192\t\trefreshType == appv1.RefreshTypeHard,\n  1674\u2192\t\tcomparisonLevel == CompareWithLatestForceResolve, localManifests, hasMultipleSources, false)\n  1675\u2192\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"compare_app_state_ms\")\n  1676\u2192\n  1677\u2192\tif goerrors.Is(err, CompareStateRepoError) {\n  1678\u2192\t\tlogCtx.Warnf(\"Ignoring temporary failed attempt to compare app state against repo: %v\", err)\n  1679\u2192\t\treturn // short circuit if git error is encountered\n  1680\u2192\t}\n  1681\u2192\n  1682\u2192\tfor k, v := range compareResult.timings {\n  1683\u2192\t\tlogCtx = logCtx.WithField(k, v.Milliseconds())\n  1684\u2192\t}\n  1685\u2192\n  1686\u2192\tctrl.normalizeApplication(origApp, app)\n  1687\u2192\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"normalize_application_ms\")\n  1688\u2192\n  1689\u2192\ttree, err := ctrl.setAppManagedResources(app, compareResult)\n  1690\u2192\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"set_app_managed_resources_ms\")\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. errors.New(\"failed to get repo objects\")\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192type resourceInfoProviderStub struct{}\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192func (r *resourceInfoProviderStub) IsNamespaced(_ schema.GroupKind) (bool, error) {\n    53\u2192\treturn false, nil\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192type managedResource struct {\n    57\u2192\tTarget          *unstructured.Unstructured\n    58\u2192\tLive            *unstructured.Unstructured\n    59\u2192\tDiff            diff.DiffResult\n    60\u2192\tGroup           string\n    61\u2192\tVersion         string\n    62\u2192\tKind            string\n    63\u2192\tNamespace       string\n    64\u2192\tName            string\n    65\u2192\tHook            bool\n    66\u2192\tResourceVersion string\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// AppStateManager defines methods which allow to compare application spec and actual application state.\n    70\u2192type AppStateManager interface {\n    71\u2192\tCompareAppState(app *v1alpha1.Application, project *v1alpha1.AppProject, revisions []string, sources []v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, noCache bool, noRevisionCache bool, localObjects []string, hasMultipleSources bool, rollback bool) (*comparisonResult, error)\n    72\u2192\tSyncAppState(app *v1alpha1.Application, state *v1alpha1.OperationState)\n    73\u2192\tGetRepoObjs(app *v1alpha1.Application, sources []v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, appLabelKey string, revisions []string, noCache, noRevisionCache, verifySignature bool, proj *v1alpha1.AppProject, rollback bool) ([]*unstructured.Unstructured, []*apiclient.ManifestResponse, bool, error)\n    74\u2192}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192// comparisonResult holds the state of an application after the reconciliation\n    77\u2192type comparisonResult struct {\n    78\u2192\tsyncStatus           *v1alpha1.SyncStatus\n    79\u2192\thealthStatus         *v1alpha1.HealthStatus\n    80\u2192\tresources            []v1alpha1.ResourceStatus\n    81\u2192\tmanagedResources     []managedResource\n    82\u2192\treconciliationResult sync.ReconciliationResult\n    83\u2192\tdiffConfig           argodiff.DiffConfig\n    84\u2192\tappSourceType        v1alpha1.ApplicationSourceType\n    85\u2192\t// appSourceTypes stores the SourceType for each application source under sources field\n    86\u2192\tappSourceTypes []v1alpha1.ApplicationSourceType\n    87\u2192\t// timings maps phases of comparison to the duration it took to complete (for statistical purposes)\n    88\u2192\ttimings            map[string]time.Duration\n    89\u2192\tdiffResultList     *diff.DiffResultList\n    90\u2192\thasPostDeleteHooks bool\n    91\u2192\trevisionUpdated    bool\n    92\u2192}\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192func (res *comparisonResult) GetSyncStatus() *v1alpha1.SyncStatus {\n    95\u2192\treturn res.syncStatus\n    96\u2192}\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192func (res *comparisonResult) GetHealthStatus() *v1alpha1.HealthStatus {\n    99\u2192\treturn res.healthStatus\n   100\u2192}\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192// appStateManager allows to compare applications to git\n   103\u2192type appStateManager struct {\n   104\u2192\tmetricsServer         *metrics.MetricsServer\n   105\u2192\tdb                    db.ArgoDB\n   106\u2192\tsettingsMgr           *settings.SettingsManager\n   107\u2192\tappclientset          appclientset.Interface\n   108\u2192\tprojInformer          cache.SharedIndexInformer\n   109\u2192\tkubectl               kubeutil.Kubectl\n   110\u2192\trepoClientset         apiclient.Clientset\n   111\u2192\tliveStateCache        statecache.LiveStateCache\n   112\u2192\tcache                 *appstatecache.Cache\n   113\u2192\tnamespace             string\n   114\u2192\tstatusRefreshTimeout  time.Duration\n   115\u2192\tresourceTracking      argo.ResourceTracking\n   116\u2192\tpersistResourceHealth bool\n   117\u2192\trepoErrorCache        goSync.Map\n   118\u2192\trepoErrorGracePeriod  time.Duration\n   119\u2192\tserverSideDiff        bool\n   120\u2192\tignoreNormalizerOpts  normalizers.IgnoreNormalizerOpts\n   121\u2192}\n   122\u2192\n   123\u2192// GetRepoObjs will generate the manifests for the given application delegating the\n   124\u2192// task to the repo-server. It returns the list of generated manifests as unstructured\n   125\u2192// objects. It also returns the full response from all calls to the repo server as the\n   126\u2192// second argument.\n   127\u2192func (m *appStateManager) GetRepoObjs(app *v1alpha1.Application, sources []v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, appLabelKey string, revisions []string, noCache, noRevisionCache, verifySignature bool, proj *v1alpha1.AppProject, rollback bool) ([]*unstructured.Unstructured, []*apiclient.ManifestResponse, bool, error) {\n   128\u2192\tts := stats.NewTimingStats()\n   129\u2192\thelmRepos, err := m.db.ListHelmRepositories(context.Background())\n   130\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   131\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to list Helm repositories: %w\", err)\n   132\u2192\t}\n   133\u2192\tpermittedHelmRepos, err := argo.GetPermittedRepos(proj, helmRepos)\n   134\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   135\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get permitted Helm repositories for project %q: %w\", proj.Name, err)\n   136\u2192\t}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"repo_ms\")\n   139\u2192\thelmRepositoryCredentials, err := m.db.GetAllHelmRepositoryCredentials(context.Background())\n   140\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   141\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get Helm credentials: %w\", err)\n   142\u2192\t}\n   143\u2192\tpermittedHelmCredentials, err := argo.GetPermittedReposCredentials(proj, helmRepositoryCredentials)\n   144\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   145\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get permitted Helm credentials for project %q: %w\", proj.Name, err)\n   146\u2192\t}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192\tenabledSourceTypes, err := m.settingsMgr.GetEnabledSourceTypes()\n   149\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   150\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, false, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get enabled source types: %w\", err)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"   429\u2192func (m *appStateManager) CompareAppState(app *v1alpha1.Application, project *v1alpha1.AppProject, revisions []string, sources []v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, noCache bool, noRevisionCache bool, localManifests []string, hasMultipleSources bool, rollback bool) (*comparisonResult, error) {\n   430\u2192\tts := stats.NewTimingStats()\n   431\u2192\tappLabelKey, resourceOverrides, resFilter, installationID, err := m.getComparisonSettings()\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"settings_ms\")\n   434\u2192\n   435\u2192\t// return unknown comparison result if basic comparison settings cannot be loaded\n   436\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   437\u2192\t\tif hasMultipleSources {\n   438\u2192\t\t\treturn &comparisonResult{\n   439\u2192\t\t\t\tsyncStatus: &v1alpha1.SyncStatus{\n   440\u2192\t\t\t\t\tComparedTo: v1alpha1.ComparedTo{Destination: app.Spec.Destination, Sources: sources, IgnoreDifferences: app.Spec.IgnoreDifferences},\n   441\u2192\t\t\t\t\tStatus:     v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown,\n   442\u2192\t\t\t\t\tRevisions:  revisions,\n   443\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   444\u2192\t\t\t\thealthStatus: &v1alpha1.HealthStatus{Status: health.HealthStatusUnknown},\n   445\u2192\t\t\t}, nil\n   446\u2192\t\t} else {\n   447\u2192\t\t\treturn &comparisonResult{\n   448\u2192\t\t\t\tsyncStatus: &v1alpha1.SyncStatus{\n   449\u2192\t\t\t\t\tComparedTo: v1alpha1.ComparedTo{Source: sources[0], Destination: app.Spec.Destination, IgnoreDifferences: app.Spec.IgnoreDifferences},\n   450\u2192\t\t\t\t\tStatus:     v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown,\n   451\u2192\t\t\t\t\tRevision:   revisions[0],\n   452\u2192\t\t\t\t},\n   453\u2192\t\t\t\thealthStatus: &v1alpha1.HealthStatus{Status: health.HealthStatusUnknown},\n   454\u2192\t\t\t}, nil\n   455\u2192\t\t}\n   456\u2192\t}\n   457\u2192\n   458\u2192\t// When signature keys are defined in the project spec, we need to verify the signature on the Git revision\n   459\u2192\tverifySignature := false\n   460\u2192\tif len(project.Spec.SignatureKeys) > 0 && gpg.IsGPGEnabled() {\n   461\u2192\t\tverifySignature = true\n   462\u2192\t}\n   463\u2192\n   464\u2192\t// do best effort loading live and target state to present as much information about app state as possible\n   465\u2192\tfailedToLoadObjs := false\n   466\u2192\tconditions := make([]v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition, 0)\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192\tlogCtx := log.WithField(\"application\", app.QualifiedName())\n   469\u2192\tlogCtx.Infof(\"Comparing app state (cluster: %s, namespace: %s)\", app.Spec.Destination.Server, app.Spec.Destination.Namespace)\n   470\u2192\n   471\u2192\tvar targetObjs []*unstructured.Unstructured\n   472\u2192\tnow := metav1.Now()\n   473\u2192\n   474\u2192\tvar manifestInfos []*apiclient.ManifestResponse\n   475\u2192\ttargetNsExists := false\n   476\u2192\n   477\u2192\tvar revisionUpdated bool\n   478\u2192\n   479\u2192\tif len(localManifests) == 0 {\n   480\u2192\t\t// If the length of revisions is not same as the length of sources,\n   481\u2192\t\t// we take the revisions from the sources directly for all the sources.\n   482\u2192\t\tif len(revisions) != len(sources) {\n   483\u2192\t\t\trevisions = make([]string, 0)\n   484\u2192\t\t\tfor _, source := range sources {\n   485\u2192\t\t\t\trevisions = append(revisions, source.TargetRevision)\n   486\u2192\t\t\t}\n   487\u2192\t\t}\n   488\u2192\n   489\u2192\t\ttargetObjs, manifestInfos, revisionUpdated, err = m.GetRepoObjs(app, sources, appLabelKey, revisions, noCache, noRevisionCache, verifySignature, project, rollback)\n   490\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   491\u2192\t\t\ttargetObjs = make([]*unstructured.Unstructured, 0)\n   492\u2192\t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load target state: %s\", err.Error())\n   493\u2192\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   494\u2192\t\t\tif firstSeen, ok := m.repoErrorCache.Load(app.Name); ok {\n   495\u2192\t\t\t\tif time.Since(firstSeen.(time.Time)) <= m.repoErrorGracePeriod && !noRevisionCache {\n   496\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// if first seen is less than grace period and it's not a Level 3 comparison,\n   497\u2192\t\t\t\t\t// ignore error and short circuit\n   498\u2192\t\t\t\t\tlogCtx.Debugf(\"Ignoring repo error %v, already encountered error in grace period\", err.Error())\n   499\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn nil, CompareStateRepoError\n   500\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   501\u2192\t\t\t} else if !noRevisionCache {\n   502\u2192\t\t\t\tlogCtx.Debugf(\"Ignoring repo error %v, new occurrence\", err.Error())\n   503\u2192\t\t\t\tm.repoErrorCache.Store(app.Name, time.Now())\n   504\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, CompareStateRepoError\n   505\u2192\t\t\t}\n   506\u2192\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   507\u2192\t\t} else {\n   508\u2192\t\t\tm.repoErrorCache.Delete(app.Name)\n   509\u2192\t\t}\n   510\u2192\t} else {\n   511\u2192\t\t// Prevent applying local manifests for now when signature verification is enabled\n   512\u2192\t\t// This is also enforced on API level, but as a last resort, we also enforce it here\n   513\u2192\t\tif gpg.IsGPGEnabled() && verifySignature {\n   514\u2192\t\t\tmsg := \"Cannot use local manifests when signature verification is required\"\n   515\u2192\t\t\ttargetObjs = make([]*unstructured.Unstructured, 0)\n   516\u2192\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   517\u2192\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   518\u2192\t\t} else {\n   519\u2192\t\t\ttargetObjs, err = unmarshalManifests(localManifests)\n   520\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   521\u2192\t\t\t\ttargetObjs = make([]*unstructured.Unstructured, 0)\n   522\u2192\t\t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load local manifests: %s\", err.Error())\n   523\u2192\t\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   524\u2192\t\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   525\u2192\t\t\t}\n   526\u2192\t\t}\n   527\u2192\t\t// empty out manifestInfoMap\n   528\u2192\t\tmanifestInfos = make([]*apiclient.ManifestResponse, 0)\n   529\u2192\t}\n   530\u2192\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"git_ms\")\n   531\u2192\n   532\u2192\tvar infoProvider kubeutil.ResourceInfoProvider\n   533\u2192\tinfoProvider, err = m.liveStateCache.GetClusterCache(app.Spec.Destination.Server)\n   534\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   535\u2192\t\tinfoProvider = &resourceInfoProviderStub{}\n   536\u2192\t}\n   537\u2192\ttargetObjs, dedupConditions, err := DeduplicateTargetObjects(app.Spec.Destination.Namespace, targetObjs, infoProvider)\n   538\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   539\u2192\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to deduplicate target state: %s\", err.Error())\n   540\u2192\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   541\u2192\t}\n   542\u2192\tconditions = append(conditions, dedupConditions...)\n   543\u2192\tfor i := len(targetObjs) - 1; i >= 0; i-- {\n   544\u2192\t\ttargetObj := targetObjs[i]\n   545\u2192\t\tgvk := targetObj.GroupVersionKind()\n   546\u2192\t\tif resFilter.IsExcludedResource(gvk.Group, gvk.Kind, app.Spec.Destination.Server) {\n   547\u2192\t\t\ttargetObjs = append(targetObjs[:i], targetObjs[i+1:]...)\n   548\u2192\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{\n   549\u2192\t\t\t\tType:               v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionExcludedResourceWarning,\n   550\u2192\t\t\t\tMessage:            fmt.Sprintf(\"Resource %s/%s %s is excluded in the settings\", gvk.Group, gvk.Kind, targetObj.GetName()),\n   551\u2192\t\t\t\tLastTransitionTime: &now,\n   552\u2192\t\t\t})\n   553\u2192\t\t}\n   554\u2192\n   555\u2192\t\t// If we reach this path, this means that a namespace has been both defined in Git, as well in the\n   556\u2192\t\t// application's managedNamespaceMetadata. We want to ensure that this manifest is the one being used instead\n   557\u2192\t\t// of what is present in managedNamespaceMetadata.\n   558\u2192\t\tif isManagedNamespace(targetObj, app) {\n   559\u2192\t\t\ttargetNsExists = true\n   560\u2192\t\t}\n   561\u2192\t}\n   562\u2192\tts.AddCheckpoint(\"dedup_ms\")\n   563\u2192\n   564\u2192\tliveObjByKey, err := m.liveStateCache.GetManagedLiveObjs(app, targetObjs)\n   565\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   566\u2192\t\tliveObjByKey = make(map[kubeutil.ResourceKey]*unstructured.Unstructured)\n   567\u2192\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load live state: %s\", err.Error())\n   568\u2192\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   569\u2192\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   570\u2192\t}\n   571\u2192\n   572\u2192\tlogCtx.Debugf(\"Retrieved live manifests\")\n   573\u2192\n   574\u2192\t// filter out all resources which are not permitted in the application project\n   575\u2192\tfor k, v := range liveObjByKey {\n   576\u2192\t\tpermitted, err := project.IsLiveResourcePermitted(v, app.Spec.Destination.Server, app.Spec.Destination.Name, func(project string) ([]*v1alpha1.Cluster, error) {\n   577\u2192\t\t\tclusters, err := m.db.GetProjectClusters(context.TODO(), project)\n   578\u2192\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   579\u2192\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to get clusters for project %q: %w\", project, err)\n   580\u2192\t\t\t}\n   581\u2192\t\t\treturn clusters, nil\n   582\u2192\t\t})\n   583\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   584\u2192\t\t\tmsg := fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to check if live resource %q is permitted in project %q: %s\", k.String(), app.Spec.Project, err.Error())\n   585\u2192\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: msg, LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   586\u2192\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   587\u2192\t\t\tcontinue\n   588\u2192\t\t}\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192\t\tif !permitted {\n   591\u2192\t\t\tdelete(liveObjByKey, k)\n   592\u2192\t\t}\n   593\u2192\t}\n   594\u2192\n   595\u2192\ttrackingMethod := argo.GetTrackingMethod(m.settingsMgr)\n   596\u2192\n   597\u2192\tfor _, liveObj := range liveObjByKey {\n   598\u2192\t\tif liveObj != nil {\n   599\u2192\t\t\tappInstanceName := m.resourceTracking.GetAppName(liveObj, appLabelKey, trackingMethod, installationID)\n   600\u2192\t\t\tif appInstanceName != \"\" && appInstanceName != app.InstanceName(m.namespace) {\n   601\u2192\t\t\t\tfqInstanceName := strings.ReplaceAll(appInstanceName, \"_\", \"/\")\n   602\u2192\t\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{\n   603\u2192\t\t\t\t\tType:               v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionSharedResourceWarning,\n   604\u2192\t\t\t\t\tMessage:            fmt.Sprintf(\"%s/%s is part of applications %s and %s\", liveObj.GetKind(), liveObj.GetName(), app.QualifiedName(), fqInstanceName),\n   605\u2192\t\t\t\t\tLastTransitionTime: &now,\n   606\u2192\t\t\t\t})\n   607\u2192\t\t\t}\n   608\u2192\n   609\u2192\t\t\t// For the case when a namespace is managed with `managedNamespaceMetadata` AND it has resource tracking\n   610\u2192\t\t\t// enabled (e.g. someone manually adds resource tracking labels or annotations), we need to do some\n   611\u2192\t\t\t// bookkeeping in order to prevent the managed namespace from being pruned.\n   612\u2192\t\t\t//\n   613\u2192\t\t\t// Live namespaces which are managed namespaces (i.e. application namespaces which are managed with\n   614\u2192\t\t\t// CreateNamespace=true and has non-nil managedNamespaceMetadata) will (usually) not have a corresponding\n   615\u2192\t\t\t// entry in source control. In order for the namespace not to risk being pruned, we'll need to generate a\n   616\u2192\t\t\t// namespace which we can compare the live namespace with. For that, we'll do the same as is done in\n   617\u2192\t\t\t// gitops-engine, the difference here being that we create a managed namespace which is only used for comparison.\n   618\u2192\t\t\t//\n   619\u2192\t\t\t// targetNsExists == true implies that it already exists as a target, so no need to add the namespace to the\n   620\u2192\t\t\t// targetObjs array.\n   621\u2192\t\t\tif isManagedNamespace(liveObj, app) && !targetNsExists {\n   622\u2192\t\t\t\tnsSpec := &v1.Namespace{TypeMeta: metav1.TypeMeta{APIVersion: \"v1\", Kind: kubeutil.NamespaceKind}, ObjectMeta: metav1.ObjectMeta{Name: liveObj.GetName()}}\n   623\u2192\t\t\t\tmanagedNs, err := kubeutil.ToUnstructured(nsSpec)\n   624\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   625\u2192\t\t\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError, Message: err.Error(), LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   626\u2192\t\t\t\t\tfailedToLoadObjs = true\n   627\u2192\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n   628\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192package controller\n     2\u2192\n     3\u2192import (\n     4\u2192\t\"context\"\n     5\u2192\tgoerrors \"errors\"\n     6\u2192\t\"fmt\"\n     7\u2192\t\"os\"\n     8\u2192\t\"strconv\"\n     9\u2192\t\"strings\"\n    10\u2192\t\"sync/atomic\"\n    11\u2192\t\"time\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/strategicpatch\"\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192\tcdcommon \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/common\"\n    16\u2192\n    17\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/gitops-engine/pkg/sync\"\n    18\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/gitops-engine/pkg/sync/common\"\n    19\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/gitops-engine/pkg/utils/kube\"\n    20\u2192\tjsonpatch \"github.com/evanphx/json-patch\"\n    21\u2192\tlog \"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n    22\u2192\tv1 \"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1\"\n    23\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/apis/meta/v1/unstructured\"\n    24\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/runtime/schema\"\n    25\u2192\t\"k8s.io/apimachinery/pkg/util/managedfields\"\n    26\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/kubernetes/scheme\"\n    27\u2192\t\"k8s.io/client-go/rest\"\n    28\u2192\t\"k8s.io/kubectl/pkg/util/openapi\"\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/controller/metrics\"\n    31\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/pkg/apis/application/v1alpha1\"\n    32\u2192\tlistersv1alpha1 \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/pkg/client/listers/application/v1alpha1\"\n    33\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/argo\"\n    34\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/argo/diff\"\n    35\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/glob\"\n    36\u2192\tlogutils \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/log\"\n    37\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/lua\"\n    38\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/rand\"\n    39\u2192)\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192var syncIdPrefix uint64 = 0\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192const (\n    44\u2192\t// EnvVarSyncWaveDelay is an environment variable which controls the delay in seconds between\n    45\u2192\t// each sync-wave\n    46\u2192\tEnvVarSyncWaveDelay = \"ARGOCD_SYNC_WAVE_DELAY\"\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192\t// serviceAccountDisallowedCharSet contains the characters that are not allowed to be present\n    49\u2192\t// in a DefaultServiceAccount configured for a DestinationServiceAccount\n    50\u2192\tserviceAccountDisallowedCharSet = \"!*[]{}\\\\/\"\n    51\u2192)\n    52\u2192\n    53\u2192func (m *appStateManager) getOpenAPISchema(server string) (openapi.Resources, error) {\n    54\u2192\tcluster, err := m.liveStateCache.GetClusterCache(server)\n    55\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    56\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n    57\u2192\t}\n    58\u2192\treturn cluster.GetOpenAPISchema(), nil\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192func (m *appStateManager) getGVKParser(server string) (*managedfields.GvkParser, error) {\n    62\u2192\tcluster, err := m.liveStateCache.GetClusterCache(server)\n    63\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    64\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n    65\u2192\t}\n    66\u2192\treturn cluster.GetGVKParser(), nil\n    67\u2192}\n    68\u2192\n    69\u2192// getResourceOperations will return the kubectl implementation of the ResourceOperations\n    70\u2192// interface that provides functionality to manage kubernetes resources. Returns a\n    71\u2192// cleanup function that must be called to remove the generated kube config for this\n    72\u2192// server.\n    73\u2192func (m *appStateManager) getResourceOperations(server string) (kube.ResourceOperations, func(), error) {\n    74\u2192\tclusterCache, err := m.liveStateCache.GetClusterCache(server)\n    75\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    76\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error getting cluster cache: %w\", err)\n    77\u2192\t}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192\tcluster, err := m.db.GetCluster(context.Background(), server)\n    80\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    81\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error getting cluster: %w\", err)\n    82\u2192\t}\n    83\u2192\tops, cleanup, err := m.kubectl.ManageResources(cluster.RawRestConfig(), clusterCache.GetOpenAPISchema())\n    84\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    85\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error creating kubectl ResourceOperations: %w\", err)\n    86\u2192\t}\n    87\u2192\treturn ops, cleanup, nil\n    88\u2192}\n    89\u2192\n    90\u2192func (m *appStateManager) SyncAppState(app *v1alpha1.Application, state *v1alpha1.OperationState) {\n    91\u2192\t// Sync requests might be requested with ambiguous revisions (e.g. master, HEAD, v1.2.3).\n    92\u2192\t// This can change meaning when resuming operations (e.g a hook sync). After calculating a\n    93\u2192\t// concrete git commit SHA, the SHA is remembered in the status.operationState.syncResult field.\n    94\u2192\t// This ensures that when resuming an operation, we sync to the same revision that we initially\n    95\u2192\t// started with.\n    96\u2192\tvar revision string\n    97\u2192\tvar syncOp v1alpha1.SyncOperation\n    98\u2192\tvar syncRes *v1alpha1.SyncOperationResult\n    99\u2192\tvar source v1alpha1.ApplicationSource\n   100\u2192\tvar sources []v1alpha1.ApplicationSource\n   101\u2192\trevisions := make([]string, 0)\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192\tif state.Operation.Sync == nil {\n   104\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationFailed\n   105\u2192\t\tstate.Message = \"Invalid operation request: no operation specified\"\n   106\u2192\t\treturn\n   107\u2192\t}\n   108\u2192\tsyncOp = *state.Operation.Sync\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192\t// validates if it should fail the sync if it finds shared resources\n   111\u2192\thasSharedResource, sharedResourceMessage := hasSharedResourceCondition(app)\n   112\u2192\tif syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"FailOnSharedResource=true\") &&\n   113\u2192\t\thasSharedResource {\n   114\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationFailed\n   115\u2192\t\tstate.Message = fmt.Sprintf(\"Shared resource found: %s\", sharedResourceMessage)\n   116\u2192\t\treturn\n   117\u2192\t}\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192\tisMultiSourceRevision := app.Spec.HasMultipleSources()\n   120\u2192\trollback := len(syncOp.Sources) > 0 || syncOp.Source != nil\n   121\u2192\tif rollback {\n   122\u2192\t\t// rollback case\n   123\u2192\t\tif len(state.Operation.Sync.Sources) > 0 {\n   124\u2192\t\t\tsources = state.Operation.Sync.Sources\n   125\u2192\t\t\tisMultiSourceRevision = true\n   126\u2192\t\t} else {\n   127\u2192\t\t\tsource = *state.Operation.Sync.Source\n   128\u2192\t\t\tsources = make([]v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, 0)\n   129\u2192\t\t\tisMultiSourceRevision = false\n   130\u2192\t\t}\n   131\u2192\t} else {\n   132\u2192\t\t// normal sync case (where source is taken from app.spec.sources)\n   133\u2192\t\tif app.Spec.HasMultipleSources() {\n   134\u2192\t\t\tsources = app.Spec.Sources\n   135\u2192\t\t} else {\n   136\u2192\t\t\t// normal sync case (where source is taken from app.spec.source)\n   137\u2192\t\t\tsource = app.Spec.GetSource()\n   138\u2192\t\t\tsources = make([]v1alpha1.ApplicationSource, 0)\n   139\u2192\t\t}\n   140\u2192\t}\n   141\u2192\n   142\u2192\tif state.SyncResult != nil {\n   143\u2192\t\tsyncRes = state.SyncResult\n   144\u2192\t\trevision = state.SyncResult.Revision\n   145\u2192\t\trevisions = append(revisions, state.SyncResult.Revisions...)\n   146\u2192\t} else {\n   147\u2192\t\tsyncRes = &v1alpha1.SyncOperationResult{}\n   148\u2192\t\t// status.operationState.syncResult.source. must be set properly since auto-sync relies\n   149\u2192\t\t// on this information to decide if it should sync (if source is different than the last\n   150\u2192\t\t// sync attempt)\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
175. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/controller/sync.go",
  "limit": 250,
  "offset": 150
}
Output
"   150\u2192\t\t// sync attempt)\n   151\u2192\t\tif isMultiSourceRevision {\n   152\u2192\t\t\tsyncRes.Sources = sources\n   153\u2192\t\t} else {\n   154\u2192\t\t\tsyncRes.Source = source\n   155\u2192\t\t}\n   156\u2192\t\tstate.SyncResult = syncRes\n   157\u2192\t}\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192\t// if we get here, it means we did not remember a commit SHA which we should be syncing to.\n   160\u2192\t// This typically indicates we are just about to begin a brand new sync/rollback operation.\n   161\u2192\t// Take the value in the requested operation. We will resolve this to a SHA later.\n   162\u2192\tif isMultiSourceRevision {\n   163\u2192\t\tif len(revisions) != len(sources) {\n   164\u2192\t\t\trevisions = syncOp.Revisions\n   165\u2192\t\t}\n   166\u2192\t} else {\n   167\u2192\t\tif revision == \"\" {\n   168\u2192\t\t\trevision = syncOp.Revision\n   169\u2192\t\t}\n   170\u2192\t}\n   171\u2192\n   172\u2192\tproj, err := argo.GetAppProject(app, listersv1alpha1.NewAppProjectLister(m.projInformer.GetIndexer()), m.namespace, m.settingsMgr, m.db, context.TODO())\n   173\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   174\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   175\u2192\t\tstate.Message = fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load application project: %v\", err)\n   176\u2192\t\treturn\n   177\u2192\t} else {\n   178\u2192\t\tisBlocked, err := syncWindowPreventsSync(app, proj)\n   179\u2192\t\tif isBlocked {\n   180\u2192\t\t\t// If the operation is currently running, simply let the user know the sync is blocked by a current sync window\n   181\u2192\t\t\tif state.Phase == common.OperationRunning {\n   182\u2192\t\t\t\tstate.Message = \"Sync operation blocked by sync window\"\n   183\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   184\u2192\t\t\t\t\tstate.Message = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %v\", state.Message, err)\n   185\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   186\u2192\t\t\t}\n   187\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   188\u2192\t\t}\n   189\u2192\t}\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192\tif !isMultiSourceRevision {\n   192\u2192\t\tsources = []v1alpha1.ApplicationSource{source}\n   193\u2192\t\trevisions = []string{revision}\n   194\u2192\t}\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192\t// ignore error if CompareStateRepoError, this shouldn't happen as noRevisionCache is true\n   197\u2192\tcompareResult, err := m.CompareAppState(app, proj, revisions, sources, false, true, syncOp.Manifests, isMultiSourceRevision, rollback)\n   198\u2192\tif err != nil && !goerrors.Is(err, CompareStateRepoError) {\n   199\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   200\u2192\t\tstate.Message = err.Error()\n   201\u2192\t\treturn\n   202\u2192\t}\n   203\u2192\t// We now have a concrete commit SHA. Save this in the sync result revision so that we remember\n   204\u2192\t// what we should be syncing to when resuming operations.\n   205\u2192\n   206\u2192\tsyncRes.Revision = compareResult.syncStatus.Revision\n   207\u2192\tsyncRes.Revisions = compareResult.syncStatus.Revisions\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192\t// If there are any comparison or spec errors error conditions do not perform the operation\n   210\u2192\tif errConditions := app.Status.GetConditions(map[v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionType]bool{\n   211\u2192\t\tv1alpha1.ApplicationConditionComparisonError:  true,\n   212\u2192\t\tv1alpha1.ApplicationConditionInvalidSpecError: true,\n   213\u2192\t}); len(errConditions) > 0 {\n   214\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   215\u2192\t\tstate.Message = argo.FormatAppConditions(errConditions)\n   216\u2192\t\treturn\n   217\u2192\t}\n   218\u2192\n   219\u2192\tclst, err := m.db.GetCluster(context.Background(), app.Spec.Destination.Server)\n   220\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   221\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   222\u2192\t\tstate.Message = err.Error()\n   223\u2192\t\treturn\n   224\u2192\t}\n   225\u2192\n   226\u2192\trawConfig := clst.RawRestConfig()\n   227\u2192\trestConfig := metrics.AddMetricsTransportWrapper(m.metricsServer, app, clst.RESTConfig())\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192\tresourceOverrides, err := m.settingsMgr.GetResourceOverrides()\n   230\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   231\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   232\u2192\t\tstate.Message = fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load resource overrides: %v\", err)\n   233\u2192\t\treturn\n   234\u2192\t}\n   235\u2192\n   236\u2192\tatomic.AddUint64(&syncIdPrefix, 1)\n   237\u2192\trandSuffix, err := rand.String(5)\n   238\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   239\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   240\u2192\t\tstate.Message = fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed generate random sync ID: %v\", err)\n   241\u2192\t\treturn\n   242\u2192\t}\n   243\u2192\tsyncId := fmt.Sprintf(\"%05d-%s\", syncIdPrefix, randSuffix)\n   244\u2192\n   245\u2192\tlogEntry := log.WithFields(log.Fields{\"application\": app.QualifiedName(), \"syncId\": syncId})\n   246\u2192\tinitialResourcesRes := make([]common.ResourceSyncResult, 0)\n   247\u2192\tfor i, res := range syncRes.Resources {\n   248\u2192\t\tkey := kube.ResourceKey{Group: res.Group, Kind: res.Kind, Namespace: res.Namespace, Name: res.Name}\n   249\u2192\t\tinitialResourcesRes = append(initialResourcesRes, common.ResourceSyncResult{\n   250\u2192\t\t\tResourceKey: key,\n   251\u2192\t\t\tMessage:     res.Message,\n   252\u2192\t\t\tStatus:      res.Status,\n   253\u2192\t\t\tHookPhase:   res.HookPhase,\n   254\u2192\t\t\tHookType:    res.HookType,\n   255\u2192\t\t\tSyncPhase:   res.SyncPhase,\n   256\u2192\t\t\tVersion:     res.Version,\n   257\u2192\t\t\tOrder:       i + 1,\n   258\u2192\t\t})\n   259\u2192\t}\n   260\u2192\n   261\u2192\tprunePropagationPolicy := v1.DeletePropagationForeground\n   262\u2192\tswitch {\n   263\u2192\tcase syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"PrunePropagationPolicy=background\"):\n   264\u2192\t\tprunePropagationPolicy = v1.DeletePropagationBackground\n   265\u2192\tcase syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"PrunePropagationPolicy=foreground\"):\n   266\u2192\t\tprunePropagationPolicy = v1.DeletePropagationForeground\n   267\u2192\tcase syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"PrunePropagationPolicy=orphan\"):\n   268\u2192\t\tprunePropagationPolicy = v1.DeletePropagationOrphan\n   269\u2192\t}\n   270\u2192\n   271\u2192\topenAPISchema, err := m.getOpenAPISchema(clst.Server)\n   272\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   273\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   274\u2192\t\tstate.Message = fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to load openAPISchema: %v\", err)\n   275\u2192\t\treturn\n   276\u2192\t}\n   277\u2192\n   278\u2192\treconciliationResult := compareResult.reconciliationResult\n   279\u2192\n   280\u2192\t// if RespectIgnoreDifferences is enabled, it should normalize the target\n   281\u2192\t// resources which in this case applies the live values in the configured\n   282\u2192\t// ignore differences fields.\n   283\u2192\tif syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"RespectIgnoreDifferences=true\") {\n   284\u2192\t\tpatchedTargets, err := normalizeTargetResources(compareResult)\n   285\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   286\u2192\t\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   287\u2192\t\t\tstate.Message = fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to normalize target resources: %s\", err)\n   288\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   289\u2192\t\t}\n   290\u2192\t\treconciliationResult.Target = patchedTargets\n   291\u2192\t}\n   292\u2192\n   293\u2192\tappLabelKey, err := m.settingsMgr.GetAppInstanceLabelKey()\n   294\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   295\u2192\t\tlog.Errorf(\"Could not get appInstanceLabelKey: %v\", err)\n   296\u2192\t\treturn\n   297\u2192\t}\n   298\u2192\tinstallationID, err := m.settingsMgr.GetInstallationID()\n   299\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   300\u2192\t\tlog.Errorf(\"Could not get installation ID: %v\", err)\n   301\u2192\t\treturn\n   302\u2192\t}\n   303\u2192\ttrackingMethod := argo.GetTrackingMethod(m.settingsMgr)\n   304\u2192\n   305\u2192\timpersonationEnabled, err := m.settingsMgr.IsImpersonationEnabled()\n   306\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   307\u2192\t\tlog.Errorf(\"could not get impersonation feature flag: %v\", err)\n   308\u2192\t\treturn\n   309\u2192\t}\n   310\u2192\tif impersonationEnabled {\n   311\u2192\t\tserviceAccountToImpersonate, err := deriveServiceAccountToImpersonate(proj, app)\n   312\u2192\t\tif err != nil {\n   313\u2192\t\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   314\u2192\t\t\tstate.Message = fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to find a matching service account to impersonate: %v\", err)\n   315\u2192\t\t\treturn\n   316\u2192\t\t}\n   317\u2192\t\tlogEntry = logEntry.WithFields(log.Fields{\"impersonationEnabled\": \"true\", \"serviceAccount\": serviceAccountToImpersonate})\n   318\u2192\t\t// set the impersonation headers.\n   319\u2192\t\trawConfig.Impersonate = rest.ImpersonationConfig{\n   320\u2192\t\t\tUserName: serviceAccountToImpersonate,\n   321\u2192\t\t}\n   322\u2192\t\trestConfig.Impersonate = rest.ImpersonationConfig{\n   323\u2192\t\t\tUserName: serviceAccountToImpersonate,\n   324\u2192\t\t}\n   325\u2192\t}\n   326\u2192\n   327\u2192\topts := []sync.SyncOpt{\n   328\u2192\t\tsync.WithLogr(logutils.NewLogrusLogger(logEntry)),\n   329\u2192\t\tsync.WithHealthOverride(lua.ResourceHealthOverrides(resourceOverrides)),\n   330\u2192\t\tsync.WithPermissionValidator(func(un *unstructured.Unstructured, res *v1.APIResource) error {\n   331\u2192\t\t\tif !proj.IsGroupKindPermitted(un.GroupVersionKind().GroupKind(), res.Namespaced) {\n   332\u2192\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"resource %s:%s is not permitted in project %s\", un.GroupVersionKind().Group, un.GroupVersionKind().Kind, proj.Name)\n   333\u2192\t\t\t}\n   334\u2192\t\t\tif res.Namespaced {\n   335\u2192\t\t\t\tpermitted, err := proj.IsDestinationPermitted(v1alpha1.ApplicationDestination{Namespace: un.GetNamespace(), Server: app.Spec.Destination.Server, Name: app.Spec.Destination.Name}, func(project string) ([]*v1alpha1.Cluster, error) {\n   336\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn m.db.GetProjectClusters(context.TODO(), project)\n   337\u2192\t\t\t\t})\n   338\u2192\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n   339\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn err\n   340\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   341\u2192\n   342\u2192\t\t\t\tif !permitted {\n   343\u2192\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"namespace %v is not permitted in project '%s'\", un.GetNamespace(), proj.Name)\n   344\u2192\t\t\t\t}\n   345\u2192\t\t\t}\n   346\u2192\t\t\treturn nil\n   347\u2192\t\t}),\n   348\u2192\t\tsync.WithOperationSettings(syncOp.DryRun, syncOp.Prune, syncOp.SyncStrategy.Force(), syncOp.IsApplyStrategy() || len(syncOp.Resources) > 0),\n   349\u2192\t\tsync.WithInitialState(state.Phase, state.Message, initialResourcesRes, state.StartedAt),\n   350\u2192\t\tsync.WithResourcesFilter(func(key kube.ResourceKey, target *unstructured.Unstructured, live *unstructured.Unstructured) bool {\n   351\u2192\t\t\treturn (len(syncOp.Resources) == 0 ||\n   352\u2192\t\t\t\tisPostDeleteHook(target) ||\n   353\u2192\t\t\t\targo.ContainsSyncResource(key.Name, key.Namespace, schema.GroupVersionKind{Kind: key.Kind, Group: key.Group}, syncOp.Resources)) &&\n   354\u2192\t\t\t\tm.isSelfReferencedObj(live, target, app.GetName(), appLabelKey, trackingMethod, installationID)\n   355\u2192\t\t}),\n   356\u2192\t\tsync.WithManifestValidation(!syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(common.SyncOptionsDisableValidation)),\n   357\u2192\t\tsync.WithSyncWaveHook(delayBetweenSyncWaves),\n   358\u2192\t\tsync.WithPruneLast(syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(common.SyncOptionPruneLast)),\n   359\u2192\t\tsync.WithResourceModificationChecker(syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"ApplyOutOfSyncOnly=true\"), compareResult.diffResultList),\n   360\u2192\t\tsync.WithPrunePropagationPolicy(&prunePropagationPolicy),\n   361\u2192\t\tsync.WithReplace(syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(common.SyncOptionReplace)),\n   362\u2192\t\tsync.WithServerSideApply(syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(common.SyncOptionServerSideApply)),\n   363\u2192\t\tsync.WithServerSideApplyManager(cdcommon.ArgoCDSSAManager),\n   364\u2192\t}\n   365\u2192\n   366\u2192\tif syncOp.SyncOptions.HasOption(\"CreateNamespace=true\") {\n   367\u2192\t\topts = append(opts, sync.WithNamespaceModifier(syncNamespace(app.Spec.SyncPolicy)))\n   368\u2192\t}\n   369\u2192\n   370\u2192\tsyncCtx, cleanup, err := sync.NewSyncContext(\n   371\u2192\t\tcompareResult.syncStatus.Revision,\n   372\u2192\t\treconciliationResult,\n   373\u2192\t\trestConfig,\n   374\u2192\t\trawConfig,\n   375\u2192\t\tm.kubectl,\n   376\u2192\t\tapp.Spec.Destination.Namespace,\n   377\u2192\t\topenAPISchema,\n   378\u2192\t\topts...,\n   379\u2192\t)\n   380\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   381\u2192\t\tstate.Phase = common.OperationError\n   382\u2192\t\tstate.Message = fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to initialize sync context: %v\", err)\n   383\u2192\t\treturn\n   384\u2192\t}\n   385\u2192\n   386\u2192\tdefer cleanup()\n   387\u2192\n   388\u2192\tstart := time.Now()\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192\tif state.Phase == common.OperationTerminating {\n   391\u2192\t\tsyncCtx.Terminate()\n   392\u2192\t} else {\n   393\u2192\t\tsyncCtx.Sync()\n   394\u2192\t}\n   395\u2192\tvar resState []common.ResourceSyncResult\n   396\u2192\tstate.Phase, state.Message, resState = syncCtx.GetState()\n   397\u2192\tstate.SyncResult.Resources = nil\n   398\u2192\n   399\u2192\tif app.Spec.SyncPolicy != nil builder.\n    38\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithDiffSettings(id []v1alpha1.ResourceIgnoreDifferences, o map[string]v1alpha1.ResourceOverride, ignoreAggregatedRoles bool, ignoreNormalizerOpts normalizers.IgnoreNormalizerOpts) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n    39\u2192\tignores := id\n    40\u2192\tif ignores == nil {\n    41\u2192\t\tignores = []v1alpha1.ResourceIgnoreDifferences{}\n make(map[string]v1alpha1.ResourceOverride)\n    48\u2192\t}\n    49\u2192\tb.diffConfig.overrides = overrides\n    50\u2192\tb.diffConfig.ignoreAggregatedRoles = ignoreAggregatedRoles\n    51\u2192\tb.diffConfig.ignoreNormalizerOpts = ignoreNormalizerOpts\n    52\u2192\treturn b\n    53\u2192}\n    54\u2192\n    55\u2192// WithTrackingMethod sets the tracking in the diff config.\n    56\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithTracking(appLabelKey, trackingMethod string) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n    57\u2192\tb.diffConfig.appLabelKey = appLabelKey\n    58\u2192\tb.diffConfig.trackingMethod = trackingMethod\n    59\u2192\treturn b\n    60\u2192}\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192// WithNoCache sets the nocache in the diff config.\n    63\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithNoCache() *DiffConfigBuilder {\n    64\u2192\tb.diffConfig.noCache = true\n    65\u2192\treturn b\n    66\u2192}\n    67\u2192\n    68\u2192// WithCache sets the appstatecache.Cache and the appName in the diff config. Those the\n    69\u2192// are two objects necessary to retrieve a cached diff.\n    70\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithCache(s *appstatecache.Cache, appName string) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n    71\u2192\tb.diffConfig.stateCache = s\n    72\u2192\tb.diffConfig.appName = appName\n    73\u2192\treturn b\n    74\u2192}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192// WithLogger sets the logger in the diff config.\n    77\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithLogger(l logr.Logger) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n    78\u2192\tb.diffConfig.logger = &l\n    79\u2192\treturn b\n    80\u2192}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192// WithGVKParser sets the gvkParser in the diff config.\n    83\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithGVKParser(parser *k8smanagedfields.GvkParser) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n    84\u2192\tb.diffConfig.gvkParser = parser\n    85\u2192\treturn b\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192// WithStructuredMergeDiff defines if the diff should be calculated using structured\n    89\u2192// merge.\n    90\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithStructuredMergeDiff(smd bool) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n    91\u2192\tb.diffConfig.structuredMergeDiff = smd\n    92\u2192\treturn b\n    93\u2192}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192// WithManager defines the manager that should be using during structured\n    96\u2192// merge diffs.\n    97\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithManager(manager string) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n    98\u2192\tb.diffConfig.manager = manager\n    99\u2192\treturn b\n   100\u2192}\n   101\u2192\n   102\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithServerSideDryRunner(ssdr diff.ServerSideDryRunner) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n   103\u2192\tb.diffConfig.serverSideDryRunner = ssdr\n   104\u2192\treturn b\n   105\u2192}\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithServerSideDiff(ssd bool) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n   108\u2192\tb.diffConfig.serverSideDiff = ssd\n   109\u2192\treturn b\n   110\u2192}\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) WithIgnoreMutationWebhook(m bool) *DiffConfigBuilder {\n   113\u2192\tb.diffConfig.ignoreMutationWebhook = m\n   114\u2192\treturn b\n   115\u2192}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192// Build will first validate the current state of the diff config and return the\n   118\u2192// DiffConfig implementation if no errors are found. Will return nil and the error\n   119\u2192// details otherwise.\n   120\u2192func (b *DiffConfigBuilder) Build() (DiffConfig, error) {\n   121\u2192\terr := b.diffConfig.Validate()\n   122\u2192\tif err != nil {\n   123\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n   124\u2192\t}\n   125\u2192\treturn b.diffConfig, nil\n   126\u2192}\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192// DiffConfig defines methods to retrieve the configurations used while applying diffs\n   129\u2192// and normalizing resources.\n   130\u2192type DiffConfig interface {\n   131\u2192\t// Validate will check if the current configurations are set properly.\n   132\u2192\tValidate() error\n   133\u2192\t// DiffFromCache will verify if it should retrieve the cached ResourceDiff based on this\n   134\u2192\t// DiffConfig.\n   135\u2192\tDiffFromCache(appName string) (bool, []*v1alpha1.ResourceDiff)\n   136\u2192\t// Ignores Application level ignore difference configurations.\n   137\u2192\tIgnores() []v1alpha1.ResourceIgnoreDifferences\n   138\u2192\t// Overrides is map of system configurations to override the Application ones.\n   139\u2192\t// The key should follow the \"group/kind\" format.\n   140\u2192\tOverrides() map[string]v1alpha1.ResourceOverride\n   141\u2192\tAppLabelKey() string\n   142\u2192\tTrackingMethod() string\n   143\u2192\t// AppName the Application name. Used to retrieve the cached diff.\n   144\u2192\tAppName() string\n   145\u2192\t// NoCache defines if should retrieve the diff from cache.\n   146\u2192\tNoCache() bool\n   147\u2192\t// StateCache is used when retrieving the diff from the cache.\n   148\u2192\tStateCache() *appstatecache.Cache\n   149\u2192\tIgnoreAggregatedRoles() bool\n   150\u2192\t// Logger used during the diff.\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

700\u2192\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionUnknownError, Message: err.Error(), LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   701\u2192\t\t}\n   702\u2192\t\tdefer cleanup()\n   703\u2192\t\tdiffConfigBuilder.WithServerSideDryRunner(diff.NewK8sServerSideDryRunner(resourceOps))\n   704\u2192\t}\n   705\u2192\n   706\u2192\t// enable structured 737\u2192\n   738\u2192\t\tisSelfReferencedObj := m.isSelfReferencedObj(liveObj, targetObj, app.GetName(), appLabelKey, trackingMethod, installationID)\n   739\u2192\n   740\u2192\t\tresState := liveObj != nil && isSelfReferencedObj,\n   748\u2192\t\t}\n   749\u2192\t\tif targetObj != nil {\n   750\u2192\t\t\tresState.SyncWave = int64(syncwaves.Wave(targetObj))\n   751\u2192\t\t}\n   752\u2192\n   753\u2192\t\tvar diffResult diff.DiffResult\n   754\u2192\t\tif i < len(diffResults.Diffs) {\n   755\u2192\t\t\tdiffResult = diffResults.Diffs[i]\n   756\u2192\t\t} else {\n   757\u2192\t\t\tdiffResult = diff.DiffResult{Modified: false, NormalizedLive: []byte(\"{}\"), PredictedLive: []byte(\"{}\")}\n   758\u2192\t\t}\n   759\u2192\n   760\u2192\t\t// For the case when a namespace is managed with `managedNamespaceMetadata` AND it has resource tracking\n   761\u2192\t\t// enabled (e.g. someone manually adds resource tracking labels or annotations), we need to do some\n   762\u2192\t\t// bookkeeping in order to ensure that it's not considered `OutOfSync` (since it does not exist in source\n   763\u2192\t\t// control).\n   764\u2192\t\t//\n   765\u2192\t\t// This is in addition to the bookkeeping we do (see `isManagedNamespace` and its references) to prevent said\n   766\u2192\t\t// namespace from being pruned.\n   767\u2192\t\tisManagedNs := isManagedNamespace(targetObj, app) && liveObj == nil\n   768\u2192\n   769\u2192\t\tif resState.Hook || ignore.Ignore(obj) || (targetObj != nil && hookutil.Skip(targetObj)) || !isSelfReferencedObj {\n   770\u2192\t\t\t// For resource hooks, skipped resources or objects that may have\n   771\u2192\t\t\t// been created by another controller with annotations copied from\n   772\u2192\t\t\t// the source object, don't store sync status, and do not affect\n   773\u2192\t\t\t// overall sync status\n   774\u2192\t\t} else if !isManagedNs && (diffResult.Modified || targetObj == nil || liveObj == nil) {\n   775\u2192\t\t\t// Set resource state to OutOfSync since one of the following is true:\n   776\u2192\t\t\t// * target and live resource are different\n   777\u2192\t\t\t// * target resource not defined and live resource is extra\n   778\u2192\t\t\t// * target resource present but live resource is missing\n   779\u2192\t\t\tresState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n   780\u2192\t\t\t// we ignore the status if the obj needs pruning AND we have the annotation\n   781\u2192\t\t\tneedsPruning := targetObj == nil && liveObj != nil\n   782\u2192\t\t\tif !(needsPruning && resourceutil.HasAnnotationOption(obj, common.AnnotationCompareOptions, \"IgnoreExtraneous\")) {\n   783\u2192\t\t\t\tsyncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n   784\u2192\t\t\t}\n   785\u2192\t\t} else {\n   786\u2192\t\t\tresState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeSynced\n   787\u2192\t\t}\n   788\u2192\t\t// set unknown status to all resource that are not permitted in the app project\n   789\u2192\t\tisNamespaced, err := m.liveStateCache.IsNamespaced(app.Spec.Destination.Server, gvk.GroupKind())\n   790\u2192\t\tif !project.IsGroupKindPermitted(gvk.GroupKind(), isNamespaced && err == nil) {\n   791\u2192\t\t\tresState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown\n   792\u2192\t\t}\n   793\u2192\n   794\u2192\t\tif isNamespaced && obj.GetNamespace() == \"\" {\n   795\u2192\t\t\tconditions = append(conditions, v1alpha1.ApplicationCondition{Type: v1alpha1.ApplicationConditionInvalidSpecError, Message: fmt.Sprintf(\"Namespace for %s %s is missing.\", obj.GetName(), gvk.String()), LastTransitionTime: &now})\n   796\u2192\t\t}\n   797\u2192\n   798\u2192\t\t// we can't say anything about the status if we were unable to get the target objects\n   799\u2192\t\tif failedToLoadObjs {\n   800\u2192\t\t\tresState.Status = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown\n   801\u2192\t\t}\n   802\u2192\n   803\u2192\t\tresourceVersion := \"\"\n   804\u2192\t\tif liveObj != nil {\n   805\u2192\t\t\tresourceVersion = liveObj.GetResourceVersion()\n   806\u2192\t\t}\n   807\u2192\t\tmanagedResources[i] = managedResource{\n   808\u2192\t\t\tName:            resState.Name,\n   809\u2192\t\t\tNamespace:       resState.Namespace,\n   810\u2192\t\t\tGroup:           resState.Group,\n   811\u2192\t\t\tKind:            resState.Kind,\n   812\u2192\t\t\tVersion:         resState.Version,\n   813\u2192\t\t\tLive:            liveObj,\n   814\u2192\t\t\tTarget:          targetObj,\n   815\u2192\t\t\tDiff:            diffResult,\n   816\u2192\t\t\tHook:            resState.Hook,\n   817\u2192\t\t\tResourceVersion: resourceVersion,\n   818\u2192\t\t}\n   819\u2192\t\tresourceSummaries[i] = resState\n   820\u2192\t}\n   821\u2192\n   822\u2192\tif failedToLoadObjs {\n   823\u2192\t\tsyncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeUnknown\n   824\u2192\t} else if app.HasChangedManagedNamespaceMetadata() {\n   825\u2192\t\tsyncCode = v1alpha1.SyncStatusCodeOutOfSync\n   826\u2192\t}\n   827\u2192\tvar revision string\n   828\u2192\n   829\u2192\tif !hasMultipleSources && len(manifestRevisions) > 0 {\n   830\u2192\t\trevision = manifestRevisions[0]\n   831\u2192\t}\n   832\u2192\tvar syncStatus v1alpha1.SyncStatus\n   833\u2192\tif hasMultipleSources {\n   834\u2192\t\tsyncStatus = v1alpha1.SyncStatus{\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/common\"\n    11\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/env\"\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192\tgrpc_middleware \"github.com/grpc-ecosystem/go-grpc-middleware\"\n    14\u2192\tgrpc_retry \"github.com/grpc-ecosystem/go-grpc-middleware/retry\"\n    15\u2192\tlog \"github.com/sirupsen/logrus\"\n    16\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc\"\n    17\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials\"\n    18\u2192\t\"google.golang.org/grpc/credentials/insecure\"\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192\targogrpc \"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/grpc\"\n    21\u2192\t\"github.com/argoproj/argo-cd/v2/util/io\"\n    22\u2192)\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192// MaxGRPCMessageSize contains max grpc message size\n    25\u2192var MaxGRPCMessageSize = env.ParseNumFromEnv(common.EnvGRPCMaxSizeMB, 100, 0, math.MaxInt32) * 1024 * 1024\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192// TLSConfiguration describes parameters for TLS configuration to be used by a repo server API client\n    28\u2192type TLSConfiguration struct {\n    29\u2192\t// Whether to disable TLS for connections\n    30\u2192\tDisableTLS bool\n    31\u2192\t// Whether to enforce strict validation of TLS certificates\n    32\u2192\tStrictValidation bool\n    33\u2192\t// List of certificates to validate the peer against (if StrictCerts is true)\n    34\u2192\tCertificates *x509.CertPool\n    35\u2192}\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192// Clientset represents repository server api clients\n    38\u2192type Clientset interface {\n    39\u2192\tNewRepoServerClient() (io.Closer, RepoServerServiceClient, error)\n    40\u2192}\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192type clientSet struct {\n    43\u2192\taddress        string\n    44\u2192\ttimeoutSeconds int\n    45\u2192\ttlsConfig      TLSConfiguration\n    46\u2192}\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192func (c *clientSet) NewRepoServerClient() (io.Closer, RepoServerServiceClient, error) {\n    49\u2192\tconn, err := NewConnection(c.address, c.timeoutSeconds, &c.tlsConfig)\n    50\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    51\u2192\t\treturn nil, nil, fmt.Errorf(\"failed to open a new connection to repo server: %w\", err)\n    52\u2192\t}\n    53\u2192\treturn conn, NewRepoServerServiceClient(conn), nil\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192func NewConnection(address string, timeoutSeconds int, tlsConfig *TLSConfiguration) (*grpc.ClientConn, error) {\n    57\u2192\tretryOpts := []grpc_retry.CallOption{\n    58\u2192\t\tgrpc_retry.WithMax(3),\n    59\u2192\t\tgrpc_retry.WithBackoff(grpc_retry.BackoffLinear(1000 * time.Millisecond)),\n    60\u2192\t}\n    61\u2192\tunaryInterceptors := []grpc.UnaryClientInterceptor{grpc_retry.UnaryClientInterceptor(retryOpts...)}\n    62\u2192\tif timeoutSeconds > 0 {\n    63\u2192\t\tunaryInterceptors = append(unaryInterceptors, argogrpc.WithTimeout(time.Duration(timeoutSeconds)*time.Second))\n    64\u2192\t}\n    65\u2192\topts := []grpc.DialOption{\n    66\u2192\t\tgrpc.WithStreamInterceptor(grpc_retry.StreamClientInterceptor(retryOpts...)),\n    67\u2192\t\tgrpc.WithUnaryInterceptor(grpc_middleware.ChainUnaryClient(unaryInterceptors...)),\n    68\u2192\t\tgrpc.WithDefaultCallOptions(grpc.MaxCallRecvMsgSize(MaxGRPCMessageSize), grpc.MaxCallSendMsgSize(MaxGRPCMessageSize)),\n    69\u2192\t\tgrpc.WithUnaryInterceptor(argogrpc.OTELUnaryClientInterceptor()),\n    70\u2192\t\tgrpc.WithStreamInterceptor(argogrpc.OTELStreamClientInterceptor()),\n    71\u2192\t}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192\ttlsC := &tls.Config{}\n    74\u2192\tif !tlsConfig.DisableTLS {\n    75\u2192\t\tif !tlsConfig.StrictValidation {\n    76\u2192\t\t\ttlsC.InsecureSkipVerify = true\n    77\u2192\t\t} else {\n    78\u2192\t\t\ttlsC.RootCAs = tlsConfig.Certificates\n    79\u2192\t\t}\n    80\u2192\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(credentials.NewTLS(tlsC)))\n    81\u2192\t} else {\n    82\u2192\t\topts = append(opts, grpc.WithTransportCredentials(insecure.NewCredentials()))\n    83\u2192\t}\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192\t// nolint:staticcheck\n    86\u2192\tconn, err := grpc.Dial(address, opts...)\n    87\u2192\tif err != nil {\n    88\u2192\t\tlog.Errorf(\"Unable to connect to repository service with address %s\", address)\n    89\u2192\t\treturn nil, err\n    90\u2192\t}\n    91\u2192\treturn conn, nil\n    92\u2192}\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192// NewRepoServerClientset creates new instance of repo server Clientset\n    95\u2192func NewRepoServerClientset(address string, timeoutSeconds int, tlsConfig TLSConfiguration) Clientset {\n    96\u2192\treturn &clientSet{address: address, timeoutSeconds: timeoutSeconds, tlsConfig: tlsConfig}\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "  1190\u2192type OperationState struct {\n  1191\u2192\t// Operation is the original requested operation\n  1192\u2192\tOperation Operation `json:\"operation\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=operation\"`\n  1193\u2192\t// Phase is the current phase of the operation\n  1194\u2192\tPhase synccommon.OperationPhase `json:\"phase\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=phase\"`\n  1195\u2192\t// Message holds any pertinent messages when attempting to perform operation (typically errors).\n  1196\u2192\tMessage string `json:\"message,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=message\"`\n  1197\u2192\t// SyncResult is the result of a Sync operation\n  1198\u2192\tSyncResult *SyncOperationResult `json:\"syncResult,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=syncResult\"`\n  1199\u2192\t// StartedAt contains time of operation start\n  1200\u2192\tStartedAt metav1.Time `json:\"startedAt\" protobuf:\"bytes,6,opt,name=startedAt\"`\n  1201\u2192\t// FinishedAt contains time of operation completion\n  1202\u2192\tFinishedAt *metav1.Time `json:\"finishedAt,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,7,opt,name=finishedAt\"`\n  1203\u2192\t// RetryCount contains time of operation retries\n  1204\u2192\tRetryCount int64 `json:\"retryCount,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,8,opt,name=retryCount\"`\n  1205\u2192}\n  1206\u2192\n  1207\u2192type Info struct {\n  1208\u2192\tName  string `json:\"name\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n  1209\u2192\tValue string `json:\"value\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=value\"`\n  1210\u2192}\n  1211\u2192\n  1212\u2192type SyncOptions []string\n  1213\u2192\n  1214\u2192// AddOption adds a sync option to the list of sync options and returns the modified list.\n  1215\u2192// If option was already set, returns the unmodified list of sync options.\n  1216\u2192func (o SyncOptions) AddOption(option string) SyncOptions {\n  1217\u2192\tfor _, j := range o {\n  1218\u2192\t\tif j == option {\n  1219\u2192\t\t\treturn o\n  1220\u2192\t\t}\n  1221\u2192\t}\n  1222\u2192\treturn append(o, option)\n  1223\u2192}\n  1224\u2192\n  1225\u2192// RemoveOption removes a sync option from the list of sync options and returns the modified list.\n  1226\u2192// If option has not been already set, returns the unmodified list of sync options.\n  1227\u2192func (o SyncOptions) RemoveOption(option string) SyncOptions {\n  1228\u2192\tfor i, j := range o {\n  1229\u2192\t\tif j == option {\n  1230\u2192\t\t\treturn append(o[:i], o[i+1:]...)\n  1231\u2192\t\t}\n  1232\u2192\t}\n  1233\u2192\treturn o\n  1234\u2192}\n  1235\u2192\n  1236\u2192// HasOption returns true if the list of sync options contains given option\n  1237\u2192func (o SyncOptions) HasOption(option string) bool {\n  1238\u2192\tfor _, i := range o {\n  1239\u2192\t\tif option == i {\n  1240\u2192\t\t\treturn true\n  1241\u2192\t\t}\n  1242\u2192\t}\n  1243\u2192\treturn false\n  1244\u2192}\n  1245\u2192\n  1246\u2192type ManagedNamespaceMetadata struct {\n  1247\u2192\tLabels      map[string]string `json:\"labels,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=labels\"`\n  1248\u2192\tAnnotations map[string]string `json:\"annotations,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=annotations\"`\n  1249\u2192}\n  1250\u2192\n  1251\u2192// SyncPolicy controls when a sync will be performed in response to updates in git\n  1252\u2192type SyncPolicy struct {\n  1253\u2192\t// Automated will keep an application synced to the target revision\n  1254\u2192\tAutomated *SyncPolicyAutomated `json:\"automated,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=automated\"`\n  1255\u2192\t// Options allow you to specify whole app sync-options\n  1256\u2192\tSyncOptions SyncOptions `json:\"syncOptions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=syncOptions\"`\n  1257\u2192\t// Retry controls failed sync retry behavior\n  1258\u2192\tRetry *RetryStrategy `json:\"retry,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=retry\"`\n  1259\u2192\t// ManagedNamespaceMetadata controls metadata in the given namespace (if CreateNamespace=true)\n  1260\u2192\tManagedNamespaceMetadata *ManagedNamespaceMetadata `json:\"managedNamespaceMetadata,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=managedNamespaceMetadata\"`\n  1261\u2192\t// If you add a field here, be sure to update IsZero.\n  1262\u2192}\n  1263\u2192\n  1264\u2192// IsZero returns true if the sync policy is empty\n  1265\u2192func (p *SyncPolicy) IsZero() bool {\n  1266\u2192\treturn p == nil || (p.Automated == nil && len(p.SyncOptions) == 0 && p.Retry == nil && p.ManagedNamespaceMetadata == nil)\n  1267\u2192}\n  1268\u2192\n  1269\u2192// RetryStrategy contains information about the strategy to apply when a sync failed\n  1270\u2192type RetryStrategy struct {\n  1271\u2192\t// Limit is the maximum number of attempts for retrying a failed sync. If set to 0, no retries will be performed.\n  1272\u2192\tLimit int64 `json:\"limit,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=limit\"`\n  1273\u2192\t// Backoff controls how to backoff on subsequent retries of failed syncs\n  1274\u2192\tBackoff *Backoff `json:\"backoff,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=backoff,casttype=Backoff\"`\n  1275\u2192}\n  1276\u2192\n  1277\u2192func parseStringToDuration(durationString string) (time.Duration, error) {\n  1278\u2192\tvar suspendDuration time.Duration\n  1279\u2192\t// If no units are attached, treat as seconds\n  1280\u2192\tif val, err := strconv.Atoi(durationString); err == nil {\n  1281\u2192\t\tsuspendDuration = time.Duration(val) * time.Second\n  1282\u2192\t} else if duration, err := time.ParseDuration(durationString); err == nil {\n  1283\u2192\t\tsuspendDuration = duration\n  1284\u2192\t} else {\n  1285\u2192\t\treturn 0, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to parse %s as a duration\", durationString)\n  1286\u2192\t}\n  1287\u2192\treturn suspendDuration, nil\n  1288\u2192}\n  1289\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"  1587\u2192type SyncStatus struct {\n  1588\u2192\t// Status is the sync state of the comparison\n  1589\u2192\tStatus SyncStatusCode `json:\"status\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=status,casttype=SyncStatusCode\"`\n  1590\u2192\t// ComparedTo contains information about what has been compared\n  1591\u2192\tComparedTo ComparedTo `json:\"comparedTo,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=comparedTo\"`\n  1592\u2192\t// Revision contains information about the revision the comparison has been performed to\n  1593\u2192\tRevision string `json:\"revision,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=revision\"`\n  1594\u2192\t// Revisions contains information about the revisions of multiple sources the comparison has been performed to\n  1595\u2192\tRevisions []string `json:\"revisions,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=revisions\"`\n  1596\u2192}\n  1597\u2192\n  1598\u2192// HealthStatus contains information about the currently observed health state of an application or resource\n  1599\u2192type HealthStatus struct {\n  1600\u2192\t// Status holds the status code of the application or resource\n  1601\u2192\tStatus health.HealthStatusCode `json:\"status,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=status\"`\n  1602\u2192\t// Message is a human-readable informational message describing the health status\n  1603\u2192\tMessage string `json:\"message,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=message\"`\n  1604\u2192}\n  1605\u2192\n  1606\u2192// InfoItem contains arbitrary, human readable information about an application\n  1607\u2192type InfoItem struct {\n  1608\u2192\t// Name is a human readable title for this piece of information.\n  1609\u2192\tName string `json:\"name,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=name\"`\n  1610\u2192\t// Value is human readable content.\n  1611\u2192\tValue string `json:\"value,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=value\"`\n  1612\u2192}\n  1613\u2192\n  1614\u2192// ResourceNetworkingInfo holds networking resource related information\n  1615\u2192// TODO: describe members of this type\n  1616\u2192type ResourceNetworkingInfo struct {\n  1617\u2192\tTargetLabels map[string]string        `json:\"targetLabels,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,opt,name=targetLabels\"`\n  1618\u2192\tTargetRefs   []ResourceRef            `json:\"targetRefs,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,opt,name=targetRefs\"`\n  1619\u2192\tLabels       map[string]string        `json:\"labels,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=labels\"`\n  1620\u2192\tIngress      []v1.LoadBalancerIngress `json:\"ingress,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=ingress\"`\n  1621\u2192\t// ExternalURLs holds list of URLs which should be available externally. List is populated for ingress resources using rules hostnames.\n  1622\u2192\tExternalURLs []string `json:\"externalURLs,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,5,opt,name=externalURLs\"`\n  1623\u2192}\n  1624\u2192\n  1625\u2192// TODO: describe this type\n  1626\u2192type HostResourceInfo struct {\n  1627\u2192\tResourceName         v1.ResourceName `json:\"resourceName,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=resourceName\"`\n  1628\u2192\tRequestedByApp       int64           `json:\"requestedByApp,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=requestedByApp\"`\n  1629\u2192\tRequestedByNeighbors int64           `json:\"requestedByNeighbors,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,name=requestedByNeighbors\"`\n  1630\u2192\tCapacity             int64           `json:\"capacity,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,name=capacity\"`\n  1631\u2192}\n  1632\u2192\n  1633\u2192// HostInfo holds host name and resources metrics\n  1634\u2192// TODO: describe purpose of this type\n  1635\u2192// TODO: describe members of this type\n  1636\u2192type HostInfo struct {\n  1637\u2192\tName          string             `json:\"name,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,name=name\"`\n  1638\u2192\tResourcesInfo []HostResourceInfo `json:\"resourcesInfo,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,name=resourcesInfo\"`\n  1639\u2192\tSystemInfo    v1.NodeSystemInfo  `json:\"systemInfo,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,opt,name=systemInfo\"`\n  1640\u2192}\n  1641\u2192\n  1642\u2192// ApplicationTree holds nodes which belongs to the application\n  1643\u2192// TODO: describe purpose of this type\n  1644\u2192type ApplicationTree struct {\n  1645\u2192\t// Nodes contains list of nodes which either directly managed by the application and children of directly managed nodes.\n  1646\u2192\tNodes []ResourceNode `json:\"nodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,1,rep,name=nodes\"`\n  1647\u2192\t// OrphanedNodes contains if or orphaned nodes: nodes which are not managed by the app but in the same namespace. List is populated only if orphaned resources enabled in app project.\n  1648\u2192\tOrphanedNodes []ResourceNode `json:\"orphanedNodes,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,2,rep,name=orphanedNodes\"`\n  1649\u2192\t// Hosts holds list of Kubernetes nodes that run application related pods\n  1650\u2192\tHosts []HostInfo `json:\"hosts,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,3,rep,name=hosts\"`\n  1651\u2192\t// ShardsCount contains total number of shards the application tree is split into\n  1652\u2192\tShardsCount int64 `json:\"shardsCount,omitempty\" protobuf:\"bytes,4,opt,name=shardsCount\"`\n  1653\u2192}\n  1654\u2192\n  1655\u2192func (t *ApplicationTree) Merge(other *ApplicationTree) {\n  1656\u2192\tt.Nodes = append(t.Nodes, other.Nodes...)\n  1657\u2192\tt.OrphanedNodes = append(t.OrphanedNodes, other.OrphanedNodes...)\n  1658\u2192\tt.Hosts = append(t.Hosts, other.Hosts...)\n  1659\u2192\tt.Normalize()\n  1660\u2192}\n  1661\u2192\n  1662\u2192// GetShards split application tree into shards with populated metadata\n  1663\u2192func (t *ApplicationTree) GetShards(size int64) []*ApplicationTree {\n  1664\u2192\tt.Normalize()\n  1665\u2192\tif size == 0 {\n  1666\u2192\t\treturn []*ApplicationTree{t}\n  1667\u2192\t}\n  1668\u2192\n  1669\u2192\tvar items []func(*ApplicationTree)\n  1670\u2192\tfor i := range t.Nodes {\n  1671\u2192\t\titem := t.Nodes[i]\n  1672\u2192\t\titems = append(items, func(shard *ApplicationTree) {\n  1673\u2192\t\t\tshard.Nodes = append(shard.Nodes, item)\n  1674\u2192\t\t})\n  1675\u2192\t}\n  1676\u2192\tfor i := range t.OrphanedNodes {\n  1677\u2192\t\titem := t.OrphanedNodes[i]\n  1678\u2192\t\titems = append(items, func(shard *ApplicationTree) {\n  1679\u2192\t\t\tshard.OrphanedNodes = append(shard.OrphanedNodes, item)\n  1680\u2192\t\t})\n  1681\u2192\t}\n  1682\u2192\tfor i := range t.Hosts {\n  1683\u2192\t\titem := t.Hosts[i]\n  1684\u2192\t\titems = append(items, func(shard *ApplicationTree) {\n  1685\u2192\t\t\tshard.Hosts = append(shard.Hosts, item)\n  1686\u2192\t\t})\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
